Wood-The target's Movement Rate is reduced to 0 and anyone adjacent to the target is halved.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Fire Hounds
Circle_7.Description: Fire. Spirit. Summoning. This spell summons two fire spirits in the form of burning hounds from a fire at least the size of a campfire. The spellcast- er crouches on all fours, howls loudly, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the flaming hounds leap from the fire. Fire hounds resemble large dogs, standing more than four feet high at the shoulder and are sur- rounded in flames that don't give off heat. Their stocky, muscular bodies are covered with short dark-brown fur and rows of sharp teeth fill their jaws. A fire hound's eyes seem to burn-deep within each socket is a ball of flame. In darkness, their eyes glow like candle flames. Fire hounds act as a single creature and the spellcaster controls them with a Standard action using the language of fire (Elemental Tongues, Play- er's Guide, p. 141). Fire hounds aren't proactive and don't attack, chase, etc., without instructions to do so. If the hounds move beyond the range from the spellcaster, they act on their most recent instructions. Hounds travel at Movement Rate 16 as a pack and must remain within 4 yards of each other at all times; hounds outside this area can do nothing except return to their pack. The hounds are nearly insubstantial. They move through open spaces around defenders unimpeded (essentially moving through them, but not lit- erally through them). Fire hounds within range of the spellcaster can be ordered to attack one target, quickly surrounding them (often inflicting Harried), by making an El- emental Tongues test against the target's Physical Defense; each hound after the first attacking the target gives +1 to this test. If successful, the Effect test determines how much fire damage is inflicted, reduced by Physical Armor. Each fire hound after the first who attacked the target gives +1 to the Effect test. Additional successes on the Elemental Tongues test give 2 to the Ef- fect test. Fire Hounds are difficult to injure, but the astral pattern containing the spell can be disrupted by attacks that target their Physical Defense (Elemen- talist Circle+8) or Mystic Defense (Elementalist Circle+4). If a single attack inflicts damage equal to or greater than the Effect Step, or the hound is suc- cessfully attacked with water, the fire hound is released and disappears.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (+1 fire hound), Increase Effect (2 Movement Rate), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Grasping Hand of Earth
Circle_7.Description: Earth. This spell causes a massive hand to rise out of the ground and seize a character who is standing on earth or stone. The spellcaster clutch- es at the air, points at the target, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, a huge earthen hand rises from the ground and clasps the target. Each round, the target may make a Strength test as a Free action against the hand's Effect Step to break free, otherwise they may perform no other actions while held in the hand's grip.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Lightning Cloud
Circle_7.Description: Air-Electric. This spell conjures a swirling black and purple cloud that crackles with electricity. The spellcaster spreads their arms wide, wiggles their fingers, claps their hands together and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the cloud finishes forming in front of them. Each subsequent round as a Standard action, the spellcaster may draw lightning from the cloud and hurl it at an opponent within range, making a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spellcaster's Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted and the target must make a Knockdown test against the Effect test after armor is applied. Additional successes on the Spellcasting test against an opponent give 2 per addition- al success to the Effect step for that lightning bolt. This spell can only cause one Knockdown test, regardless if a Wound is inflicted.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2), In- crease Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Lightning Shield
Circle_7.Effect: Inflicts Harried if shield is struck and 2 to attacks with shield
Circle_7.Description: Air-Electric. This spell charges a shield with electricity, jolting anyone who strikes it. The spellcaster snaps their fingers, creating a spark of elec- tricity which leaps to the willing target's shield, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the front surface of the shield hisses and pops, cor- uscating with blue-white electricity. Close combat attacks directed against the shield risk striking it. If an opponent misses with a close combat attack against Physical Defense, they strike the shield. This doesn't include using active defenses (e.g. Avoid Blow). When the shield is struck or strikes an op- ponent-such as using Shield Bash-the opponent is Harried until the end of the next round as electricity courses through their body. The adept also gains 2 to Shield Bash tests and their effective Storm Shield rank if using those abilities; the target must possess Storm Shield to gain the bonus.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Shield Bash and Storm Shield), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Tar- gets (+rank)

Circle_8.Name: Crushing Hand of Earth
Circle_8.Description: Earth. This spell causes a massive hand to rise out of the ground and seize a character  standing on earth or stone, exerting injurious crushing force on him. The magician  shakes a fist at the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's  Mystic Defense. If successful, the magician's Effect test determines how much damage  is inflicted each round'this requires his concentration or the spell ends. Each round,  the target may make a Strength test against the Effect Step to break free, otherwise,  he may perform no other actions while held in the spell's grip.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Duration (+2 rounds),  Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Earth Wall
Circle_8.Effect: Create a contiguous wall of earth
Circle_8.Description: Earth. This spell summons an earthen wall. The magician must be standing on  earth or stone making shoveling actions, as if digging a dirt palisade, and then makes  a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, he summons up to Spellcasting Rank contiguous  wall sections, each 4 yards high, 2 yards wide, and 2 yards thick. Summoning and positioning  a section takes the magician one round and requires his concentration. The  wall may not be placed on a living being'doing so immediately destroys that section,  which still counts toward the maximum number of wall sections summoned. The wall  can be physically or magically attacked. Treat each section of wall as a barrier with  Physical and Mystic Armor of 12 and a Death Rating 40.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Additional Sections (+2), Increase  Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Ironskin
Circle_8.Effect: +3 to Physical Armor, Wound Threshold and Knockdown test
Circle_8.Description: Earth ' Metal. This spell melds the target's body with a form of elemental metal,  strengthening it. The magician grasps a piece of metal, crosses both fists and punches  the target, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If the  test succeeds, the target gains +3 to Physical Armor, Wound Threshold and Knockdown  tests. The effects of this spell are reflected in the target's appearance, which  takes on a dark, metallic look. Also, the target's weight effectively doubles and he loses  all buoyancy, including the ability to swim (the spell in no way bolster's the target's  respiratory processes, so casting on an ally while afloat would be a remarkably bad  idea).   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Increase Effect (+1 to all bonuses),  Additional Targets (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Mantle of the Lightning Vanguard
Circle_8.Effect: Target gains flight and increased speed
Circle_8.Description: Air ' Electric. Binding. This spell summons an air elemental and temporarily  binds it to a willing target and requires cloud cover. The magician reaches to the sky  with one hand and touches the target with his other hand, then makes a Spellcasting  test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, lightning will strike his upraised  hand and travel to the target, lifting him from the ground as electricity courses  through his body.The target gains a flying Movement Rate of 16 and can concentrate  to surround himself with a 2-yard radius field of electricity which will levitate any  willing targets in the area, allowing them to travel with him.  When doing this, the bound target may only move and perform Free Actions.  The bound target gains +2 to Initiative tests. The target's presence is always heralded  and may not perform Blindside or Surprise attacks.  If the target touches the ground for any reason, the spell immediately ends.  If the spell ends prematurely for any reason, the target takes one Wound and this  spell may not be cast again for a full day. This spell may only have one active casting  at a time.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Movement  Rate), Increase Effect (+2 Initiative)

Circle_8.Name: Venomous Fury
Circle_8.Effect: +3 to close combat Damage tests and poison
Circle_8.Description: Wood. This spell enhances a melee weapon with elemental power. The magician  shouts out a battle cry, then touches the melee weapon held by the target and makes  a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the melee weapon  gleams bright green and appears oily and slick, adding +3 to Damage tests and  poisoning the opponent if at least one point of damage is inflicted. The poison inflicts  paralysis with an instant onset time, and a Mystic Defense and Step equal to the magician's  Elementalist Circle+3. Effects of the poison wear off when the spell ends.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 Damage bonus),  Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Vital Springs
Circle_8.Effect: Heals all allies within the area of effect
Circle_8.Description: Water. This spell heals all of the magician's allies within the area of effect. The  magician kneels and places his palm on the ground, closes his eyes, sheds a tear and  makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area of effect. If  successful, bright, sparkling effervescent water bubbles up from the ground in the  area, disappearing into the sky as it refreshes everyone affected. All targets within the  area may make a Recovery test. The magician may give his own Recovery tests to any  targets (one per target) for use with this spell. Targets that do not have a Recovery  Test available are unaffected.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Recovery test bonus)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Recovery test bonus), Increase Area (+2  yards)

Circle_8.Name: Blade Fury
Circle_8.Description: Air-Electric. Earth-Metal. Fire. Water-Cold. Wood. This spell enhances a melee weapon with elemental power. The spellcaster shouts a battle cry, touches a melee weapon held by the willing target, and makes a Spellcast- ing (6) test. If successful, the weapon gleams silver, then green with leaves etched in the weapon, followed by fire burning away the leaves, then cold moving down the length extinguishing the flames, and finally crackling light- ning driving away the ice. The wielder gains +4 to Attack and Damage tests with the weapon, and attacks gain one of the listed elements. The wielder can change to a different listed element once per round as a Simple action. The weapon clearly displays the current element, though the exact appear- ance varies based on both the spellcaster and the wielder.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 to Attack and Damage tests), Additional Target (+rank) Spirits of Death's Sea Threads: 2 Weaving: 12/17 Casting: 6 Range: Touch Duration: 4 hours Effect: 2 to resist fear Fire. This spell creates a potent invigorating drink and requires a mug of an alcoholic beverage. The spellcaster waves their hands over the cup and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the beverage turns into a smoking, molten liquid resembling lava. The cup's entire contents must be consumed to gain any benefit from the spell. Swallowing the liquid requires the target to make a Toughness (6) test. If successful, the target becomes energized and invigorated, much like the Air Sailors who sail Death's Sea. The target gains a 2 to Defenses against tests to resist the ongoing effects of fear-causing spells, talents, or abilities (including intimidation) and tests to end the associated ongoing effects. If failed, the target gains no benefit, but takes Step 4/D6 damage from the hot liquid scalding their insides (no armor provides protection). This spell may be cast once per day and the duration is measured from when the spell is cast, not when the affected beverage is consumed.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to re- sist fear), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_8.Name: Storm Manacles
Circle_8.Description: Air-Electric. This spell restrains the target with cloudy shackles. The spellcaster works a drop of water into a wisp of cloud which they blow to- ward the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the wisp darkens and grows to form a set of manacles that shackle the target's arms or legs-while shackled, the target is Harried. If the target attempts any hostile action against the spellcaster or their obvi- ous allies, the manacles zap them with lightning. The spellcaster's Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted. A target can only take damage from this spell once per round.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Thorn Fury
Circle_8.Effect: +6 to Damage tests, reduces Wound Threshold by 3, and heals
Circle_8.Description: Wood. This spell enhances a melee weapon with magical thorns. The spellcaster shouts a battle cry, touches a melee weapon held by the willing target, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the weapon gleams bright green and is wrapped in wicked thorns, adding +6 to Damage tests and reducing the target's Wound Threshold by 3 for attacks with the weap- on as the thorns pierce the opponent and drink greedily. Additionally, the wielder heals damage equal to the damage bonus from Thorn Fury when the weapon causes a Wound.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Damage tests), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_8.Name: Wind Knife
Circle_8.Description: Air. The spellcaster shapes the air around them, concentrating it until it's like a blade and throws it at an opponent. The spellcaster grasps at the air with both hands-pulling it, swirling it around, and shaping it-and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the denser air shimmers and leaves ripples around it as it darts to the tar- get, slicing them and knocking them back. The Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted and the Knockdown Difficulty the target must resist. This spell can only cause one Knockdown test, regardless if a Wound is inflicted. Additional successes throw the target back with the force of the spell, this distance is treated as falling damage (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 167).  Success Levels: Additional Effect (2 yards knock back)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Effect (2 yards knock back), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Cloud Banish
Circle_9.Description: Air. This spell causes clouds to disperse, reducing the chance of rain (death or otherwise), hail, lightning, poisonous gas, or other effects associated with clouds. The spellcaster makes warding gestures, com- mands the clouds to disperse, and makes a Spellcasting test against a Difficulty Number determined by the type of clouds: typical, fluffy, white, happy clouds are 10, angry storm clouds are 15, and gale-force storm clouds are 20, or more if particularly intense. Magically cre- ated clouds require an additional success to affect. If successful, the clouds fully disperse in an hour. Additional successes and extra threads can reduce this time. Any effects from the clouds remain until they're fully dis- persed.  Success Levels: Reduce Duration (-10 min- utes)  Extra Threads: Reduce Duration (-10 min- utes), Increase Area (+1 mile) Dragon's Breath Threads: 2 Weaving: 13/18 Casting: TMD Range: 40 yards Duration: Instant Area of Effect: 2 yards wide Effect: WIL+10/Physical Fire. This spell creates a gout of intense flame, often compared to a dragon's breath. The spellcaster inhales deeply, lets loose a great roar, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense within range of the direction they're facing. If success- ful, the spellcaster's Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted. This spell cannot be affected by Range Pattern or similar effects.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Ef- fect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_9.Name: Fire Wall
Circle_9.Description: Fire. This spell summons a wall of flame that may be placed on the ground or hung in the air in a place of the spellcaster's choosing. The spellcaster waves their arms around, as if painting on a large canvas, summoning nearby flame between their hands, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the flames flow from the spellcaster's hands to the location. Anything living or full of living things (such as a ship) exits the wall if it's summoned on top of them. Each 2-yard wide section of the wall is a barrier with Physical Armor 5 and Death Rating 50. The wall can be attacked, however missile and throw- ing weapons have no effect, and non-magical weapons are destroyed after the attack. Fire and mystic attacks do no damage, but water attacks ignore armor. After a close combat attack, the spellcaster makes an Effect test and the attacker takes damage equal to the result. This damage is inflicted even if the wall section is destroyed by the attack. Other than water-based at- tacks, the Physical Armor cannot be reduced.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to Armor and +10 to Death Rating), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Area (2 yards tall), Increase Area (+10 yards wide)

Circle_9.Name: Heart of Stone
Circle_9.Description: Earth. This spell hardens the target's heart against external influences. The spellcaster closes their eyes, places a hand over their heart, inhales and exhales deeply, calming themselves, and makes a spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target's skin and eyes become dull, and their face becomes largely emotionless and expressionless. They gain 2 to their Mystic and Social Defenses against fear effects, attempts to read or affect their emo- tions (e.g. Taunt but not Distract), and their tests to resist or remove fear or emotion-manipulation effects. The target also finds it impossible to tap into abilities which require strong emotions or exceptional aggression, such as the Frenzy ability and Aggressive Attack combat option.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2)

Circle_9.Name: Water Wall
Circle_9.Description: Water. This spell summons a wall of water that may be placed on the ground or on water in a place of the spellcaster's choosing. The spellcaster puts their fingers in a vessel of at least one quart of water, stirs the water around, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the water flows from the spellcaster's hands to the location. Anything living or full of living things (such as a ship) exits the wall if it is summoned on top of them. Each 2-yard wide section of the wall is a barrier with Physical Armor 10 and Death Rating 60. The wall can be attacked, however missile and throw weapons have no effect. Water and mystic attacks do no damage, but air at- tacks ignore armor. Other than air-based attacks, the Physical Armor cannot be reduced. The wall automatically recovers damage equal to the Effect Step at the end of each round. This is doubled if located on top of a large source of water, such as a lake or river.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to Armor and +10 to Death Rating), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Area (2 yards tall), Increase Area (+10 yards wide)

Circle_9.Name: Wood Blade
Circle_9.Description: Wood. This spell transforms a piece of wood at least one yard long (or appropriate to the spellcaster's one-handed Size limit) into a one-handed melee weapon. The spellcaster grips the piece of wood in both hands, wield- ing it like a blade, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the wood transforms into a weapon at the spellcaster's one-handed Size limit with an appearance unique to the spellcaster. Regardless of the appearance, it has no special abilities (e.g. wood whips don't entangle). The spellcaster can make close combat attacks with it using their Spell- casting talent for the Attack test and the Effect test determines damage. The Attack test is a Simple action if the spellcaster used Elementalism to attempt weaving threads this round. Note: the spellcaster cannot make more than one Spellcasting test in a round. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time. This spell cannot be cast on (e.g. Fifth Circle Wizard Karma ability) or by an- other character (e.g. Loan Spell).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Ef- fect Step)

Circle_10.Name: Earth and Air
Circle_10.Effect: Converts earth to air or air to earth
Circle_10.Description: Air. Earth. This spell converts earth to air or air to earth. The spellcast- er focuses entirely on the spell-no movement, speech, or other action is possible while weaving the threads-bringing to mind the essence of earth and air, imagining reversing the two, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense (minimum 10). If successful, the spell converts either earth to air or air to earth, as designated by the spellcaster. The desig- nated material in the area is altered for the duration, after which it returns to normal. Earth turned to air cannot be blown elsewhere and returns to its original location. Anything else within that area which wasn't present at the transformation is moved outside of it once the air turns back to earth. This doesn't affect metal, living or animate targets, such as the air in their lungs, rockworms, elementals, nor does air turn back to earth in their lungs, but it will as any remaining is exhaled.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Fire and Water
Circle_10.Effect: Converts fire to water or water to fire
Circle_10.Description: Fire. Water. This spell converts fire to water or water to fire. The spell- caster focuses entirely on the spell-no movement, speech, or other action is possible while weaving the threads-bringing to mind the essence of fire and water, then imagines reversing the two, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense (minimum 10). If successful, the spell converts either fire to water or water to fire, as designated by the spellcast- er. The designated contiguous material in the area is altered for the dura- tion, after which it returns to normal. This doesn't affect living or animate targets, such as firebirds, characters affected by elemental spells, elemen- tals, etc. The spellcaster can create raging fires in the middle of a sea and the fire doesn't burn out normally until the spell ends. Water isn't immediately turned to steam by the surrounding flames (if applicable) and remains a liq- uid for the duration.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Petrify
Circle_10.Description: Earth. This spell turns the target to stone. The spellcaster casts a hand- ful of powdered stone at the target and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mystic Defense. If successful, the target begins turning to stone. Each round, the target makes a Toughness (Iron Constitution applies) or Will- power test against the Effect Step (target's choice). If the target succeeds at one test, the spell ends. After each failed test, more of the target's body turns stone-like-this is only a cosmetic effect for now. After three failed tests, the target turns to stone. The target is allowed an- other test each day at dawn to escape. Any damage inflicted on the petrified target remains when the spell ends, though it can be repaired with appropriate magic (in- cluding the Weaponsmith's Craftsman ability). These repairs can- not heal damage received prior to the effect. Dead targets don't decompose, but this doesn't 'pause the clock' with regard to potential life saving effects.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Stone Spike
Circle_10.Description: Earth. This spell creates a spire of stone that forcefully emerges under the target. The spell- caster kneels, punches the ground, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic De- fense; the target cannot be more than four yards above the ground. If successful, a four-yard tall stone spike strikes the target from below and the Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted. The stone spike remains as an impass- able obstacle until someone destroys it (1-yard radius stone block).  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2), In- crease Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_10.Name: Summon Elemental Merchant
Circle_10.Description: Fire. Summoning. This spell summons a fiery servant to negotiate and transport goods on behalf of the summoner and requires a source of flame at least the size of a campfire. The spellcaster raises their arms up high and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster summons a fire elemental in the form of a seven-foot dwarf. Once summoned, the spellcaster tells the el- emental merchant the required items and provides them with the necessary money. Once this is provided (or an hour passes and the spirit leaves out of boredom), the spirit travels to the specified city or nearest widely known settlement if none is indicated, and purchases the goods. The spirit always pays market price for the location and cannot acquire anything not easily available there. For example, rare items require a major city and last chance salves aren't available unless the spellcaster indicates a specific seller. After all purchases are complete or the spirit has 200 lbs of goods, they return to the spellcaster. The spirit's fiery body doesn't harm the goods or anything else in the area. They travel overland at 20 miles an hour and trav- el 24 hours a day-assuming the terrain isn't difficult and there's no need to cross water. The spirit never crosses water, even with a bridge or boat. If engaged in combat, the spirit flees, but a successful attack against Physical or Mystic Defense 10 banishes the spirit and they drop the goods. However, the spirit travels at such a speed, it's unlikely to be engaged and along with its form convinces nearly all would-be attackers to wait for the next one. Once the goods are returned to the spellcaster, the spirit either returns to the location to complete the rest of its list (if there was more than it could carry), or the summoning is complete and the spirit departs. The spellcaster can only have one casting of this spell active at a time and it can only be cast once per day.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (+50 lbs)

Circle_11.Name: Cloud Maze
Circle_11.Description: Air. Ritual. This ritual creates a disorienting maze of fog. The spellcaster walks the perimeter of the area with a censer full of water which streams out vapor while weaving the threads. Each Thread Weaving test (or equiv- alent) takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster moves to the middle of the area, pulling in the nascent cloud behind them, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the area fills with disorienting fog. All senses are dampened within the cloud maze and it's very easy to get lost within the area. Vision is impaired to only 2 yards. Sound is muffled in the area and makes communication with anyone more than 2 yards away impossible without specific communication magic (e.g. Air Speaking or Thought Link). Simply increasing the volume is ineffective. This also renders abilities that rely on sound (e.g. Battle Shout and Taunt) useless in the area. Scents are absorbed and distributed throughout the maze. Creatures that rely on hear- ing or smell as primary senses suffer full blindness. A character must succeed at a Willpower test against the Effect Step to travel through or find something within the area. If successful, they manage to focus and not become disoriented or separated from their allies. If failed, they're separated from their allies and lost within the cloud maze for the next hour after which they exit the maze where they entered. Magical effects that lead through the area (e.g. Mystic Pursuit, Safe Path, Tracking, etc.) are unaffected-this only affects the senses of those within the area. An extra thread to remove targets allows them to successfully nav- igate the cloud maze without disorientation, but doesn't reduce the other effects.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+10 yards), Remove Targets (-rank)

Circle_11.Name: Frozen Harbor
Circle_11.Description: Ritual. Water-Cold. This spell freezes an expanse of water, trapping any vessels within. The spellcaster must stand within sight of the target water and within range of any connected part of it. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. After all the threads are woven, the spellcaster makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the affected area is covered with a 1-yard thick sheet of ice. The ice immobilizes any ships within and is strong enough to support the mass of a fully armed and armored obsidi- man. Even with Ironskin. The ice eventually melts on its own-in temperate weather, it's gone completely in a week and breaks up sufficiently for ships to navigate in a day or two. The ice has a barrier Death Rating of 60 and PA of 10. This spell can be cast once per day.  Success Levels: Increase Area (+100 yards)  Extra Threads: Increase Area (+100 yards)

Circle_12.Name: Air Fortress
Circle_12.Description: Air. Ritual. This ritual spell creates a translucent fortress from clouds. The spellcaster can make it tinted so it's easier to see-the exact color is unique to the spellcaster. The spellcaster paces out the dimensions of the fortress while weaving the threads. This must be performed under an open sky with significant clouds. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 1 hour. The spellcaster can weave threads up to their El- ementalist Circle and must weave at least 4. They pull the clouds to themselves and make a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the des- ignated area fills with the clouds. Each thread yields a 6-yard square section with a 3-yard tall wall surrounding the floor space. Each section must be connected, but can be attached as the spellcaster sees fit (adjacent or stacked) with internal walls, ceilings, and stairwells added or removed as needed to divide internal space. Ex- ternal walls and floors can also be removed for aesthetic purposes. External walls are barriers with Physical Armor 10 and Death Rating 40. The fortress moves vertically at Movement Rate 8 and travels overland at 5 miles an hour. It continues on its last heading until the spellcaster directs it oth- erwise. The spellcaster can have one casting of this spell at a time and it must be recreated after the duration expires.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+7 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+7 days)

Circle_12.Name: Elemental Redoubt
Circle_12.Description: Earth. Ritual. This ritual creates a stone fortress. The spellcaster carves their personal glyph into a series of stones while weaving the threads. Each Thread Weaving test (or equiva- lent) takes 30 minutes. While weaving threads, the spellcaster Names up to their Elementalist Circle characters and declares a command word or phrase. The spellcaster places the stones on the ground in front of them, arranging them to indicate the internal structure of their redoubt, and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If success- ful, a stone fortress emerges from the ground. This ziggurat is 20-yards tall and has a 30 * 30- yard base. It's surrounded by 10-yard tall and 2-yard thick crenelated stone walls which can be accessed from within. The exterior of the wall is 40-yards long * 40-yards wide and has no obvious entrance. Characters Named while weaving threads can state the command word and a 4-yard square en- try opens on the closest wall until they pass through. If the character wishes for more char- acters to pass through first, they must wait out- side of the wall. Another character can close an opening on the closest wall with the command word if necessary. The exact details (location of ramps and entrances, etc.) are unique to each spellcaster and remain consistent. Increasing the area affects the dimensions of the ziggurat and walls equally. The fortress is unfurnished.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+10 * 10 yards), Increase Area (+10-yards high)

Circle_13.Name: Storm Ship
Circle_13.Description: Air. Summoning. Ritual. This ritual spell summons an air spirit and fashions it into an airship and arguably the fastest mode of trans- portation. The spellcaster prepares a ritual cir- cle that could contain the ship while weaving the threads. It must be prepared under an open sky and with significant clouds present. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster reaches out, pulling in some of the clouds between their hands, brew- ing a storm within, and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the storm releases into the circle and an air spirit appears within. The spellcaster must pay the spirit one True air kernel per thread woven. Afterward, the spirit is fashioned into an impossibly sleek airship made from a crackling and swirling storm. The spellcaster communicates or facilitates communicating the destination to the storm ship, which departs at the spellcaster's command. Effectively com- municating the destination to the air spirit requires Elemental Tongues and Navigation-'Parlainth' (or similar) is meaningless to the spirit. Once the journey begins, it continues until the stated destination is reached, the spell- caster aborts the travel, or the duration expires. The destination cannot be changed mid-flight, only stopped. It carries up to six passengers (regardless of their size) and travels at 30 miles per hour for the duration without pause or need for rest. The space a passenger would occupy can be exchanged for 100 lbs of cargo instead. When commanded to stop or the duration expires, the storm ship lands as safely as possible. The spellcaster can have one casting of this spell at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (+10 MPH), Additional Targets (2 passengers)

Circle_14.Name: Council of the Elements
Circle_14.Description: Element [Air, Earth, Fire, Water, or Wood]. Ritual. Spirit. This powerful ritual summons all the elemental spirits of the appropriate type within the area and calls them to attend the spellcaster. The spellcaster creates one glyph per thread that combines their personal sigil with representations of the chosen element. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster introduces the element to each glyph (such as breath- ing into an air glyph) and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, they can use their Summon [Elemental Spirits] test within the spell's duration to summon a council of elementals. The talent still takes 30 minutes to per- form but the test result is compared to the Mystic Defense of all the spirits of the appropriate element in the area. All spirits the test succeeds against arrive at the spellcaster within 30 minutes under a metaphorical banner of peace to be respected by all parties. If the peace is broken, the spellcast- er has explaining to do and amends to make. If they're responsible for the breach, they can never summon elementals of that element again and all react violently to them on sight. This summoning and council must be con- cluded within the duration. Once the council concludes, the spell immedi- ately ends. Once the council of the elements is assembled, the spellcaster may bring a single matter before them and ask their judgment. The topic can be any- thing of concern to the elementals, whether relating to their domain, threats to their element in general, requesting a favor and compensation, etc. This is deliberately broad, but it must be of interest to them, even if that interest is only benefiting through payment. The council weighs what the spellcaster says and offers, then provides its advice. The spellcaster cannot force the spirits to obey in any way, though can use Elemental Tongues of the appro- priate language to sway them through persuasive arguments. These spirits can be powerful allies if they choose to help the spellcaster; they know ev- erything related to their element in their domain. The gamemaster determines the spirits' reaction based on a few factors: the relevance of the topic, offers and risks, the spellcaster's presentation of the topic, and how much respect they showed for the elementals in the past. Has the spellcaster forged friendships, treated them well, ensured they were compensated for summonings, engaged in a contest of wills, bound spirits to items, etc. At the gamemaster's discretion, the spirits choose whether or not to support the position depending on the aforementioned factors. If the spellcaster showed complete irreverence for the element in the past, the re- action could range from outright denial of the request and returning home, to complete outrage and overtly hostile action once the council is complete and the onus of peace is lifted. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time. This spell cannot be cast on (e.g. Fifth Circle Wizard Karma ability) or by an- other character (e.g. Loan Spell). Each element of the Council of the Elements spell is different and must be learned separately.  Success Levels: Increase Area (+1 mile)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to the Summon test), Increase Effect (2 to the Elemental Tongues tests), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_14.Name: Terracotta Legion
Circle_14.Effect: Raises a legion of clay soldiers
Circle_14.Description: Earth. Ritual. This ritual spell fashions and animates a legion of clay sol- diers. The ritual must be performed in an area with significant clay deposits, such as a typical field or forested area and the spellcaster fashions clay into small, humanoid soldier statuettes with their personal glyph on the fore- head while weaving threads. The statuettes may be transported, but care must be taken when moving them. Each Thread Weaving test (or equiva- lent) takes eight hours; only one test can be made per day. After weaving the final thread, the spellcaster breathes on the statuettes and makes a Spell- casting (10) test. If successful, clay from the earth rises around each statu- ette, increasing them to the typical size of the appropriate Namegiver-this doesn't affect their attributes. Every 25 clay soldiers are a group and this spell creates one group, but extra threads can create more groups. The spellcaster can mentally command their clay soldiers within line of sight-this requires their concentration as a Standard action. If left uncon- trolled, clay soldiers act on their most recent instructions. They must re- main within the spell's area, which follows the spellcaster, and become inert if they leave it, regaining animation if the spellcaster moves to include them. Clay soldiers are armed with whatever weapons they were fashioned with, or just their fists-the details are cosmetic and the results are the same. This is also true for armor. If a clay soldier is killed, it breaks into pottery shards. Clay soldiers use the statistics listed below; they're the same for all Namegivers. If a clay soldier suffers a Wound, one of the soldiers within is destroyed. Regardless of the number of members in the legion, they act as a group and receive only the listed actions. They suffer Wound penalties as normal, reflecting the legion's losses. Area spells can cause additional Wounds for each full multiple of the Wound Threshold inflicted on the clay soldiers. Abilities that inflict a penal- ty on a single target (e.g. Mind Dagger's Physical Defense penalty) or abili- ties that provide a bonus against one opponent (e.g. Maneuver, Pack Tactics) cost an additional success against the clay soldiers. If the penalty is a 'rider' on the damage (e.g. Earth Darts) and no extra success on the test, it inflicts damage but not the penalty. The clay soldiers are considered a single target for abilities with a variable difficulty based on the number of targets (e.g., Battle Bellow). A knocked down soldier automatically stands up and has no penalty to their action(s). The spellcaster can only have one active instance of this spell at a time. Clay Soldiers DEX: 4 Initiative: NA Wounds: 21 STR: 7 Physical Defense: 6 Wound Threshold: 13 TOU: 7 Mystic Defense: 6 PER: 4 Social Defense: 11 Knockdown: 9 WIL: 4 Physical Armor: 5 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 3 Mystic Armor: 5 Move: 12 Actions: 1; Attack: 22 (27) Powers: Immune to Fear Mob Mentality: The clay soldiers cannot be Harried or Blindsided from positioning. Abilities still cause these as usual. Resist Pain (10) Special Maneuvers: Sweeping Attack (Opponent, Close Combat): The attacker may spend two additional successes on an Attack test to treat the attack as an area attack; they must be adjacent to at least two clay soldiers to use this spe- cial maneuver. This special maneuver only costs one additional success if the attacker is using a two-handed weapon.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+7 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+7 days), Increase Effect (+1 group), Increase Area (+100 yards)

Circle_15.Name: Call Forth the Maelstrom
Circle_15.Description: Element [Air, Earth, Fire, Water, or Wood]. Ritual. Spirit. This ritual spell stirs up a natural disaster of the spellcaster's choosing related to the ap- propriate element, such as an earthquake, tidal wave, forest fire, etc. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes seven days, during which the spellcaster contacts the various elemental spirits who must make the re- quired alterations for the impending maelstrom. They can do nothing else other than what is required to live (eat, sleep, etc.). Small signs of its ap- proach are noticeable as each thread is woven: tremors, high winds, unusual changes in temperature, etc. After weaving the last spell thread, the spell- caster raises their arms and makes a Spellcasting (25) test. If successful, the area is devastated. Most inhabitation in the area is seriously damaged or destroyed. The gamemaster determines specific individual effects of the maelstrom based on its type and location. Once cast, the spellcaster has no control over the natural disaster. This spell isn't subtle and anyone with knowledge of Elementalism can discern the building power. Contact with spirits of the appropriate or oppo- sition element means they're likely aware something is going on requiring their attention and causing significant agitation. It's also an excellent way to get the wrong kind of attention from dragons in the area. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time. Each element of the Call Forth the Maelstrom spell is different and must be learned separately.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_1.Name: Assuring Touch
Circle_1.Effect: +3 to Mystic and Social Defense; +3 Willpower test bonus against fear
Circle_1.Description: This spell increases resistance to fear. The magician thinks positive thoughts, then  gently touches the target while murmuring something reassuring like 'everything's  going to be okay' and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense.  If successful, the target adds +3 to his Mystic and Social Defense against fear-causing  attacks and gains a +3 bonus to any Willpower tests he makes to resist the effects of  fear or Intimidation (see the Gamemaster's Guide).   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Additional  Target (+Rank)

Circle_1.Name: Best Face
Circle_1.Description: Illusion. This spell disguises one's identity. The magician splashes water or cologne  on the target's face then makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic  Defense. If successful, the target's face changes to match a face envisaged by the magician'  if he is copying a specific face, he must have seen that individual within the  last month.  The target gains a +3 bonus to Disguise Self (and may use Disguise Self as a default  skill if they do not possess it) or Charisma-based tests when trying to convince  others of his false identity and attempting to persuade them to act according to that  misperception. Any Interaction tests made against the target are considered Sensing  tests (see Illusions, p.266). The spell only affects the target's head and neck, including  hair and ears. The maximum possible size difference between the target's face and  that being mimicked is 100 percent in any dimension'the magician must find another  way to disguise obvious differences. The target's voice is unchanged, so he must  try to mimic the speech characteristics of the character whose face he has borrowed  as best he can.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Cloak
Circle_1.Description: Figment. This spell masks a character, making him less noticeable to others. The  magician quietly touches the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's  Mystic Defense. If successful, the target becomes semi-transparent and gains a +3  bonus to any Action tests he makes that are aimed at concealment or stealth, such  as Stealthy Stride tests and may use Stealthy Stride as a default skill if they do not  possess it.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Increase  Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_1.Name: Disaster
Circle_1.Description: Figment. This spell creates the figment of a disaster happening just beyond normal  sight. The magician glances around casually, then stops and stares hard at a fixed  point on the horizon, saying out loud something like 'Oh, Passions, look over there!'  or 'What in the world can that be?' and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest  Mystic Defense among the target group, +1 for each additional target. If successful,  the evidence of a disaster of the magician's choosing is created: a plume of smoke rising  from the roofs of a village, the sounds of people screaming, or some other minor,  but distracting, effect.  The target's attention is drawn to the supposed disaster and is sufficiently distracted  to allow the equivalent of a Blindsided attack against him. The magician may  affect up to Spellcasting Rank targets. The spell cannot be cast inside small, enclosed  environments, such as rooms with no windows. If used against a target engaged in  combat, or another equally engaging activity, the magician must achieve an additional  success for the spell to affect him. Anyone affected by the spell targeted by an Action  test (such as an Attack test) which draws their attention is no longer affected by the  spell. If this spell has previously affected a target and they were attacked as a result, it  may not be used again on that target until the following day.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Encrypt
Circle_1.Description: Illusion. This spell makes a selected tract of text jumbled and unreadable. The  magician runs his hands over the text and then makes a Spellcasting test against the  Mystic Defense of the book or paper on which it is written (minimum 6). A grimoire's  Mystic Defense is equal to the Dispel Difficulty of the highest-Circle spell it contains  (see Grimoires, p. 252). If successful, the text becomes incomprehensible to anyone  other than the magician. Anyone attempting to read the encrypted text must make a  successful Perception, Read and Write Language, or Patterncraft test as appropriate  against the spell's Sensing Difficulty to read it.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours)

Circle_1.Name: Ephemeral Bolt
Circle_1.Description: Illusion. This spell creates a wriggling, glowing green bolt that squirms its way  through the air toward an opponent. The magician wriggles his finger and makes a  Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, his Effect test determines  how much damage is inflicted.  Additionally, the target suffers a -2 penalty to any Willpower tests until the end  of the next round. This spell may be disbelieved (see Disbelieving Illusions, pg. 267).  This spell may be placed in the same spell matrix as True Ephemeral Bolt.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Fun With Doors
Circle_1.Description: Figment. This spell creates different visual effects, all based around the use of  doors. The magician must be able to see the target door, trapdoor or archway. He  gestures in the air as if painting the door with an imaginary brush, then makes a Spellcasting  test against the target's Mystic Defense (minimum 6). If successful, the magician  may create a variety of illusions involving the target. Any interaction with the  door will reveal its true nature. False doors created with this spell may be no bigger  than 3 square yards in size. New doors cannot be created further than 4 yards away  from the original. And obviously, you can't run through a fake door'   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Monstrous Mantle
Circle_1.Effect: +2 close combat Attack and Damage tests, and +2 Physical Defense
Circle_1.Description: Illusion. This spell improves a character's combat prowess by making onlookers  believe they have transformed into a monster.  The magician makes snarling and growling sounds, then touches the target and  makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, twin helixes  of light appear around the target, rotating and spinning into the form of a monstrous  humanoid creature, typically increasing in size, and gaining unnatural characteristics  such as axes, tentacles or giant pincers for hands.  The target gains a +2 bonus to his Attack and Damage tests for close combat and  adds +2 to his Physical Defense. The illusion does not give the target any special attack  forms or abilities. Any physical tests which interact with the target are considered  Sensing tests; for example, Attack, Avoid Blow and Riposte tests.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 to all bonuses), Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_1.Name: Send Message
Circle_1.Effect: Passes a paper message to a chosen target
Circle_1.Description: This spell creates and transfers a message to a character within line of sight of the  magician. The magician makes tiny, rapid gestures in the air to represent writing the  note's text, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful,  a temporary paper message is conjured in the target's hand, pocket, pouch,  or elsewhere on his body (according to the magician's choice). The note may contain  no more than a few short sentences, with a maximum number of words equal to the  magician's Spellcasting Rank ' 10.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Increase Effect (+20 words), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: True Ephemeral Bolt
Circle_1.Effect: WIL+1/Mystic and -2 Penalty to next Sensing test
Circle_1.Description: This spell looks and acts exactly like the Ephemeral Bolt spell, p. 296, but packs  less punch than its illusory twin. The magician wriggles his finger and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, a glowing green bolt  squirms through the air and hits the target. The magician's Effect test determines how  much damage is inflicted and any target has a -2 penalty to their next Sensing test as  their perceptions become skewed.  This spell may be placed in the same spell matrix as Ephemeral Bolt.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (-2 penalty), Increase  Effect (Penalty applies to additional Sensing test), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Trust
Circle_1.Effect: Creates a friendly relationship between magician and target
Circle_1.Description: This spell inspires a character to trust the magician. The magician smiles and nods  toward the target, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense.  If successful, the target will not deceive or harm the magician and others obviously  Friendly to him for the duration of the spell as long as the magician and his friends do  not deceive or harm the target. When the spell ends, the target becomes aware of the  manipulation and his Attitude towards the magician decreases by one degree.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Unseen Voices
Circle_1.Description: Figment. This spell creates hidden voices to distract characters. The magician  mutters quietly to himself and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic  Defense.  If successful, he creates a number of illusory voices equal to his Spellcasting Rank.  Optionally, the magician may choose to have only one target, in which case only they  will hear the voices. The spell provides a nearly limitless number of different voices  that can be used. A magician need only use whatever voices are required to achieve  the desired result. By concentrating, the magician controls what each voice says'if  he loses concentration, the voices repeat what they said in the previous round. The  magician does not gain any special ability to hear what is being said by the target, but  he must able to hear what the target is saying if he wants to use the voices in a real  conversation. The magician may use the Mimic Voice talent with this spell to replicate  specific voices. In which case, use the Mimic Voice talent as usual if the spell is successful.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Bellow of the Thundras
Circle_1.Description: This spell enhances a willing target's voice and music they're making, allowing them to be better heard. The spellcaster inhales deeply, places one hand on their chest, extends the other, acts as though loudly addressing a crowd, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the target's voice and music they're playing are amplified and can be heard clearly by those listening within the area. This gives the target 2 to tests where they are ad- dressing a crowd, such as Emotion Song (Player's Guide, p. 141), Leadership (Player's Guide, p. 156), or Making an Impression (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 144). If an affected action is initiated within the spell's duration, the benefit applies for the duration of the action. The area accompanies the target.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to affected tests), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Light Show
Circle_1.Effect: Transfers coins to a hidden space
Circle_1.Description: Figment. This spell taps into the astral plane, releasing some energy to create orbs of light. The spellcaster closes their eyes, lowers their head, stretches out their arms dramatically, then lifts their head and opens their hands suddenly, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, they sum- mon a number of orbs of light equal to their Illusionist Circle. Each orb pro- duces as much light as a torch. These floating orbs appear within range, and the spellcaster can change their color, move them, dim, turn them off, and/ or restore them to the default brightness at will if they are within range. If used to enhance a performance, including using Emotion Song, the lights give the performance 2 to the test. The light show must be incorpo- rated into the performance and actively controlled by the spellcaster, not just be present. To control the orbs, whether moving or to incorporate them in a performance, they must be within range of the caster. One orb may be moved per round as a Simple action to any space within range.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 hour)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 hour), Increase Effect (2 to per- formance test), Increase Effect (+Rank orbs), Increase Range (+10 yards) Pauper's Purse Threads: 2 Weaving: 5/10 Casting: TMD Range: Touch Duration: Rank minutes Effect: Transfers coins to a hidden space Illusion. This spell makes a container in a target's possession hold less than it really does. The spellcaster touches the container and makes a Spell- casting test against the target's Mystic Defense (minimum 6). If successful, up to 50 coins are transferred from the container to an extra-dimensional pocket, invisible to those without astral perception. Inspecting the container (including using Picking Pockets) or using astral perception is a Sensing test. If a Sensing test through astral perception is successful, a mag- ical effect is detected, though not the purpose. If a Sensing test through physical inspection is successful, the pocket immediately ends and the coins are returned to the container.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 min- utes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 min- utes), Increase Effect (+50 coins)

Circle_1.Name: Phantom Astral Spear
Circle_1.Description: Illusion. This spell creates a phantom spear, similar to Astral Spear (Player's Guide, p. 318). The spellcaster pulls their arm back, hurls it for- ward as if throwing a spear, and makes a Spell- casting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, an ethereal spear forms that cross- es the distance between the two adversaries and strikes the target. The target suffers Mystic damage equal to the Effect test result.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Phantom Crow Tear
Circle_1.Description: ness Illusion. This spell creates an illusory crow spirit, similar to Invoke Crow Tear (The Adept's Journey: Mystic Paths, p. 315). The spellcast- er raises their arms out, caws, stretches their hands like talons toward the target as their call finishes, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, an illu- sory crow with a blood-stained beak and talons flies from the spellcaster's hands toward the target and manifests as it strikes at their eyes. The target suffers Physical damage equal to the Effect test result and Partial Blindness until the end of the next round. The target cannot subsequently disbelieve or use a Sensing test to re- move the Partial Blindness effect.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Rope Guide
Circle_1.Description: This spell animates the movement of a piece of rope. The spellcaster touches the target rope and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the rope animates to wriggle along the ground within the area. The spellcaster can concentrate to direct the rope at Movement Rate 10. The spellcaster uses the Effect Step in place of a Dexterity Step if it's required to tie itself (this cannot be affected by Willforce) and it can also untie itself if it was tied with this spell. The rope cannot pull any weight-the spellcaster can direct the rope to tie itself around the bars of a window, but characters still must climb down or up on their own.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Warning Signal
Circle_1.Description: This spell alerts the spellcaster to the presence of intruders. The spell- caster points where they want to place the alarm, whistles, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the Spellcasting test result is compared against the Mystic Defense of anything passing within the area. If successful, the alarm goes off and emits a piercing whistle, alerting anyone within at least 100 yards from the center of the area. Anyone within the area suffers -2 to all Action tests for one minute from the disorientation caused by the loud noise. This spell can only be cast on a location, not an object or living thing.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 hour)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 hour), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_2.Name: Blindness
Circle_2.Description: Illusion. This spell affects the target’s visual perception. The magician closes one  eye, points at the target, blinks twice, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, the target’s vision is obscured with an inky blackness;  the target is effectively “blinded” and suffers a Full Darkness penalty to sight-based  Action tests. Any sight-based Action tests he makes are considered Sensing tests. Targets  who don’t require vision to “see” are unaffected by this spell.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)

Circle_2.Name: Displace Image
Circle_2.Description: Illusion. This spell makes a character “invisible” by displacing the image that others  see and shifting it a short distance. The magician quickly traces an outline of the  target, then touches the “shoulder” of the outline, followed by the shoulder of the target,  and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, an  outlined figure shrouded in a shimmering blur appears drifting 4 yards away, quickly  transforming into an identical image of the target. The target is no longer visible in his  actual location, the illusion having displaced his image.  Attacks made against the image have no effect, and the magician must concentrate  to maintain the facade. Each round that the magician does not concentrate gives  all attackers a cumulative +5 to Sensing tests against the illusion. Attack tests made  against the image count as Sensing tests (see Illusions, p.266).   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Innocent Activity
Circle_2.Description: Illusion. This spell masks a character’s activity. The magician waves his hands  about as if painting on a large canvas, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, everyone will only see an image of the target performing  an apparently innocent activity appropriate to the setting. If the target moves  from his current location, the illusion is broken. The target may otherwise perform  any action, even attacking an opponent, during the spell’s duration. Any obviously  inexplicable phenomena as a result of action on the part of the target, such as voices,  spells, or attacks, allows those viewing or listening to make a Perception test as a  Sensing test (see Illusions, p. 266). If successful, the sensing character sees through  the illusion, and the spell ends. If failed, the sensing character concocts some other  plausible explanation for what happened.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Mind Fog
Circle_2.Description: This spell clouds a characters perceptions and makes him forget what he was  about to do. The magician scratches his temple as if trying to remember something,  then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the  target can only react to actions made against him, until he remembers what it is he  wants to do. Appropriately defending yourself against hooligans is considered a reaction,  though why they are under attack will remain a mystery. The target may make  a Willpower test each round against the Effect Step if they have reason to suspect  something is amiss. Each round in combat gives the target a cumulative +5 to the  Willpower test.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Phantom Flame
Circle_2.Description: Illusion. This spell appears like the Wizard spell Flame Flash (pg. 344), but is an  illusion. The magician makes wild, fanning gestures and crackling, whooshing sounds,  then extends his hands outward and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, flames flash from his fingertips, bathing the target in  magical fire. The magician’s Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted each  round. A successful Sensing test will negate the effects during subsequent rounds.  This spell can be disbelieved (see Disbelieving Illusions, pg. 267).   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: See the Unseen
Circle_2.Description: This spell allows a character to better see hidden things. The magician lightly  touches the target’s eyes, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic  Defense. If successful, he gains a +5 bonus to sight-based Perception tests made to  detect hidden objects or characters. The spell does not enable the target to see things  that cannot normally be seen, such as Horrors lurking in astral space, although it will  help when combined with a talent or ability that allows such astral viewing.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+1 bonus), Additional  Target (+Rank)

Circle_2.Name: And Their Money
Circle_2.Effect: -2 Social Defense against tests involving finances
Circle_2.Description: This spell makes others more amenable to financial requests. The spell- caster clicks a couple of coins together in their hand and makes a Spellcast- ing test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target suffers -2 to Social Defense against all tests involving finances, including Haggle tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (-2 So- cial Defense), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Billowing Cloak
Circle_2.Description: This spell enhances the ability to impress others. It can only be cast on a willing target wearing a cloak or who has at least shoulder-length hair (a t'skrang's crest is considered 'long hair' for this purpose). The spellcaster tosses their head and flips their hair (if any) or crest back, places their hand on the target, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the air behind the target becomes animated, billowing their cloak or hair out behind them. The target gains 2 to Action tests to improve Attitude (such as First Im- pression or Making an Impression).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_2.Name: Chicanery
Circle_2.Effect: Grants 2 to Conceal Objects, Fast Hand, and Picking Pockets
Circle_2.Description: This spell enhances the target's manual dexterity. The spellcaster fum- bles around their clothes, searching for something, looks accusingly at the target, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, subtle magic makes the target's fingers more nimble, an intuition when no one is looking, and their clothes more helpful at hiding things. They gain 2 to Conceal Objects, Fast Hand, and Picking Pockets tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (+1 to both effects), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_2.Name: Impossible Knot
Circle_2.Effect: Creates the illusion of an impossible knot
Circle_2.Description: Illusion. This spell ties an incredibly difficult knot. The spellcaster ties a knot in a rope, cord, etc, up to 10 yards long, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the knot doesn't come loose on its own and is extremely difficult to untie or break. Tests made to cut or untie the knot are Sensing tests (Dexterity to untie, appropriate Attack test to cut). This doesn't make the knot any more effective at being a knot against forces immune to illu- sions, such as gravity.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (+5 yards)

Circle_2.Name: Phantom Liquid Arrow
Circle_2.Description: Illusion. This spell creates a phantom spear, similar to Liquid Arrow (Magic: Deeper Secrets, p. 18). The spellcaster scoops up a handful of water, hurls it at the target, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mys- tic Defense. If successful, the liquid transforms into the illusion of an arrow that strikes the target. The target suffers Physical damage equal to the Effect test result.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Remove Shadow
Circle_2.Description: This spell removes a character's shadow. The spellcaster touches the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense or Difficulty Number 6 if they're willing. If successful, the spellcaster pulls away what appears to be a fine veil of silky gray material that folds in on itself and vanishes. The target casts no shadow, even in strong light, and shows no reflection in mirrors or other reflective surfaces, gaining 2 to Action tests or Difficulty Numbers (if an Action test generates a Difficulty Number, the bonus isn't added twice) to be unseen or hidden. They're also immune to abilities that affect a target's shadow or reflection, such as Shad- ow Tether (Player's Guide, p. 340). Unfortunately, those without shadows or reflections are often believed to be Horror-touched.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Action tests or Difficulty Numbers), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Tailor
Circle_2.Effect: Illusory alterations to the target's clothing
Circle_2.Description: Illusion. This spell alters the style and color of a character's clothes, improving or worsening their appearance. The spellcaster clenches their teeth as if biting a thread, touches the target, and makes a Spellcasting test (6) test for willing targets, or against the target's Mystic Defense otherwise. If successful, the target's clothes change, taking on a more fashionable and flattering cut, or appearing threadbare and bedraggled-the spellcaster chooses. If the former, the target gains 2 to Charisma-based tests where appearance is important, such as improving a target's Attitude or asking for favors. If the latter, the target suffers -2 to such tests. This cannot stack with other benefits based on clothing (e.g., Clean) as it replaces the clothing's ap- pearance with the illusion.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Effect (2 or -2 as appropriate), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_3.Name: And Then I Woke Up
Circle_3.Description: This spell enhances the ability to perceive the truth of things. The magician claps  his hands together loudly and makes a Spellcasting test against his Mystic Defense. If  successful, he gains a +5 bonus to Sensing tests.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_3.Name: Blinding Glare
Circle_3.Description: Effect: Inflicts the Full Darkness status on everyone in the area of effect  This spell creates a blinding flash of light. The magician opens his eyes as wide as  possible, then covers them and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic  Defense among all characters in the area of effect. If successful, the target is blinded  and suffers a Full Darkness penalty to sight-based Action tests for 1 round per success.  Targets who do not require vision to “see” are unaffected by this spell.   Success Levels: See text   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+2 yards)

Circle_3.Name: Fog of Jeer
Circle_3.Effect: Enrages and humiliates all within area of effect
Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell conjures a fog that causes those wrapped in its misty tendrils to  become enraged and humiliated. The magician wraps his arms about him and shakes  as if barely containing laughter, then points to where the fog will appear and makes  a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area of effect. If successful,  a luminous, sparkling white fog coalesces into a 4-yard radius sphere around the  position.  Once conjured, the fog cannot move and all those within it are barraged by laughing,  mocking voices and figments of those they know humiliating them. They are considered  Harried and must declare the Aggressive Attack option (even if they receive  no real benefit) for all actions while within the area of effect. All actions are considered  Sensing tests.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+2 yards)

Circle_3.Name: Nobody Here
Circle_3.Effect: Make onlookers ignore a group of characters
Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell hides characters from others. The magician concentrates on  the thought of “nobody here” and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic  Defense among any targets (including himself) within the area of effect. If successful,  those affected become invisible to anyone outside the area of effect able to view them.  Once cast, the spell’s effect does not move with the magician and only the original targets  gain the benefit of the invisibility, not those who enter later. Targets who exit the  area of effect become visible to others—they can regain their invisibility by moving  back inside the circle.  A character who walks into the area of the illusion makes a Perception test as a  Sensing test (see Illusions, p. 266). The viewing character adds a +3 bonus to his Perception  test if a concealed target manipulates the environment in a way that requires  explanation, such as moving books in plain sight, opening or shutting doors, and so  on. The viewing character gains a +5 bonus to his Perception test if a concealed target  makes physical contact with him. If successful, the illusion is revealed for that observer.  If failed, the viewing character’s sensory feedback is magically suppressed—his  senses detect any characters that would normally be visible, but the knowledge is  suppressed by the spell. If a concealed target causes damage to another character, the  attacked character is no longer affected by the illusion and any onlookers immediately  make a Sensing test with a +5 bonus.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+2 yards)

Circle_3.Name: Phantom Warrior
Circle_3.Effect: Creates three images of the target
Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell forms three phantom images of a character, making him more  difficult to hit in combat. The magician moves his hands in slow, abstract patterns,  then points at the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, three identical images of the target appear, remaining within 1  yard of the target at all times, occasionally overlapping with him as they move around.  The images do not move in complete sync with the target—one image may swing a  sword high, another swing low, and the third thrust home, while the target adopts a  defensive stance. The images confuse opponents—the target adds +1 per image (+3)  to his Physical Defense during the spell’s duration. Attacks made by the target are  more difficult for opponents to avoid or defend against—a -1 per image (-3) penalty  is applied to any Avoid Blow, Riposte, or other defensive Action test made in response  to the target’s attack. These tests are considered Sensing tests.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 Image) Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+Rank)

Circle_3.Name: Conceal Tracks
