Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell conceals the tracks of a group with magic, confusing anyone attempting to track them. The spellcaster brushes the ground and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, an illusion conceals the target group's tracks (physical and astral), affecting anyone looking for them. The group can be up to Illusionist Circle individuals (animals count as individu- als). Perception-based tests specifically searching for the tracks are Sensing tests. This spell can be cast on a given group's tracks once per day and only affects tracks made after the spell is cast.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_3.Name: False Floor
Circle_3.Effect: Create the illusion of a trap
Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell creates the illusion of an obviously disguised fake floor, or other similar trap, on the normal floor. The spellcaster imagines the type of trap concealed by the surface, pantomimes installing the trap, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, an illusion of the imagined trap trigger appears on the floor. Characters encountering the illusion believe something is wrong with the surface and the gamemaster indicates the trap might be triggered if the character crosses the floor section, with potentially disastrous results. Interacting with the illusion with appropriate tests al- lows Sensing tests as usual, revealing the illusion if successful. Regardless of the Sensing test, if a character crosses the surface, nothing is triggered. This spell may be cast on any surface, not just floors.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_3.Name: Hidden Weapons
Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell conceals weapons carried in plain sight. The spell- caster pricks their finger, taking 1 Strain, touches a weapon with their blood, rubs the blood vigorously with a cloth to remove it, and makes a Spellcast- ing test. If successful, everyone except whoever is carrying the weapon ig- nores its existence. Interacting with the weapon allows those around them to make appropriate Sensing tests and any active search for weapons is a Sensing test. Drawing the affected weapon immediately ends this spell. It doesn't al- low surprise attacks.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_3.Name: Papers Please
Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell causes official documentation, symbols of authority, etc., to appear authentic and appropriate. The spellcaster shuffles briefly through some objects, selects one, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the affected object appears as it should based on the observer's expectations. A bundle of associated papers is one object, but travel papers for multiple people are multiple objects. The object must be the appropriate size and shape, or it requires an additional success (for example, a rock re- quires an additional success to look like a badge of authority, but any papers suffice to appear as a cargo manifest). Inspecting an affected object gives the inspector a Sensing test using the appropriate ability. If the story provided about the documentation doesn't match what the inspector expects to be present (whether the inspector is correct in their expectation or not), the inspector is granted +5 to the Sens- ing test for each major detail that doesn't match and would typically raise a red flag. When the spell is cast on an appropriate forgery, the spell's Sensing Difficulty gains +1 for every point above 10 on the Detection Difficulty.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Duration (hours), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_3.Name: Time Flies
Circle_3.Description: Illusion. This spell alters the perception of time. The spellcaster lights a candle or torch and makes a Spellcasting (6) test, specifying if they are speeding up or slowing down time and to what degree (up to a factor of 2 change). If successful, the temporal perception of anyone who enters the area alters-time seems to pass faster or slower as appropriate within that area. For example, a target may believe two hours passed for every hour of real time, or 30 minutes passed when an hour actually passed. The spell only changes perceptions and has no effect on durations or Recovery tests. If an affected target notices time seems to be passing differently, they may make a Perception or other appropriate test as a Sensing test. The target believes external methods to determine the passing of time (e.g., movement of the sun or an hourglass) confirm their perceptions unless they make a successful Sensing test.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 fac- tors), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Clarion Call
Circle_4.Description: Figment. This spell creates an imaginary voice that persuades others to do the  magician’s will. The magician talks quietly to himself and then makes a Spellcasting  test against the highest Mystic Defense among the target group, +1 for each additional  target.  If successful, an illusory voice of considerable authority translates the magician’s  words into a language appropriate to each target. The target then acts according to  the voice’s suggestions, as long as the action is not obviously harmful to others. With  three successes, the target is fooled into taking the suggested course of action, even if  it is harmful to someone else.  If convinced to follow a course of action that would hurt another, or go against  the target in some way, the target may make a Willpower test against the Effect step  before he carries out the command. The magician may affect up to Spellcasting Rank  targets. The target will not harm himself and suggestions to do so immediately end  the spell’s effect on him.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Great Weapon
Circle_4.Description: Illusion. This spell makes a Size 2 or larger melee weapon appear bigger and more  dangerous than it actually is. The magician makes threatening overtures and then  makes a Spellcasting test against the wielder’s Mystic Defense. If successful, opponents  facing the weapon become Harried for the spell’s duration. Any defensive Action  tests made in combat against the wielder or weapon are considered Sensing tests.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Additional Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Notice Not
Circle_4.Effect: Creates an illusion that the target is not there
Circle_4.Description: Illusion. This spell masks a character, making others ignore him entirely. The magician  quietly touches the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, anyone in the target’s area will ignore them and subconsciously  move out of the way to avoid his presence. If the target interacts with anything  in the area, onlookers may make Sensing tests as appropriate. Anyone actively  aware (such as guards) may make Perception tests as Sensing tests. Any intention to  cause violence will result in the spell ending immediately. This spell explicitly does  not protect against any form of astral sensing (e.g. Astral Sight).   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Phantom Lightning
Circle_4.Description: Illusion. This spell appears like the Lightning Bolt spell (p. 278), but is an illusion.  The magician raises his hands toward the sky, then points at the target and makes a  Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, crackling bolts of  electricity fly from the magician’s fingers, striking the target. The magician’s Effect  test determines how much damage is inflicted. The initial Spellcasting test is used  against any additional targets that may result. Additional targets from Successes cannot  cause additional lightning bolts. This spell can be disbelieved (see Disbelieving  Illusions, pg. 267).   Success Levels: Additional Target (+1)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Stop Right There
Circle_4.Description: This spell immobilizes a character. The magician holds up a hand and shouts  “Stop!” as loudly as possible, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic  Defense. If successful, the target’s feet freeze in place—his Movement Rate is 0 and  it cannot be increased, but may otherwise act normally, though he is Harried. Each  round, the target may make a Willpower test against the Effect Step, with a +5 bonus  if he has taken any damage that round. If successful, he regains movement and the  spell ends.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Suffocation
Circle_4.Description: This spell suffocates characters. The magician holds his breath, then exhales and  makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense among the target group  in the area of effect. If successful, affected targets lose their breath and start suffocating—  they are Harried for all actions except Willpower tests and their Movement  Rate is halved until they move out of the area of effect or the spell ends. Each round,  the magician’s Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted. Each round, the  targets may make a Willpower test against the Effect Step to end the effect.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+2 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Unmask
Circle_4.Description: This spell reveals a character’s true physical appearance to anyone watching. The  magician makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful,  he may make a Dispel Magic test with a +5 bonus as a free action against the highest  Dispel Difficulty of any spell or talent concealing the target’s appearance. If successful,  the effects of those enchantments are suspended (not dispelled) and the target’s true  appearance is revealed for a number of rounds equal to the magician’s Spellcasting  rank.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Increase  Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_4.Name: Blazing Fists of Rage
Circle_4.Effect: WIL+4/Mystic and -2 to Physical Defense and other Action tests.
Circle_4.Description: Illusion. This spell engulfs the spellcaster's hand in illusory flames for an attack. The spellcaster holds out their palm, a flaming mystic symbol appears on it then engulfs their hand in flame, and they make a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, they appear to strike the target's pattern with their mystic flames and brilliant red sparks fly from the strike, accompanied by an explosive sound effect. The target suf- fers Mystic damage equal to the Effect test result and -2 Physical Defense and Action tests which aren't close combat Attack tests until the end of the next round. This spell may be placed in the same spell matrix as True Blazing Fists of Rage.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), In- crease Effect (-1 to Physical Defense and Action tests), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Drunken Stagger
Circle_4.Description: This spell causes a character to become inebriated. The spellcaster drinks a beverage, directs a slurred comment at the target, and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mystic Defense. If successful, the target becomes drunk (even if normally unable to be drunk) and suf- fers -2 Social Defense and to Willpower-based tests. Their judgment is also similarly impaired, but there are no specific penalties other than a roleplaying guideline. Once per hour, the target may make a Willpower test against the Effect Step (this Willpower test doesn't suffer the pen- alty from this spell). If successful, the spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours)

Circle_4.Name: False Enchantment
Circle_4.Effect: Gives the item fake magical abilities
Circle_4.Description: and history Illusion. This spell imbues an item with an illusory magical power. The fake power can't be activated, but others can detect and be deceived by it. The spellcaster imagines the fabulous properties they wish the item to have and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the spellcaster makes the item a new Mystic Defense up to the Spellcasting test result. This is primarily to thwart magically obtaining information about the item, but there are other uses as well. If an attempt to magically obtain information about the item succeeds, the investigator obtains the fake information planted by the spellcaster. The item's temporary Mystic Defense and Key Knowledges should be noted at the time of casting, along with the Sensing Difficulty. Tests made to examine the item with magic are Sensing tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days)

Circle_4.Name: Friend or Foe
Circle_4.Effect: Create an illusion of identical corpses.
Circle_4.Description: Illusion. This spell makes it nearly impossible to distinguish between friend or foe in a fight. The spellcaster drags their nails over their face and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense among all combatants-including friends, enemies, and the caster-within range. If successful, they all take on the appearance of corpses with the same height and build as the spellcaster. All Action tests are Sensing tests, though even knowing it's an illusion isn't enough to pierce it.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Puppet Show
Circle_4.Description: Figment. This spell creates the image of a being the spellcaster can con- trol. The spellcaster pantomimes the outline of the figure with both hands and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster creates the image of a creature or humanoid Namegiver of their choice. This can't be a specific creature or Namegiver. Creatures can be no bigger than roughly a brithan or granlain in size and Namegivers no bigger than a troll. The spellcaster can concentrate and control the figment for the round, this includes making appropriate noises for the image and moving around. However, the image cannot leave the spell's area. Anyone closely interacting with the image realizes it's only a figment.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Secret Door
Circle_4.Description: Illusion. This spell creates an illusion to conceal a door, window, hallway, or other opening with an illusion. The spellcaster drags their finger across the target, muttering something about losing their mind and it was just right here, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the affected area is covered by an illusion causing it to appear like a continuation of the surface to those unaware of the illusion. Passersby are allowed Sensing tests if they know there was once an opening here, see someone interact with it, or are specifically looking for it. The area must be sufficient to cover the entire opening.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_4.Name: True Blazing Fists of Rage
Circle_4.Effect: WIL+4/Mystic and 2 to the next Attack or Spellcasting test
Circle_4.Description: This spell engulfs the spellcaster's hand in illusory flames for an attack. The spellcaster holds out their palm, a flaming mystic symbol appears on it then engulfs their hand in flame, and they make a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, they appear to strike the target's pattern with their mystic flames and brilliant red sparks fly from the strike, accompanied by an explosive sound effect. The target suffers Mystic damage equal to the Effect test result and the caster gains 2 to their next Attack or Spellcasting test. This spell may be placed in the same spell matrix as Blazing Fists of Rage.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to next Attack or Spellcasting test)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (2 to next Attack or Spellcasting test), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Bond of Silence
Circle_5.Description: This spell prevents a character from speaking directly (though he can still make  indirect hints) about a specific topic, event, or individual. The magician holds his  breath and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful,  whenever the target attempts to speak about the forbidden topic, he makes a Willpower  test against the Effect step. If successful, he can speak normally and freely and  the spell ends. If failed, his voice falters and he cannot speak until he changes the  subject to one he can discuss freely.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Eye of Truth
Circle_5.Description: This spell allows seeing beyond the veil of illusion with near-absolute clarity. The  magician rubs clean, clear water over the target’s eyes and then makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, the target gains a +5 bonus to any Sensing tests that he makes  against illusions for the spell’s duration.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Additional  Targets (+Rank)

Circle_5.Name: Illusion
Circle_5.Description: Illusion. This spell creates illusory effects. The magician makes a Spellcasting (6)  test. If successful, he creates an illusion, filling an area up to 10 yards in radius. The  illusions created are simple and cannot include Namegivers or other complex images.  For example, an illusory door could swing a few inches, but not open or close entirely.  An illusory banner might wave in the breeze, but could not be removed. The magician  can maintain the illusion beyond the initial duration by making additional Spellcasting  (6) tests up to his Spellcasting Rank in number. If any Spellcasting test fails,  the illusion disappears and the spell ends. Those who interact with the illusion make  Sensing tests using abilities appropriate to the activity. For example, a character opening  an illusory door would make a Dexterity test, which also serves as the Sensing test.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase  Area (+10 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Phantom Fireball
Circle_5.Description: Illusion. This spell creates an illusory ball of flame, similar to that produced by  the Fireball spell (p. 283). The magician makes a Spellcasting test against the highest  Mystic Defense of any character in the area of effect. If successful, a small globe of fire  erupts from his hand, moving quickly to the designated point of impact, expanding  to the size of a beach ball then suddenly exploding in an intense gout of flame. The  magician’s Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted on each target. Those  affected are also Partially Blind (-2 penalty to Actions) until the end of the next round.  This spell can be disbelieved (see Disbelieving Illusions, pg. 267).   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase  Area (+2 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Presto!
Circle_5.Description: Illusion. This spell connects the openings of two small containers just large  enough for someone to put their hand through, such as a hat and a jar, no more than  10 yards apart. The magician must be holding one of the containers and the other  must be visible to him. The magician waves his hand over one of the objects chanting  magic words and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the magician can reach  through the opening of the container he is holding and his hand emerges either from  or into the distant container. The magician may pull back through the mini portal any  object that could feasibly fit through the opening. The openings are linked by a poorly  understood warping of astral space. The warping is real, but the spell takes a number  of shortcuts which rely on the premises of illusions to bend magic in ways which can  be broken to disrupt the link. A successful Sensing test destroys the links and ends  the spell.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Switch
Circle_5.Description: Illusion. This spell switches the magician’s appearance with another character.  The magician makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful,  the magician takes on the target’s appearance, and vice-versa.  Action tests made against the target or magician are considered Sensing tests  (see Illusions, p. 266).   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Clothing Gone
Circle_5.Effect: Gives the perception of a wardrobe malfunction
Circle_5.Description: Illusion. This spell makes a character believe their clothes and armor vanished, leaving them naked and vulnerable. The spellcaster removes an item of their clothing and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mys- tic Defense. If successful, the target is distracted (and perhaps also embar- rassed) by their clothing and armor disappearing-only the target believes this. They suffer -2 to Charisma-based Action tests and Social Defense. Vio- lence negates these penalties, though just threats of violence don't. Action tests that specifically interact with their clothing are Sensing tests. The tar- get must be wearing clothes.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Disguise Metal
Circle_5.Description: Illusion. This spell disguises one type of metal as another. The spellcast- er imagines two types of metal while touching the target and makes a Spell- casting (6) test. If successful, 10 lbs of metal are disguised as the other type of metal. Inspection and effects that can determine metal are Sensing tests. Only the metal's appearance is concealed by the spell, not its shape. Disguis- ing a steel sword as mercury is useless because the blade isn't a liquid at room temperature. The face and perceived weight of a coin can be disguised to make them appear as a higher or lower denomination if they're the same rough size and shape, but handling the coins is a Sensing test, particularly by experienced merchants who may carefully inspect coinage for debasing.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (+10 lbs)

Circle_5.Name: Enter and Exit
Circle_5.Effect: Shows the quickest ways in and out
Circle_5.Description: This spell shows the fastest ways in and out of a location. The spellcast- er looks to the left, then right and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, they unerringly know the most direct routes in and out of the location with- in the area. The route revealed must be one the spellcaster can traverse (in the gamemaster's judgment), though this may not be helpful to their allies. The spell doesn't reveal the location of traps or guards, nor does it provide passwords or other means of entry or exit. If a route avoids these things, the spell shows it. The spell can reveal the existence of hidden passages if they comprise the fastest route, comparing the Spellcasting result against the passage's Detection Difficulty. If successful, the route travels through the passage, though the spellcaster still must find the passage when confronted with the dead end. If the passage is hidden by an illusion, the spellcaster gains a +10 to the Sensing test. The spellcaster loses this knowledge and all benefits when the duration expires.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Ef- fect (+5 to Sensing tests), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Flesh Eater
Circle_5.Description: Illusion. This spell makes the target's flesh look as if it is rotting and falling off. The spellcaster shakes their head, scratches the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If suc- cessful, the target goes into a state of mild shock as they see their flesh crawling with maggots and rotting away before their eyes, and they're Harried. Action tests to inspect or treat injuries, such as Cold Purify and Physician, are Sensing tests. Other actions directly involving the tar- get's skin (e.g., Fireblood) may apply as well at the gamemaster's discretion. Additional threads for Effect inflict addi- tional instances of Harried on the target, see Overwhelmed (Player's Guide, p. 388).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 min- utes), Increase Effect (+1 Harried), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Memory Blank
Circle_5.Description: This spell temporarily suppresses memo- ries. The spellcaster looks at the target, asks, 'What was that?' and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If success- ful, the target forgets the last two minutes. The target can be affected in the middle of what they're doing, in which case they forget what they're doing and why. Each hour or when the target encounters something that tugs at a for- gotten memory, the target can make a Willpow- er test against the Effect Step as a Free action. The target remembers every- thing they forgot if successful or when the spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 min- utes), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Metal Scream
Circle_5.Description: This spell causes screams and wails like metal scraping against metal to surround the targets. The spellcaster grimaces, silently mouths horrible cries, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area (minimum 6). If successful, the area screams and shrieks with a piercing, unnerving effect. Anyone in the area suffers -5 to Willpower-based tests and tests to notice things (e.g., Perception) from the high-pitched screaming.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (-2 to penalty), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2 yards), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_6.Name: Astral Shadow
Circle_6.Description: Illusion. This spell makes living beings in the physical world more difficult to  see in astral space. The magician waves a silk veil over the target’s face and makes a  Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target is easily  missed when viewed in astral space.  Locating the target using astral sensing abilities, such as the Astral Sight talent  (p. 209), is considered a Sensing test. Apart from obscuring his presence in astral  space, the target does not gain any protection against spells or Horror powers, such  as Horror Mark.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_6.Name: Chosen Path
Circle_6.Description: This spell makes others follow a path of the magician’s choosing when they are  presented with a choice of directions. The magician considers a path (a set of doors,  a branch in the road, and so on) visible to him, then indicates his choice and makes  a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, when others are faced with a choice of direction  at the same location, the target group believes they have chosen, but in reality they  select the path determined by the magician. The deception becomes apparent once  they are out of range or the spell ends. Should the target group decide to backtrack  and take the correct path from the same point, the target group makes a Willpower  test against the Effect Step to see if they are diverted down the wrong path again.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Flying Carpet
Circle_6.Effect: Creates the classical mode of transportation for magicians
Circle_6.Description: This spell enchants a carpet or other piece of cloth, no bigger than four square  yards in size, to become a flying platform. The magician vigorously shakes the carpet  or cloth and then makes a Spellcasting (7) test. If successful, the material animates,  floating several inches above the ground. The base carrying capacity of the carpet is  200 pounds. The carpet has Physical and Mystic Defenses equal to the caster and if  the carpet takes damage equal to or in excess of its Death Rating (30), the spell ends.  The magician can verbally control the carpet, requiring his concentration. He may  turn over command of the carpet to another character, who may verbally command it  the same way. The magician may regain control of the carpet with a successful Spellcasting  (7) test. The carpet is not intelligent and is easily confused, so commands  issued to it must be simple, limited to a couple of words at best. The carpet normally  flies a few inches above the ground at Movement Rate 16, but can fly as high as 100  yards if commanded to do so, rising 2 yards vertically for every 2 yards of horizontal  movement. The magician may extend the duration to a year and a day by taking 2  Blood Magic Damage. This damage cannot be healed until the carpet is destroyed or  the spell ends.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Increase Effect (+200 pounds), Increase  Effect (+2 Movement Rate)

Circle_6.Name: Illusory Missiles
Circle_6.Description: Illusion. This spell creates an illusory barrage of missiles. The magician throws his  arm as if throwing a rock, then makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic  Defense of any character in the area of effect. If successful, a hail of illusory missiles  saturates the area. The magician’s Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted  on each target. This spell can be disbelieved (see Disbelieving Illusions, p. 267).  This spell may be placed in the same spell matrix as True Missiles.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase  Area (+2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Memory Scribe
Circle_6.Description: This spell alters a character’s memory, making him recall things differently from  how they really occurred. The magician touches the target and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the magician may change one  fact that the target remembers about a particular topic or event per success. A successful  Willpower test against the Effect Step ends the spell.  The target also makes a Willpower test each time he “recalls” the false memory.  If successful, or when the spell ends, the target’s memory returns to normal. The  magician may extend the spell’s duration to a year and a day by taking 3 Blood Magic  Damage. This damage cannot be healed until the spell ends.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Additional Target (+1)

Circle_6.Name: True Missiles
Circle_6.Effect: WIL+4/Physical and -2 to next Sensing test
Circle_6.Description: This spell looks and acts exactly like the Illusory Missiles spell, p. 311, but packs  less punch than its illusory twin. The magician throws his arm as if throwing a rock,  and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, a hail of real missiles saturates the area. The magician’s Effect test  determines how much damage is inflicted and any target has a -2 penalty to their next  Sensing test as their perceptions become skewed.  This spell may be placed in the same spell matrix as Illusory Missiles.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase  Area (+2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: True Switch
Circle_6.Description: This spell looks exactly like the Switch spell but results in actual physical transfer  rather than simply looking like it does. The magician makes a Spellcasting test against  the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the magician magically swaps places with  the target. The physical transfer is not possible across magical or living barriers.   Success Levels: None   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Background Color
Circle_6.Description: Illusion. This spell creates an illusory crowd of people performing ev- eryday tasks. The spellcaster pantomimes quickly painting a mural of the area with imagined people and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, an illusory crowd of random people fills the area. They're performing the everyday tasks an onlooker expects and are appropriate to the setting (e.g., the expected diversity of Namegivers doing things that make sense for the location).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Crafty Thought
Circle_6.Description: This spell plants a recurring thought in a character's mind to distract, confuse, or influence them. The spellcaster talks conversationally to the target and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mystic Defense. If success- ful, a simple thought is planted in the target's mind. This can be a recurring thought, an image, or a short scene. If it's a scene, there are the following limitations: it can be no more than 6 seconds long, in a room no larger than the common room of a typical tavern (approximately 400 sq. ft.), with no more than four characters involved. The target doesn't have to act on the thought. If the target is distracted by the thought, they suffer -2 to Awareness tests and Social Defense against Engaging Banter. If the recurring thought is tempting to the target, the tar- get suffers -2 to Social Defense against attempts to persuade them to take the appropriate action. For example, tempting Gerra the fence to purchase hot stolen goods, shaving a little more off the price since the item is just go- ing to sit, or ignoring his family's wishes and running away with the man he loves. If the thought is confusing or disturbing, they suffer -2 to Willpower tests and Social Defense against Arcane Mutterings. If the thought isn't believable to the target (e.g., falling for the seduction attempts for someone they are not attracted to), the target can make a Will- power test each minute against the Effect Step. This test is unaffected by penalties to Willpower tests from this spell. If the thought is exceptionally unbelievable, the target gains 2 to +6 depending on how unbelievable at the gamemaster's discretion.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Effect (-2 to penalties), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_6.Name: Dancing Disks
Circle_6.Description: This spell creates spinning disks under the targets' feet. The spellcaster spins around in increasingly tight circles, stops, selects up to their Illusionist Circle targets, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic De- fense among the targets, +1 for each additional target after the first. If suc- cessful, glowing disks illuminated with markings and colors distinct to the spellcaster appear under the targets' feet. Once per round as a Free action, the spellcaster may yell their command word (which must be appropriate, 'Dance,' 'Switch,' 'Turn,' etc., are all common) and every target makes a Strength test against the Effect Step. All targets who fail have their facing changed until their next action; use the Scatter Diagram (Player's Guide, p. 394) to determine their new facing. Facing is determined as a group.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_6.Name: Silent Converse
Circle_6.Description: This spell allows the spellcaster to carry on a whispered conversation with a character within line of sight and range, regardless of the interven- ing noise. The spellcaster whistles a quiet and haunting trill, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster and all affected may use Air Speaking as a default skill if they don't possess it (minimum range 10 yards), Air Speaking doesn't cost Strain if they possess the talent, and they gain +3 to Air Speaking tests. Targets using Air Speaking as a default can only speak to other targets of this instance of the spell. The conversation can be overheard like any other whispers.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Air Speaking tests), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_6.Name: Spotlight
Circle_6.Description: This spell creates spotlights to illuminate characters. The spellcaster clicks their fingers, chooses a number of targets up to their Illusionist Circle, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense of those targeted. If successful, a bright light illuminates each affected target. The Spellcasting test result is a Sensing test against all illusions currently affect- ing the target. If a target tries to avoid detection or becomes affected by an illusion, the result of their Action test is compared against the Spellcasting test result. If successful, the target avoids the spotlight's beam and the light for that target goes out, or the illusion affects the target successfully. If failed, the target's attempt to use stealth fails and the illusion is Sensed by all who can see the target.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_6.Name: Tick Tock
Circle_6.Effect: Gives a bonus or penalty to Initiative
Circle_6.Description: This spell manipulates the speed at which some characters act. The spellcaster claps their hands and taps a foot in time, and makes a Spellcast- ing (10) test. If successful, they choose even or odd before Initiative is deter- mined each round. After Initiative is determined (but before other Initiative modifiers applied after Initiative is determined), they can give each charac- ter with the selected Initiative result (even or odd) either +4 or -4; some af- fected characters can receive the bonus, others the penalty. This changes the Initiative order and cannot reduce Initiative results below 1 as normal.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 and -2), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Dancing Dragon
Circle_7.Description: Illusion. This spell summons the illusion of a ferocious dragon composed of inky  shadows.  The magician uses his hands to make a shadow image of a dragon’s head on a  wall or other surface and makes a Spellcasting (12) test. If successful, the 6 yards tall,  10 yards long and 4 yards wide dragon coalesces into existence from shadows cast in  the area. Each round, the magician may concentrate to designate a target adjacent to  the dragon that it will attack using the magician’s Spellcasting Step. If successful, the  magician may make a Damage test using the Effect Step. The dragon has a Movement  Rate of 16 and may fly over obstacles if there is space. Any test that interacts with the  dragon (e.g. Attack tests, Avoid Blow) is considered a Sensing test.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Silent Stampede
Circle_7.Description: This spell silences the sounds made by a group of characters or animals, including  movement, talking, breathing, horse whinnying, and so on. The magician wanders  around the group touching each person and animal affected by the spell, then  makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense among the target group,  +1 for each additional target. If successful, any sounds made by the target group are  suppressed—all members of the group gain a +4 bonus to their stealth-based Action  tests. Targets may use Stealthy Stride as a default skill even if they do not possess it.  The Detection Difficulty of hearing-based Perception tests made to detect any members  of the target group is increased by +4. The magician may affect up to Spellcasting  Rank targets × 3. The spell does not conceal the target group from sight, smell, or  touch-based Perception Tests.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_7.Name: Stampede
Circle_7.Description: Illusion. This spell creates the illusion of a stampede. The magician stamps and  snorts, then makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense among the  target group within range, +1 for each additional target.  If successful, the targets believe a stampede is about to hit them and become Harried.  Casting this spell in an area where a stampede would normally be impossible  (indoors, on an airship, and so on) allows those affected to make a Sensing test (p.  266) at +5. The magician may affect up to Spellcasting Rank targets.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Twisted Tongues
Circle_7.Description: Illusion. This spell jumbles speech. The magician mutters nonsense syllables, then  makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense among the target group,  +1 for each additional target. If successful, the targets’ words are rendered nonsensical.  For example, someone saying “Kill them all!” might translate to “Slig covo bebop  buttery!” No one understands anything said by the affected targets. Any Speak  Language tests made by affected targets are considered Sensing tests (see Illusions,  p. 266). The magician may affect up to Spellcasting Rank targets.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Vertigo
Circle_7.Effect: –2 penalty to Action tests per success
Circle_7.Description: This spell totally disorients a character. The magician spins quickly around in  circles, making himself slightly dizzy in the process, then makes a Spellcasting test  against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target can no longer tell which  way is up or down and becomes severely disoriented, suffering a –2 penalty to his  Action tests per success.   Success Levels: See text   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 Success), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Walk Through
Circle_7.Effect: Creates temporary pathway through an obstacle
Circle_7.Description: This spell creates a temporary pathway through a wall, gate, or other obstacle no  more than 2 yards thick. The magician makes a Spellcasting test against the obstacle’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, an opening is created, although not a very stable one—it  writhes, expands, and contracts randomly. The successes determine how large the  opening is in comparison to humans.  With one success, one character can wriggle through each round, requiring a  successful Dexterity (7) test to do so; two successes allows one character to move  easily through the gap each round (no Dexterity test required); three successes allows  a number of characters equal to the magician’s Spellcasting Rank to move easily  through the opening each round.   Success Levels: See text   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 success)

Circle_7.Name: Dream Sight
Circle_7.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell allows eavesdropping on a character's dreams. The target must be asleep in a location known to the spellcaster and the spellcaster must possess something personal belonging to the target-the object doesn't need to be a pattern item: something the target used or wore recently, or something from them, such as their hair. The spellcaster weaves one spell thread each hour, sprinkling a little fine dust over the object as they do so. After weaving all the threads, the spellcaster picks up the object and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spellcaster can view the target's dreams-they cannot interact with them, only see them played out. A Namegiver's dreams might give insights into their hidden traits, secrets, hopes, or fears. The gamemas- ter determines what the spellcaster sees and what useful information is im- parted by the viewing.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 hour)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 hour), Increase Range (+1 mile)

Circle_7.Name: Eyes Have It
Circle_7.Description: This spell temporarily blinds targets. The spellcaster touches their eye- lids and clothes with their fingers and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If suc- cessful, pairs of varied and stylized mirror-like eyes equal to their Illusionist Circle appear on the spellcaster's clothing. As a Simple action each round, the spellcaster focuses their gaze on a target with eyes and makes an Effect test against their Mystic Defense. If successful, the target's eyesight is stolen and their eyes become glazed, turning milky white, becoming blinded with a Full Darkness penalty (Player's Guide, p. 387). The appropriate number of mirror eyes transforms into a duplicate of the target's eyes; if there aren't enough mirror eyes available, the test fails. As a Simple action each round, a victim makes a Willpower test against the Effect Step. If successful, the mirrored eyes disappear from the spellcast- er's clothing and the target can see again.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Additional Targets (2 pairs of mirror-like eyes)

Circle_7.Name: Impenetrable Fortress
Circle_7.Description: Illusion. Ritual. This spell creates the illusion of being intensely watched and in imminent danger around one building. The spellcaster places one hand on the target and traces their personal glyph on it with the other while weaving threads-they may state up to Patterncraft rank Names or give a passphrase at this time. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster quietly says, 'I have a bad feeling about this,' and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the area is affected by a subtle illusion that causes all who pass within 4-yards to have the distinct feeling they're being watched by someone or something within the area. Only one building is affected if the area extends to multiple buildings. This is enough to keep all but the most persistent would-be intruders at bay with a 'bad feeling about this.' The feeling intensifies for those who attempt to enter the affected area and they also see imminent danger at every turn. Actions to enter and explore the area, and Sustained actions take twice as long due to these feelings, associated Action tests suffer -2, and Movement Rates are halved due to the increasing paranoia. The subtle nature of the illusion makes it particularly difficult to detect, though not impossible. Inspection of the spell pattern suggests there are hidden magical traps, which is exactly as expected based on their feelings. Anyone initially Named or who speaks the passphrase is unaffected by the illusion and unlikely to know it exists at all.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (-2 to affect- ed tests), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Incessant Talking
Circle_7.Description: This spell forces the target to babble nonsense. The spellcaster babbles nonsense of their own and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spellcaster subtly takes over the target's voice, making them babble meaningless words. The target cannot make Ac- tion tests which require speaking, but can still use Arcane Mutterings. An af- fected spellcaster may still cast spells that don't require speech. Each round, the target may attempt to end the influence with a Willpower test against the Effect Step as a Simple action. If successful, the spell ends and the target regains control of their speech.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Phantom Star Shower
Circle_7.Description: Illusion. This spell creates a phantom spray of ghostly, multicolored sparks, similar to Star Shower (Magic: Deeper Secrets, p. 178). The spellcast- er stretches out their hands, spins, intones words of magic that sound sus- piciously like 'Wheeeee!' and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense of targets in the area. If successful, the sparks spray from their outstretched hands, accelerating through the area and flashing brightly when they touch a living pattern. The targets suffer Mystic damage equal to the Effect test result.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Area (2-yard radius)

Circle_7.Name: Reversal of Passion
Circle_7.Description: This spell reverses a character's emotions. The spellcaster mimics the target's facial expressions, abruptly changes their expression to the opposite of what the target is displaying, and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mystic Defense. If successful, the target's emotions are reversed so they feel the opposite of what they were feeling when the spell took effect-hate becomes love, greed becomes generosity, etc. The target acts in accordance with the new emotion and suffers -2 Social Defense against effects which take advantage of their new emotional state. Each round, the target may make a Willpower test as a Simple action against the Effect Step. If success- ful, they regain control of their emotions and the spell ends. If the target is a questor and the emotion is directly contrary to their Passion's ideals, they may add their Questor devotion rank to their Willpower test.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Effect (-2 Social Defense), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Face Lift
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell allows the magician to convince the target and all onlookers  that the target’s facial features have been removed, leaving him blind and unable  to speak. The magician scrubs furiously at his face with his hands, then looks at the  target and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mystic Defense. If successful, the  target’s face becomes completely featureless. The target may not speak or use any  Talents or abilities that require speech and is effectively “blinded”, suffering a Full  Darkness penalty to sight-based Action tests. Any sight or vocal-based Action tests he  makes are considered Sensing tests.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Form Exchange
Circle_8.Effect: Trade location and appearance with a target
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell swaps the location and appearance of the magician and another  character. The magician looks intently at the target, noting his appearance and  position and then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful,  the magician exchanges form with the target—he now looks like the target  and stands where the target originally stood, while the target now occupies the magician’s  position and looks like him. When the spell ends the illusory image disappears  but the target and magician remain in their new locations.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Other Place
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell connects two entrances that are no more than a mile apart. The  spell must be cast twice within a two-hour period, first on the destination portal and  then on the departure portal.  The magician makes a Spellcasting test against his own Mystic Defense for each  portal. If both tests are successful, characters passing through the departure entrance  instantly cross the distance to the destination. The portals are linked by a poorly understood  warping of astral space. The warping is real, but the spell takes a number of  shortcuts which rely on the premises of illusions to bend magic in ways which can be  broken to disrupt the link. A successful Sensing test destroys the links and ends the  spell.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Increase Range (+1 mile)

Circle_8.Name: Rebel Limb
Circle_8.Description: This spell takes control of a limb. The magician shakes one of his own limbs loosely  at the target, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If  successful, he takes over one of the target’s limbs, controlling its movement as long as  the target is within range. The limb acts with any applicable physical talents or skills  possessed by the owner—an arm can attack, a leg can kick, and so on. Each round,  the target may regain control by making a successful Willpower test against the Effect  Step. If his limb is attacking him, the target gains a +3 Willpower test bonus.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Shadow Spell
Circle_8.Description: This potent spell reduces the effectiveness of a magician’s spells by diverting  most of the power back into astral space. The magician makes a Spellcasting test  against the target magician’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the magician’s Effect test is  subtracted from any spell Effect tests made by the target. If the spell reduces a target’s  Effect test to zero, the spell becomes a harmless shadow of itself—appearance without  substance. Each round, the target may make a Willpower test against the Effect  Step. If successful, the spell ends. This spell does not affect talents, creature powers,  or any form of magic other than spell magic.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Stench
Circle_8.Effect: Prevent actions due to overpowering stench
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell creates the suggestion of a horrid smell, causing those subjected  to it to retch and puke. The magician crushes several blossoms from a stinkweed or  similarly pungent plant and then makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic  Defense among any characters in the area of effect.  If successful, a hideous odor arises that renders affected targets unable to do anything  except try to hang on to their last meal. An affected target must make a successful  Willpower test as a Sensing test to take any action, including movement. If successful,  he can act normally and realizes the illusion, but is still affected by the smell while  he remains within the area of effect. If failed, he can do nothing except double up and  vomit and is considered Knocked Down.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+2 yards

Circle_8.Name: Astral Nightmare
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell deceives others into believing they were sent into the astral plane. The spellcaster brings up memories or imaginings of astral space and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target believes they were suddenly transported into the as- tral plane. The spellcaster must concentrate to control the illusion-if they stop concentrating, the illusion fades away and the spell ends. Action or Effect tests for anything the spellcaster conjures up (e.g. creatures, astral phenomena, etc.) use any Step up to the spell's Effect Step. The spellcaster may voluntarily lower the Effect Step to simulate low-power effects. Damage inflicted on the target allows them to make a Perception test as a Free action as a Sensing test. The gamemaster may allow other abilities to be used in place as appropriate or situations to provoke Sensing tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Dream Send
Circle_8.Effect: Allows the spellcaster to interact with dreams
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. Ritual. This ritual spell allows interaction with a target's dreams. The target must be asleep in a location known to the spellcaster and the spellcaster must possess something personal belonging to the tar- get-the object doesn't need to be a pattern item: something the target used or wore recently, or something from them, such as their hair. The spellcaster weaves one spell thread each hour, sprinkling a little fine dust over the ob- ject as they do so. After weaving all the spell threads, the spellcaster picks up the object and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic De- fense. If successful, they can create dreams for the target while the spell is in effect. The dreams created can cause different effects. If they're used to soothe and relax, the target gains an additional Recovery Test the next day. If the dreams are used to frighten, the target loses one Recovery Test the next day. Other effects are possible, such as creating extreme guilt, or the need to possess an item. The exact effect the dreams have upon the target are determined by the gamemaster, but they cannot be used to force the target to perform an action they wouldn't otherwise. However, they can give -2 to certain actions in a situation which calls those feelings to mind for the next day. These effects require at least 10 minutes and any additional time spent can help refine the circumstances for the spellcaster's purposes. When the spell is cast, the target must make a Perception test as a Free action as a Sensing test. The gamemaster may allow other abilities to be used in place as appropriate. If successful, they immediately wake up and the spell ends. Each previous night in the past week this spell was success- fully cast on the target gives them +5 to the Sensing test (even if they don't want it); this doesn't include the current casting of the spell.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Range (+1 mile)

Circle_8.Name: Leaping Lizards
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell creates a mob of medium, illusory creatures. The spellcaster scatters small objects in the area, such as pebbles, kneels down to touch one, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, 8 illusory crea- tures are created-these can be real or imaginary. Despite the name, any medium creature can be created. The creatures are controlled by the spell- caster and require their concentration to direct their actions. They must stay within range of the creatures or the spell ends. Any Action tests made to interact with the creatures are Sensing tests. The creatures use the statistics listed below; they're the same for all creatures, which means not all creatures are good choices. Regardless of the number of creatures, they only take one action per round and act as a group. If a creature suffers a Wound, it is destroyed. Area effects can cause ad- ditional Wounds for each full multiple of the Wound Threshold inflicted on the creatures. A knocked down creature automatically stands up and has no penalty to their action(s). The creatures are considered a single target for abilities with a variable difficulty based on the number of targets (e.g., Battle Bellow). Abilities that inflict a penalty on a single target (e.g. Mind Dagger's Physical Defense pen- alty) or abilities that provide a bonus against one opponent (e.g. Maneuver, Pack Tactics) cost an additional success against the creatures. If the penalty is a 'rider' on the damage (e.g. Earth Darts) and no extra success on the test, it inflicts damage but not the penalty. The spellcaster can have one active casting o this spell at a time and it can be cast up to Illusionist Circle times per day. Leaping Lizard DEX: 7 Initiative: NA Wounds: 8 STR: 7 Physical Defense: 10 Wound Threshold: 13 TOU: 7 Mystic Defense: 10 PER: 4 Social Defense: 10 Knockdown: 11 WIL: 4 Physical Armor: 5 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 3 Mystic Armor: 5 Move: 12 Actions: 1; Attack: 19 (19) Powers: Immune to Fear Mob Mentality: The creatures cannot be Harried or Blindsided from positioning. Abilities still cause these as usual. Special Maneuvers: Sweeping Attack (Opponent, Close Combat): The attacker may spend two additional successes on an Attack test to treat the attack as an area attack; they must be adjacent to at least two creatures to use this special maneuver. This special maneuver only costs one additional success if the attacker is using a two-handed weapon.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (+1 group), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Phantom Espagra Frenzy
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell creates an illusory espagra spirit, similar to Invoke Espagra Frenzy (The Adept's Journey: Mystic Paths, p. 335), to attack up to three targets within 2 yards of each other. The spellcaster claws suddenly to each side and behind them and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense of the targets. If successful, an illusory spirit dives from the sky in a glimmering blue bolt, attacking the target(s) in a frenzy of violence. The spellcaster divides three attacks between the targets as they wish; tar- gets may be attacked more than once. The target suffers Physical damage equal to the Effect test result.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Pleasant Visions
Circle_8.Description: Illusion. This spell distracts targets with pleasant thoughts and fanta- sies. The spellcaster remembers a pleasant scene or experience, chooses a number of targets up to their Illusionist Circle, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense, +1 for each additional target. If suc- cessful, the targets' heads are filled with pleas- ant thoughts and sensations. They won't take actions, other than appropriate Sensing tests at the gamemaster's discretion. If there are inten- tions to attack a target, the spell immediately ends for all targets.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 min- utes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 min- utes), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_9.Name: Do Unto Others
Circle_9.Effect: Creates an illusory version of a spell
Circle_9.Description: Illusion. The spellcaster casts an illusory version of a non-Illusionism spell which they suffered the effects of and committed them to memory with Infuse Memory. This spell cannot cause damage, be a ritual, and/or be higher than Eighth Circle. The spellcaster repeats the casting requirements for the spell, even if they don't normally know them, and makes a Spellcasting test against the same Difficulty as the mimicked spell requires. If successful, an illusory version of the spell is created that uses the spellcaster's ranks, Steps, Circle, etc., instead (the spellcast- er's Illusionist Circle is used for any Circle re- quirements). The range is the same as the origi- nal spell, but cannot be more than 20 yards (ex- cept for extra threads). Spells with Self or Touch range cannot have their range increased with extra threads. Tests made in response to the spell are Sensing tests as usual. Once a memory from Infuse Memory is used to cast this spell, it's removed and cannot be used again, even if the Spellcasting test wasn't successful.  Success Levels: As per the mimicked spell.  Extra Threads: As per the mimicked spell.

Circle_9.Name: One of the Crowd
Circle_9.Description: Illusion. The target changes their appearance to automatically blend in with the local populace. The spellcaster blanks their mind, relaxing their features as if sleeping, and passes their hand over their face each time they weave a spell thread. With each pass, the spellcaster's face changes appear- ance, perhaps a t'skrang merchant, then a dwarf clown, followed by an ork mercenary, etc., before returning to normal, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the spellcaster nods toward and touches the target. For each success on the Spellcasting test, the target can pass their hand over their face to automatically change their appearance to match the local hu- manoid Namegivers of their current area. This change automatically dis- rupts any attempts to track the target, such as visually, or through talents like Mystic Pursuit or Tracking. If the illusion was penetrated through a Sensing test, this also resets the illusion. Direct interaction with the target is a Sensing test. The spellcaster can only have one casting of this spell active at any time.  Success Levels: See text  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (hours), Increase Effect (+1 success), Increase Effect (2 to Disguise Self), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Revulsion
Circle_9.Description: Illusion. The target's appearance changes into a grotesque parody of it- self, filling those who see them with disgust. The spellcaster touches the tar- get and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mystic Defense. If successful, the target's appearance fills all who see them with revulsion. Each round, the spellcaster selects someone they can see who can also see the target and makes an Effect test as a Simple action against their Social Defense. Each success inflicts -1 Social Defense with additional effects based on the number of successes: 1) They're visibly repulsed and must look away, be- coming uncomfortable and queasy; 2) they must leave the target's presence or become sick; 3+) they become physically sick and must leave. Any hostile action from the target or their allies breaks the illusion.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Effect (-2 Social Defense)

Circle_9.Name: Shift Walls
Circle_9.Description: This spell moves one or more wall sections. The spellcaster forms a square in the air with their fingers and makes a Spellcasting test against the wall's Mystic Defense (minimum 10). If successful, the spellcaster moves up to two wall sections within range, each no larger than the area. The wall section detaches itself from the rest of the wall, following closely behind the spellcaster until it reaches its new position. The spellcaster must concen- trate while moving wall sections and doesn't have to move their maximum number of sections immediately. Once a section is moved and set, it cannot be moved again by this spell while it's active, including other castings of this spell. The walls glide back to their original position over one minute after the spell ends. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 sections)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 sec- tions)

Circle_9.Name: Thundering Walls
Circle_9.Description: This spell causes the walls of a single room or walled enclosure to re- verberate with thunder and lightning, inflicting fear on those within. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the room (minimum 10). If successful, the walls of the room shudder, then roil with clouds flashing with lightning. As a Standard action each round (including the round it is successfully cast), the spellcaster causes one of the following effects: Thunder: This creates an impossibly loud boom reverberating through the room. The Spellcasting test result is compared against the Social De- fense of each character in the room. Affected characters are Harried until the end of the next round. This is a fear effect. Lightning: This creates a horrific barrage of lightning bolts, striking a target in the room. The Spellcasting test result is compared against the tar- get's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target is struck by the lighting and the Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted. Each additional success causes the lightning to strike an additional target in the room, using the same Spellcasting test result and Effect test result. Additional successes on secondary targets doesn't cause additional lighting.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_9.Name: Wall of Unfire
Circle_9.Description: Illusion. This spell creates a wall of illusory fire. The spellcaster makes crackling and hissing sounds, like a roaring fire, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a number of contiguous wall sections equal to the spellcaster's Spellcasting rank appear within range. If a character passes through the wall without a successful Sensing test, the Effect test deter- mines how much damage is inflicted. If the target takes a Wound, their Knockdown test is a Sensing test.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2-yards high), Ad- ditional Targets (+rank sections)

Circle_10.Name: Afterlife
Circle_10.Effect: Give the gift of a good scare
Circle_10.Description: Illusion. This spell makes the spellcaster and the target appear to vanish. The spellcaster hums a dirge, bows to the target, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target believes they're transported to a tribunal where they're being judged by spirits of the dead. Naturally, the spellcaster controls their actions. The spellcaster makes all the appropriate tests required to interact with them and make the target believe they're in the afterlife. The target doesn't need to believe they're actually dead. Unless the illusion is successfully Sensed, the target is likely to uphold their end of any bargains made with the spirits of the dead. The spellcaster must concentrate for the duration, making only the afore- mentioned tests to make the target believe and interact with them. The tar- get's tests interacting with the spirits are Sensing tests. Anyone uninvolved with the spell sees the target swallowed by chains and darkness, hauled off somewhere, while the spellcaster simply disap- pears. Neither actually move, but are concealed by an illusion for the dura- tion.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Interaction tests), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1) Death's Visage Threads: 2 Weaving: 14/19 Casting: TMD Range: Touch Duration: Rank *10 minutes Effect: Give the gift of a good scare Illusion. The target takes on the appearance of a servant of Death, terrifying all who look upon them. The spellcaster touches the target and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mys- tic Defense. If successful, each onlooker sees the target's visage becomes whatever is appropriate to a servant of Death to them. The target makes a Frighten test each round against any onlook- er within 20 yards; they use the spellcaster's Frighten Step if they don't possess the talent, or their Charisma Step if the spellcaster doesn't possess the talent. Anyone affected by the tar- get's Frighten tests cannot approach the target as long as they're affected and most run scream- ing until they can no longer see the servant of Death. If the target directly interacts with any- one outside of a Frighten test, they're allowed Sensing tests as normal.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Frighten tests)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 min- utes), Increase Effect (2 to Frighten tests)

Circle_10.Name: Dilapidated Shack
Circle_10.Description: Illusion. This spell causes a building to ap- pear far more dilapidated. The spellcaster runs their finger down an object, looks at it and is briefly disgusted, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the interior (and exterior if applicable and at the spellcaster's desire) ap- pears dilapidated and wretched. Broken furni- ture, dubious floor coverings, moldy wall hang- ings, unbearable scents, etc. It doesn't create fur- niture from nowhere or conceal existing furni- ture, but causes even the most fashionable and/ or comfortable furniture to look like a carrier for unknown diseases. While the interior reflects the spellcaster's prejudices, not everyone sees exactly the same thing. There are some small incongruous details reflecting their expectations for dilapidation which vary between Namegivers. Action tests related to pursuing these details are Sensing tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Instant Mansion
Circle_10.Description: Illusion. This spell causes a building to appear far more luxurious. The spellcaster runs their finger down an object, looks at it and is briefly pleased, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the interior (and exterior if applicable and at the spellcaster's desire) appears luxurious. Fine furniture, floor coverings, wall hangings, scents, etc. It doesn't create furni- ture from nowhere, but causes even the most broken down furniture to feel comfortable. While the interior reflects the spellcaster's tastes, not everyone sees exactly the same thing. There are some small incongruous details re- flecting their expectations for luxury which vary between Namegivers. Ac- tion tests related to pursuing these details are Sensing tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+10 yards), Increase Effect (2 to Interaction tests).

Circle_10.Name: Insurgent Limbs
Circle_10.Description: This spell takes control of a limb. The spellcaster shakes each of their limbs they intend to control loosely at the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, they take over one of the target's limbs, controlling its movement as long as the target is within range. The limb acts with any applicable physical talents or skills possessed by the owner-an arm can attack, a leg can kick, and so on. This uses the caster's equivalent action for the round (e.g., using Melee Weapons would use up the caster's Standard action). The target cannot use their affected limbs. Each round, the target may regain control on their turn with a suc- cessful Willpower test against the Effect Step as a Simple action. If the tar- get's limb is attacking the target, the target gains +3 to the Willpower test.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1 limb)

Circle_10.Name: Reality Storm
Circle_10.Description: Illusion. This spell causes complete pandemonium within the area. The spellcaster waves their hands wildly, runs in circles, screams, and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the area becomes pure chaos. They have no control over what exactly appears within, but it's at least somewhat be- lievable to those present and onlookers, such that it's not immediately obvi- ous as an illusion. What transpires doesn't damage those affected, but those affected can certainly damage each other depending on their dispositions. Those in the area are driven to excessive reactions and find it impossible to focus on anything else. Some examples might include a dragon attack, army of the undead, spirits on parade, a magical river full of psychic piranha and emeralds running through the air, Horrors attacking, windlings flying through the area dropping orichalcum all around, etc. Interactions with the illusions are used as Sensing tests. Manipulate Reality (Companion, p. 29) doesn't work with this spell for unknown reasons. The most popular theory among Masters is someone made a huge mistake and the spell and all knowledge of it was altered by another Illusionist to prevent that from happening ever again. If true, was it accomplished through an illusion, meaning it could be penetrated and return that functionality (which is better left gone, but it's the principle), or is the change real? If the latter, is there some unaltered version remaining, lost to time, outside of our reality, or known by a ghost master? Regardless, everyone knows applying Manipulate Reality to this spell is an awful idea. But it's the principle.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 hour)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 hour), Increase Effect (2 to Sensing Difficulty), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_11.Name: Haunted House
Circle_11.Effect: Creates a variety of special effects within a building
Circle_11.Description: Illusion. Ritual. This ritual spell turns a house or other dwelling into a hotbed of supernatural activity. The spellcaster must hide an item inside the target building to cast the spell. They light a candle for each thread they in- tend to weave and pour salt on the ground, creating a complex series of sig- ils that look impressive, but are meaningless. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 30 minutes. The spellcaster states loudly, 'They are here, the doors are open!' and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, they can produce phenomena typical of a haunted house, adding one phenome- non each night for the duration. These include moving furniture, tempera- ture changes, creaking noises, altered reflections, voices, blood dripping from the walls, inexplicable shadows, the appearance of ghosts, etc. The spellcaster can also invent special effects, borrowing from local legends for authenticity.
