Activating or deactivating a phenomenon is a Standard action. Some phenomena don't need to be deactivated (e.g., a shadow that goes around a corner and disappears) while others can be delayed for a set amount of time (e.g., in five minutes) or set with a trigger (e.g., when someone enters this room) at the spellcaster's desire. These phenomena cannot directly cause injury, but can do so indirectly-typically by causing victims to fall. Action tests made against the phenomena are Sensing tests. Due to this, it is often best to make them fleeting and not interact directly with inhabitants.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_12.Name: Phantom Daylight
Circle_12.Description: Illusion. Ritual. This ritual spell conjures a small, bright spark that rises upward and expands in size until it replaces the sun. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster rearranges their cloth- ing, ensuring nothing obscures their vision, points their hand upward, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a spark flies from the spellcast- er's hand, streaking toward the sky, where it takes on the shape of the sun. The spark hangs in the same place in the sky; the sun is no longer seen. The light radiates outward for the area. Those affected believe the area is glowing with sunlight. While the light is real, the belief it is sunlight is an illusion. Photosynthesis in plants doesn't occur, only typical respiration, everything behaves as though the sun is out. This leads to very confused animals. Anything that naturally fears the sun refuses to go out, despite there being no real danger. Direct interactions with incongruities of the illusion can elicit Sensing tests with an appropriate Action test.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_12.Name: Phantom Eclipse
Circle_12.Description: Illusion. Ritual. This ritual spell conjures the illusion of a dark cloud that billows upward to block the sun. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster pulls their cloak down over their face or blocks their eyes with their hands and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If suc- cessful, a cloud of darkness in the shape of a large disk rises out and away from the spellcaster, causing those in the area to believe the sun is com- pletely blocked. While those affected believe the area is cloaked in natural darkness, plants grow unaffected, they can still get sunburns, go blind staring at the sun, and anything that suffers penalties from sunlight or bright light still suffers them. Characters with the ability to see in the dark see exactly as well as they expect to with no suspicion due to their vision. Direct interac- tions with incongruities of the illusion can elicit Sensing tests with an ap- propriate Action test.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_13.Name: Soaring Gallery
Circle_13.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell enchants a fine quality carpet no larger than four square yards to become a glorious flying platform. The spellcaster carefully lays out the carpet or rug, which must be of fine quality, though can have seen better days so long as it's well-loved by the spellcaster, and uses their hand to stroke it gently, moving the weave subtly to form glyphs along it. Woven threads turn the carpet threads with the glyphs silvery and irides- cent, creating a new pattern along the carpet. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 30 minutes. Once the threads are complete, the spellcaster spreads their arms wide, presenting the carpet to the world, and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, a very stylish ship of sorts forms under- neath the carpet, which becomes the centerpiece of the gallery floor. The gallery is a strange hybrid of a theater balcony and galley, complete with comfortable seats, embellished railing, and functioning oars. However, the oars serve no function, but certainly make it look grand! The gallery car- ries up to Illusionist Circle Namegivers regardless of their size in style and comfort. Unoccupied space can be filled with up to 200 lbs of cargo which is lashed unobtrusively to the eaves of the gallery. The gallery cruises overland at a very comfortable 15 miles per hour. It continues on its last heading until the spellcaster directs it otherwise. The spellcaster verbally controls the gal- lery, requiring their concentration as a Standard action. The spellcaster can have one casting of this spell at a time and it must be recreated after the duration expires.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 week)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 week), Increase Effect (+5 MPH), Increase Area (2 Namegivers)

Circle_14.Name: My Imaginary Friend
Circle_14.Description: Figment. Ritual. This spell creates an entity for the spellcaster to act through. The spell- caster spends time designing its appearance, outlining it with chalk, and adding details as they weave the threads. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 30 minutes. They make a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the entity becomes real in some ways, but not so much in others. It's a figment with a feature- less and ghostly form most of the time. How- ever, the spellcaster can choose to inhabit this form as a Standard action when it's adjacent to them. When inhabited, it takes on the intended appearance and the spellcaster can control it (including speaking), use non-physical talents (e.g. First Impression, but not Great Leap), and perceive through it as though it's their body while it's within range. However, they cannot cast spells through it. When inhabiting it in this way, it appears real and isn't easy to discern as a figment, though it cannot interact physically by default. If the spellcaster intends to impersonate someone with the entity, they must use Disguise Self and Mimic Voice to actually sell the imper- sonation, but the entity will have the correct form. This allows the spellcaster to impersonate Namegivers which might otherwise be outside of their abilities. Regardless of the form inhabited, the spell- caster uses their attributes at all times. If they use a form with different movement methods, they use that form's default movement abilities and speed. For 1 Karma Point as a Free action at the beginning of the round, the inhabited figment becomes real and they can physically interact with things, cast spells, use physical talents, etc. for one round (until the end of the round). This must be used in consecutive rounds to weave threads to cast a spell. However, damage inflicted on the entity is suffered by the spellcaster during this time. While inhabiting the entity, the spellcaster is unaware of their surround- ings and largely helpless. Leaving the entity requires a Standard action and can be done anywhere. Though this requires them to go pick it up at a later time if they want it back, allowing the duration to expire, or dissipate the entity, effectively ending this spell. They may only dissipate an entity when leaving it. The spellcaster may have Illusionist Circle instances of this spell at one time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Range (+100 yards)

Circle_15.Name: Rewriting (Your) History
Circle_15.Description: Illusion. Ritual. This spell modifies the target's memories. The target must be present largely unmoving throughout the thread weaving and spellcasting process-restrained is acceptable. While weaving threads, the spellcaster mutters to the target about the 'changes' they will be experienc- ing, indicating the memories the spellcaster intends to edit for the benefit of the target. It might even be true. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster whispers to the target, 'We're going to get you fixed up, right away,' touches their forehead, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. This cannot be reduced, even if the target is willing as their mind recoils at the invasive magic involved. If suc- cessful, the spellcaster can alter, add, or remove one memory. This can be through a general request about an event, person, item, etc. as the spellcast- er doesn't read the target's mind in the process-it helps if they got to know the target beforehand so they know what they're looking for, though the magic does the heavy lifting. All modifications must be complete within the time spent weaving threads-if this time ends before all the potential mem- ories are affected, they're lost. The target can be further affected by an addi- tional session and a long period may be required to completely replace the target's memories (and likely personality as well). While this can potentially remove the knowledge of magical abilities, it doesn't remove the ability to use them instinctively as a situation arises. The changes to the target's memories are effectively permanent until the illusion is Sensed. Interacting with the affected memories likely triggers a Sensing test, and if the interaction is notably incongruous with the memory, the target gains +5 to the Sensing test-treating this as an ally attempting to convince the target of the illusion. Sensing the illusion only affects the associated memory and not the spell as a whole; each affected memory is treated as a separate illusion. If Reality Manipulation is applied to this spell to make it real, the chang- es are real and there's no coming back. This rarely goes well for the target in the long run and almost certainly triggers a psychotic break at some point that ends very poorly for everyone. Everyone.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Sensing Difficulty)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 memory), Increase Effect (2 Sens- ing Difficulty)

Circle_1.Name: Astral Spear
Circle_1.Description: This spell weaves energy from astral space to form a phantom spear. The magician  pulls his arm back, then hurls it forward as if throwing a spear and makes a  Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, an ethereal spear  forms that crosses the distance between the two adversaries and strikes the target.  The magician’s Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Bone Circle
Circle_1.Description: This ritual spell “invites” a wandering spirit to live within a circle made of bones  and transforms them into a bone spirit. The bones of at least one animal are used to  construct a circle no bigger than the area of effect. The magician spends one hour  per thread walking the circumference, scratching or tracing magical inscriptions and  placing bones to form the circle’s outline. After completing the circle, he touches the  bones and makes a Spellcasting test against the spirit’s Mystic Defense. If successful,  a bone spirit is summoned to live within the circle.  The spirit is an ally spirit of Strength up to the magician’s Nethermancer Circle.  It is not very intelligent and has limitations on the powers it can possess. The bone  spirit’s powers are selected when the bone circle is created and may only have the  following spirit powers: Aid Summoner, Confusion, Durability, Evil Eye, and Manifest.  It cannot leave the bone circle, but will take physical action against anyone attempting  to enter or already in the circle.  Some spells, such as Grave Message (p. 327) and Spirit Portal (p. 341), require the  use of a bone circle. A magician may have a number of active bone circles equal to his  Spellcasting Rank.  Kicking away the bones and erasing the inscriptions is not sufficient to destroy  a bone circle—it just makes it more difficult to see the circle’s outline and therefore  avoid disturbing the spirit that dwells there. A bone circle can be dispelled, but the  magician must touch the circle to do so, risking assault from the spirit. The bone spirit  will not harm its summoner, but cannot otherwise be controlled.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 months)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 months)

Circle_1.Name: Dark Messenger
Circle_1.Effect: Sends nightflyer to deliver a message
Circle_1.Description: This spell commands a nocturnal flying creature to deliver a message.  It must be cast after sunset and only on a nightflyer already under the magician’s  control, by being an animal companion or through use of the Command Nightflyer  talent, p. 135. The message must be short—no more than twice his Spellcasting Rank  words in length.  The magician strokes the nightflyer, whispers his message to it, names the recipient,  and then makes a Spellcasting test against the creature’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, the creature flies off, unerringly seeking out the designated recipient  and delivering the message in a whispery version of the magician’s own voice. If  the nightflyer cannot reach the recipient before the spell ends or sunrise (whichever  comes first), the message is lost.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Increase Effect (+Rank words), Increase  Effect (+2 movement rate),

Circle_1.Name: Ethereal Darkness
Circle_1.Description: This spell draws a piece of darkness from another dimension. The magician cups  his hands together, then touches the target character, object or location, commands  the darkness to billow forth, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic  Defense. If successful, the darkness summoned expands to fill a 4-yard radius sphere,  centered on the target.  The darkness extinguishes all light, causing those within the area of effect to suffer  a Full Darkness penalty to sight-based Action tests. Ordinary light does not penetrate  Ethereal Darkness. Those with knowledge of Nethermancy are unaffected by  Ethereal Darkness—they can see normally, as if the spell had never been cast.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+2 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Experience Death
Circle_1.Description: This spell allows a magician to see and experience a character’s dying moments.  By doing this, the magician touches the spirit world to recreate the events of the  character’s death. The magician closes his eyes, then points his palms out toward the  corpse and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense (as if he were  still alive). If successful, the last few rounds of the target’s life are played out to the  magician, ending with the target’s death.  The magician must enter into a trance, concentrating to maintain the spell. While  in this state, he senses everything from the point of view of the deceased. Unfortunately,  this also includes taking any damage the deceased suffered before he died.  The Effect test gives the magician temporary Damage Points. Any damage inflicted  on the magician first reduces his temporary Damage Points before being added to  his Current Damage (Mystic Armor provides protection). The magician cannot take  Wounds from the effects of this spell. If his Current Damage equals or exceeds his  Unconsciousness or Death Rating, he still passes out or dies, however.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Life Circle of One
Circle_1.Description: This spell creates a magical circle to repel undead, Horrors, or Horror constructs.  The magician spits into his hand, then outlines a 4-yard radius circle with his fingers  and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the circle resists any undead, Horror,  or Horror construct attempting to enter. The magician makes a Spellcasting test  against the entity’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, the circle repels and harms the entity. The Effect test determines  how much damage is inflicted on the entity.  The spell can only repel a single entity each round. Multiple enemies can easily  swamp the circle if they surround it. Should more than one entity enter, the circle  will attempt to repel the being with the highest Mystic Defense. Given sufficient  preparation time, a magician may cast multiple life circles inside each other, creating  concentric circles to protect those standing in the innermost one and affording some  protection from multiple entities for a few minutes.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Area (+2 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Shadow Meld
Circle_1.Description: This spell allows a character to become virtually invisible in the shadows. The  magician makes a Spellcasting Test against the target’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, the target gains a +4 bonus to all Stealthy Stride tests while near  shadows for the duration of the spell and may use Stealthy Stride as a default skill  if they do not possess it. Though the target can still be seen normally in light, the  presence of light does not end the spell—the target becomes near-invisible again by  simply stepping back into a shadow.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_1.Name: Soul Armor
Circle_1.Description: This spell enhances a character’s Mystic Armor. The magician draws jagged blue  sigils of protection in the air and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic  Defense. If successful, a glimmering suit of blue chain mail appears, momentarily covering  the target before it sinks into his body. The target adds +3 to his Mystic Armor.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Mystic Armor),  Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Soulless Eyes
Circle_1.Description: This spell enhances the ability to intimidate others. Traditionally it gives the target’s  eyes a cruel cast that makes viewers have no doubt about what they are willing  to do, though it manifests in different fashions for some magicians. The magician  glares and smiles cruelly, then places his hand on the target and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target gains a +3 bonus to  any Intimidation tests made for the duration of the spell.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes).   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_1.Name: Spirit Dart
Circle_1.Effect: WIL + 2/Mystic and reduce Mystic Armor by 2
Circle_1.Description: This spell attacks a character with a small dart shaped from astral energy. The  magician pinches himself and flicks his wrist as if throwing a dart, then makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the Effect test determines  how much damage is inflicted. After damage is inflicted, the target’s Mystic  Armor is reduced by 2 until the end of the next round.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (-2 Mystic Armor),  Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Spirit Grip
Circle_1.Description: This spell draws on the malevolent energy of the spirits of the dead to harm others.  The magician touches his victim and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, the Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted  and the target’s Defenses (Mystic and Physical) are reduced by 2 until the end of the  next round.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (-1 Defenses)

Circle_1.Name: Detect Undead
Circle_1.Description: This spell senses nearby undead. The spellcaster smears the willing target's brow with dust or dirt, then makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If suc- cessful, the Spellcasting test result is used against the Mystic Defense of any undead in range. This sense isn't blocked by objects. They sense the number of undead-but not the direction, position, or type-in the area if the Spell- casting result is equal to or greater than the undead's Mystic Defense. This doesn't detect Horrors or Horror constructs unless they are also undead.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Preserve
Circle_1.Description: This spell keeps inanimate matter from spoiling or decaying. The spell- caster touches an item and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense (typically 6). If successful, all items within the area are held in stasis and don't decay for the spell's duration. Spellcaster's often use this spell to preserve cadavers for study, as well as for more mundane tasks such as preserving alchemical ingredients.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Putrefy
Circle_1.Description: This spell putrefies food and other non-living organic matter with a glance. The spellcaster makes a swallowing noise, grimaces slightly, looks at the food they wish to putrefy and makes a Spellcasting test against the high- est of target's Mystic Defense (minimum 6) or anyone eating it. If successful, up to 10 pounds of non-living organic matter immediately decomposes. When cast on a living target, all the food they consumed is affected, typically followed by digestive pyrotechnics and illness. This spell is also used to help with food production by creating necessary fertilizer.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (+5 lbs)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+5 lbs), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Spirit Labor
Circle_1.Description: Spirit. Summoning. This spell-sometimes referred to as 'Pack Bags' by spellcasters without much creativity-summons small spirits to perform household chores, such as packing bags, washing dishes, and sweeping floors. The spellcaster arranges all the materials necessary for the task- such as gathering all the items to be packed, ensuring the chests, satchels, or other containers to be packed are open-and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the air fills with swarming, multi-colored points of light-tiny spirits come to perform the task-which have a collective carrying capacity of 40 lbs. The spirits can get in the way of others while performing these duties.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Undead Struggle
Circle_1.Description: This risky spell allows direct engagement in magical combat with an undead target. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting test against the undead target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spellcaster and target are locked in a magical battle of wills-neither may take any actions other than those required for Undead Struggle until one participant is destroyed or the spell ends. Each round on the spellcaster's turn, the spellcaster makes an Effect test and the target makes a Willpower test. The participant with the lower result takes Mystic damage equal to the higher test result, reduced by Mys- tic Armor. Ties result in both participants taking the same damage. If either participant is attacked by an external source, the spell immediately ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step)

Circle_2.Name: Aspect of the Fog Ghost
Circle_2.Effect: +3 to close combat Attack and Damage tests, +3 to Physical Defense
Circle_2.Description: Binding. This spell coalesces fog from the surrounding air and the caster may bind  a maleficent spirit to a willing target within this fog when the spell is cast. The magician  blows openly into the air and makes a Spellcasting test against target’s Mystic  Defense. If successful, the target gains +3 to close combat Attack and Damage tests,  and Physical Defense for the duration of the spell. During this time, the spirit will urge  them to attack the nearest target each round with the exception of spirits and undead.  If they wish to resist this urge, they can make a Willpower test against the spirit’s  Social Defense (12).  If they successfully resist the compulsion or if they attack any spirits or undead,  the spell will end and the bound spirit will immediately depart (though possibly hold  ing a grudge against the offender) and this spell and Summon Fog Ghost may not be  cast for a full day. This spell may only have one active casting at a time (including  Summon Fog Ghost).  This spell may be stored in the same spell matrix as Summon Fog Ghost.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 bonus), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_2.Name: Chilling Circle
Circle_2.Description: Water–Cold. This spell creates a circle of icy mist. The magician spins round in  a circle, then suddenly stops to kneel, touches the ground and makes a Spellcasting  test against the highest Mystic Defense of all characters inside the area of effect. If  successful, an eerie rune unique to the magician appears on the ground and a cloud  of freezing mist rises from the glowing shape. All characters, except the caster, take  Nethermancer Circle +4 damage each round and their Movement Rate is halved while  they are in the circle.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Area (+2 yards), Remove Targets (-Rank)

Circle_2.Name: Death’s Head
Circle_2.Effect: Use Frighten talent as Simple Action
Circle_2.Description: This spell transforms the magician’s  head into a gore-spattered skull, terrifying  those whom he sets his gaze on. The magician  touches his face and makes a Spellcasting  test against his Mystic Defense. If  successful, he may use the Frighten talent  as a Simple action for the duration of the  spell.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2  rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 bonus  to Frighten)

Circle_2.Name: Night’s Edge
Circle_2.Effect: Adds Step 3/D4 cold damage to weapon/Physical and reduces target’s
Circle_2.Description: Mystic Defense by 2.  Water–Cold. This spell coats a weapon with a layer of inky frost pulled directly  from astral space. The magician exhales into his clenched fist and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, shadowy tendrils of freezing  fog swirl out of his fist and envelop the weapon. The wielder adds a D4 Bonus Die to  the weapon’s Damage test and any target that takes damage from the weapon suffers  a -2 penalty to their Mystic Defense until the end of the next round. The spell can be  cast on weapons made from any material—wood, metal, bone, and so on.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Damage Step), Increase Range (+10 yards),  Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_2.Name: Shadow’s Whisper
Circle_2.Description: This spell allows the magician to hear anything a target speaks near a shadow.  The magician makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystical Defense. If successful,  the magician hears any sound the target makes while he is within 2 yards of  any shadow, including the target’s own. The target may make a Perception test against  the Spellcasting test.  If successful, he detects that he is somehow being observed.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Range (+2 yards from  shadow), Increase Range (+50 yards)

Circle_2.Name: Shield Mist
Circle_2.Description: This spell creates a shield of mist that hardens and moves to deflect blows directed  against the magician. The magician moves his hands over his body while wisps of  mist pour from his fingertips and then makes a Spellcasting test against his Mystic Defense.  If successful, he gains a +4 bonus to Avoid Blow tests (p. 129) against close and  ranged combat Attack tests directed at him. If the Avoid Blow test succeeds, the mist  deflects the blow and the magician suffers no harm. The magician is visible within the  mist and can easily see out. The mist does not hinder his actions   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_2.Name: Summon Fog Ghost
Circle_2.Description: Summoning. This spell coalesces fog from the surrounding air and coaxes a maleficent  spirit to inhabit it. The magician blows openly into the air and makes a Spellcasting  test against the spirit’s Mystic Defense (see below); the magician may choose  a lower effective Circle for the spell effect. If successful, the fog ghost appears where  the magician wishes within the spell’s range, whereupon it immediately attacks the  nearest character (even the magician himself). Each round, the fog ghost indiscriminately  attacks whoever is nearest to it using the Effect Step for Attack and Damage  tests until it is destroyed or the spell ends.  The fog is nearly impervious to physical attacks but the astral pattern containing  the spirit can be disrupted by any attack which targets the fog ghost’s Mystic Defense  (Nethermancer Circle+5) or Physical Defense (Nethermancer Circle+15). To successfully  disrupt the pattern, a character must inflict damage in a single attack equal to or  greater than the Effect Step. Destroying the fog does not harm the spirit, which simply  returns to its netherworld and waits for another chance to harm the living.  A fog ghost will not attack undead or other spirits. The spell cannot be cast in  desert or other very dry environments, nor can it be cast when the temperature is  below freezing. This spell may only have one active casting at a time (including Aspect  of the Fog Ghost).  This spell may be stored in the same spell matrix as Aspect of the Fog Ghost.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_2.Name: Animate Skeleton
Circle_2.Description: This spell animates the skeletal remains of the dead. The spellcaster chants aloud and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the targeted skeleton animates and rises from their resting place within one round. The spell animates one skeleton within range, but the spellcaster can animate additional skeletons within range by weaving additional threads-one skeleton per additional thread. These don't count toward the spellcaster's extra threads limit. The maximum number is their Nethermancer Circle in skeletons, if the spellcaster has access to sufficient remains-such as found in a graveyard or an old battle site. These remains must be intact, or mostly intact, to be animated. The spellcaster can mentally command skeletons within line of sight- this requires their concentration. If left uncontrolled, the skeletons act on their most recent instructions. The spellcaster doesn't have to maintain con- centration, it's only required when they wish to issue commands. When the duration ends or any skeletons move beyond the spell's area, which follows the spellcaster, they collapse into a pile of inanimate bones and must be re- animated. Skeletons use the statistics listed below; they're the same for all Name- givers except windlings, which cannot fly when animated and aren't threat- ening enough to be taken seriously. If a skeleton suffers a Wound, it's de- stroyed and cannot be reanimated. Regardless of the number of skeletons animated, they only take one action per round and act as a group. They gain +1 to the following abilities per skeleton after the first: Attack Step and Damage Step. Destroying a skeleton naturally reduces these bonuses (de- stroying a skeleton doesn't also inflict Wound penalties). Skeletons can be armed with any discarded weapons, unanimated bones, or just their bony claws-the details are cosmetic and the results are the same. This is also true for armor. Area spells can cause additional Wounds for each full multiple of the Wound Threshold inflicted on the skeletons. Abilities that inflict a penalty on a single target (e.g. Mind Dagger's Physical Defense penalty) or abilities that provide a bonus against one opponent (e.g. Maneuver, Pack Tactics) cost an additional success against the skeleton. If the penalty is a 'rider' on the damage (e.g. Earth Darts) and no extra success on the test, it inflicts damage but not the penalty. The skeletons are considered a single target for abilities with a variable difficulty based on the number of targets (e.g., Battle Bellow). A knocked down skeleton automatically stands up and has no penalty to their ac- tion(s). The spellcaster can only have one instance of this spell active at a time. This spell is notably rare and known to few Nethermancers. Those who know it are very reluctant to teach it to anyone, and never to students they don't personally know well. It gives Nethermancers a bad reputation at best, leading many to assume they're necromancers. Anyone meeting a character using or known to use this spell is typically greeted with an Unfriendly or Hostile Attitude (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 142). Animated Skeleton DEX: 6 Initiative: NA Wounds: 1 per skeleton STR: 6 Physical Defense: 8 Wound Threshold: 6 TOU: 4 Mystic Defense: 8 PER: 4 Social Defense: 5 Knockdown: 8 WIL: 4 Physical Armor: 3 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 3 Mystic Armor: 3 Move: 12 Actions: 1; Attack: 11* (11*) Powers: Immune to Fear Mob Mentality: The animated skeletons cannot be Harried or Blindsid- ed from positioning. Abilities still cause these as usual. Special Maneuvers: Sweeping Attack (Opponent, Close Combat): The attacker may spend two additional successes on an Attack test to treat the attack as an area attack; they must be adjacent to at least two skeletons to use this special maneuver. This special maneuver only costs one additional success if the attacker is using a two-handed weapon.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_2.Name: Death Trance
Circle_2.Effect: Puts the target into suspended animation
Circle_2.Description: This spell places a willing or unconscious character into a state of sus- pended animation. The spellcaster holds their breath, closes their eyes, crosses their arms across their chest, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense (if unconscious) or 6 (if willing). If successful, the target enters a state of suspended animation, during which the target appears dead-no heartbeat, breathing, pallor, etc.-but isn't. Because the target isn't dead, they don't decay or suffer the normal symptoms of death, nor do they suffer the ongoing effects of any afflictions they are suffering (such as bleeding, disease, poison, etc.) and they cannot heal. The target re- mains in suspension and awakens with no ill effects when the spell ends if the effect expires during this time. Otherwise the target is still subject to the remaining duration.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours)

Circle_2.Name: Netherlight
Circle_2.Description: This spell gathers pure light from astral space. The spellcaster squints their eyes, points their finger in the air, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a globe of spectral light appears, hovering at the target location, a yard above the spellcaster's head, and illuminates the area. This effect is stationary. Any Horror, Horror construct, or undead attempting to enter the area makes a Willpower test against the Effect Step. If successful, the light goes out and the spell ends. Otherwise, the entity remains outside the area until the spell ends. Casting this spell multiple times in the same area doesn't create layers of protection and has no additional effect.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Area (2-yard radius)

Circle_2.Name: Sculpt Darkness
Circle_2.Description: This spell molds an existing area of darkness or shadow into any shape the spellcaster desires. The spellcaster moves their hands as if sculpting an invisible medium and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the darkness is molded into the desired shape. The spell masks the activity of anyone hidden within the darkness's depths-characters gain +5 to stealth- based Action tests and add +5 to the Detection Difficulty for tests made to detect them (if the Detection Difficulty doesn't already benefit from the Ac- tion test bonus). A shadow can hide only one character. At the gamemaster's discretion, this spell also adds +5 to Spellcasting and Effect tests for other darkness/shadow spells cast from within the sculpted shadow, such as Ethereal Darkness and Shadow Hunter. The darkness cannot change in volume, nor can actual objects be formed from it-only the shape of a given shadow can be altered. The shadow must remain within the range of the spell and cannot move once sculpted. If sculpted again, the original location is used (no sculpting the darkness mul- tiple times to drag it around). This doesn't prevent the darkness from look- ing out of place to perceptive observers.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to affected actions and difficulties), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Translator Spirit
Circle_2.Effect: +3 to Speak Language and Read and Write Language tests
Circle_2.Description: Binding. This spell summons a language spirit and binds it to a willing target to act as a translator. The spellcaster speaks to their hand, 'Seerie, I cannot understand you,' holds their palm to their ear, and makes a Spell- casting (6) test. If successful, the spirit appears and merges with the target's head, a faint and indistinct shimmering surrounding it. The target gains +3 to all Speak Language and Read and Write Language tests, and can compre- hend the dialects of any languages they already speak and read. This doesn't allow them to speak or read new languages (or their derived dialects), but improves their faculty with languages they currently know.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to affected tests) 87 anyone hidden within the darkness's depths-characters gain +5 to stealth- based Action tests and add +5 to the Detection Difficulty for tests made to detect them (if the Detection Difficulty doesn't already benefit from the Ac- tion test bonus). A shadow can hide only one character. At the gamemaster's discretion, this spell also adds +5 to Spellcasting and Effect tests for other darkness/shadow spells cast from within the sculpted shadow, such as Ethereal Darkness and Shadow Hunter. The darkness cannot change in volume, nor can actual objects be formed from it-only the shape of a given shadow can be altered. The shadow must remain within the range of the spell and cannot move once sculpted. If sculpted again, the original location is used (no sculpting the darkness mul- tiple times to drag it around). This doesn't prevent the darkness from look- ing out of place to perceptive observers.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to affected actions and difficulties), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Translator Spirit
Circle_2.Effect: +3 to Speak Language and Read and Write Language tests
Circle_2.Description: Binding. This spell summons a language spirit and binds it to a willing target to act as a translator. The spellcaster speaks to their hand, 'Seerie, I cannot understand you,' holds their palm to their ear, and makes a Spell- casting (6) test. If successful, the spirit appears and merges with the target's head, a faint and indistinct shimmering surrounding it. The target gains +3 to all Speak Language and Read and Write Language tests, and can compre- hend the dialects of any languages they already speak and read. This doesn't allow them to speak or read new languages (or their derived dialects), but improves their faculty with languages they currently know.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to affected tests)

Circle_3.Name: Arrow of Night
Circle_3.Effect: +6 bonus to a missile Damage test and a -2 penalty to Mystic Armor
Circle_3.Description: This spell covers an arrow with a chilling sheath of darkness, forming a silhouette.  The arrow must be fired within one round of the spell being cast or the enchantment  is lost. The magician wraps his hand around the head of the arrow and makes  a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, darkness drawn from astral space wraps itself  around the arrow and adds a +6 bonus to the missile’s Damage test.  If the target suffers damage from the spell, they suffer a -2 penalty to their Mystic  Armor until the end of the next round. Handling the shaft causes 1 Strain to the character  who fires it. The arrow is consumed by the spell, crumbling to dust the round  after it strikes. The spell also works on any missiles, including crossbow bolts, sling  stones or bolts, and blowpipe darts.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Damage), Increase Effect (-2 Mystic Armor  penalty), Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_3.Name: Aspect of the Bone Spirit
Circle_3.Description: Binding. This spell summons the bone spirit from one of the magician’s Bone Circles  within Spellcasting rank x10 miles, binding the bone spirit to a willing target.  The magician takes two bones and throws one on the ground, stomping on it, touches  the target with the other and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic  Defense. If successful, the bone spirit materializes and melds with the target. The target’s  appearance becomes pale and faded, their bones significantly more prominent.  The target gains +4 to Mystic Defense and Mystic Armor.  For the duration of the spell, If the target fails to obey any of the caster’s commands,  the spell immediately ends. If the spell ends prematurely, that Bone Spirit may  not be summoned or bound again for a full day.  This spell may be in the same spell matrix as Summon Bone Spirit.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 miles)

Circle_3.Name: Aspect of the Cowardly Skulk
Circle_3.Effect: Grants a target superior scouting abilities at a cost
Circle_3.Description: Binding. This spell summons a spirit of shadow and temporarily binds it to a willing  target. The magician reaches into his own shadow and draws out handful of an  inky black substance which he then proceeds to smear on the target and makes a  Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the spirit materializes  over the target and merges with him. The target immediately drops into a crouch  and takes on a squirrel-like demeanor.  The target of the spell gains a +4 bonus to Perception-based tests to gain information  from direct observation and Action tests to escape detection.  The target may also move at full speed while avoiding detection. Any active threats  against the target cause him to immediately flee at his fastest movement to safety, after  which this spell immediately ends and the target must flee from any threats for the  next hour. This spell may only have one active casting at a time.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_3.Name: Fog of Fear
Circle_3.Effect: Allows Frighten use against multiple targets at range
Circle_3.Description: This spell conjures a mist that causes fear in those wrapped in its misty tendrils.  The magician wraps his arms about himself as if cold, points to where the fog will appear  and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a luminous, wispy-gray and cold  fog coalesces into a 4-yard radius sphere around the position. Once conjured, the fog  cannot move. The magician may use his Frighten talent against all targets within the  affected area as a single action, causing his menacing visage to appear formed from  the substance of the fog.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 bonus to talent), Increase Range (+10 yards),  Increase Area (+2 yards)

Circle_3.Name: Grave Message
Circle_3.Description: This spell magically sends a message to another Namegiver. The magician speaks  a message no longer than fifty words inside a Bone Circle (p. 318), a cemetery, or a  place where spirits are active, then makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the  message is successfully sent to a specific bone circle, cemetery, or place in which spirits  are active, and addressed to a Named character or “any <insert description> who  happens by.” The message is spoken to the target in a quavering spirit voice only once.   Success Levels: Increase Range (+20 miles)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 days), Increase Range (+20 miles)

Circle_3.Name: Pain
Circle_3.Effect: Cause the target 3 temporary Wounds and reduce movement
Circle_3.Description: This spell inflicts excruciating agony on a character.  The magician makes stabbing motions with his hands and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target is racked with crippling  waves of agony. The effect of this spell causes the target 3 Wounds and his Movement  Rate is halved for the duration of the spell. The spell has no effect on creatures  that don’t feel pain or are used to or inured against it. This includes undead, most  Horrors and Horror constructs, and blood elves.  If used against cadaver men (see the Gamemaster’s Guide) it immediately sends  them into a frenzy.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 Wound), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_3.Name: Summon Bone Spirit
Circle_3.Description: Summoning. This spell summons the bone spirit from one of the magician’s bone  circles (p. 318) within Spellcasting rank x10 miles, allowing the spirit to exist outside  the circle for a brief period. The magician takes two bones and throws one on  the ground, stomping on it, then brandishes the other and makes a Spellcasting test  against the bone spirit’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the bone spirit appears before  him. The bone spirit cannot move more than 10 yards from the magician. If it is forced  to do so, the spell ends and the spirit returns to its bone circle.  The bone spirit obeys any command the magician gives it—entering combat, reporting  happenings within sight of its bone circle, and so on. Bone spirits do not enjoy  leaving their circles, so they resist frequent summoning attempts. Each additional  attempt to summon the same bone spirit within a day requires an additional success.  The bone spirit returns to its circle when the spell ends. If the spirit is destroyed in  combat, its bone circle is also destroyed.  This spell may be in the same spell matrix as Aspect of the Bone Spirit.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 miles)

Circle_3.Name: Animate Spirit Object
Circle_3.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a spirit to inhabit and animate an object, al- tering the item's shape to give it ambulatory capabilities as required. The object retains much of its original shape and is still recognizable as such, and can be no larger than a roughly human-proportioned statue three yards tall. The spellcaster silently points at the target object and makes a Spell- casting (10) test. If successful, a spirit is summoned which immediately takes possession of the object. The summoned spirit is reasonably intelli- gent and can follow complex commands. This is most commonly used to animate and move heavy objects, or impress guests. The spellcaster can verbally control the spirit object within hearing dis- tance, although this requires concentration. They aren't required to main- tain concentration at all times, only when the spellcaster wishes to control the spirit. If the object is too far away to hear the spellcaster, the spirit acts on its most recent instructions, which may be less than perfect, depending on how mischievous it's feeling. However, the spirit never engages in com- bat and immediately departs the object if attacked, leaving it in the location and position. Affecting multiple objects with spirits means they must all be performing the same task under observation by the spellcaster, or the spell- caster can only concentrate on one at a time. Although it willingly obeys the summoner, the spirit is capricious; if it can possibly create mischief while still acting within the boundaries of its instructions, the spirit does so.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_3.Name: Astral Whisper
Circle_3.Description: This spell enables the spellcaster to hear sounds originating in astral space, such as those made by spirits and Horrors. The spellcaster cups their hands to their ears and makes a Spellcasting test. The result is compared to the Mystic Defense of all astral beings (including Namegivers and Horrors in astral form) within the area. If successful, the spellcaster can hear the sounds the entity makes. At the gamemaster's discretion, they may ask the spellcaster to make Awareness tests to identify specific sounds or speech. Many spellcasters using this spell were driven mad, particularly in Hor- ror-infested areas, such as the Badlands and Parlainth. The spellcaster may unknowingly receive a Corruption Point (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 456) when using this spell in a Corrupt region.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to related Awareness tests), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_3.Name: Bone Dance
Circle_3.Description: Binding. This spell binds a bone spirit to the target's skeleton, mov- ing the target against their will. The spellcaster does a herky-jerky jig and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, each round on the target's turn, the spirit makes a Willpower test (the Ef- fect Step) against the higher of the target's Strength or Willpower step. If successful, the target is Harried, their Movement Rate is reduced to 0 for the round (this cannot be increased), and they haphazardly move Step 2/D4-1 yards (minimum 0) in a random direction. The target may make a Willpow- er test at the end of their turn against the Effect Step. If successful, the spirit is expelled from the target. Note: A target cannot have more than one spirit bound at a time. If the target already has a spirit bound to their body (e.g. a binding spell or a spirit power like Enhance Summoner), this spell fails.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_3.Name: Pattern Spike
Circle_3.Effect: WIL+3/Mystic and -2 to an Action test (see text)
Circle_3.Description: This spell drives a mystic spike into the target's pattern, disrupting it, and leaves a trailing thread to the spellcaster. The spellcaster uses both hands to pull at the target as though manipulating a skein, forms a point with the fingers of one hand, drives it into their other palm, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted and the spellcaster can inflict -2 as a Free action to one test the target makes by the end of the next round. This can be used after the Action test result is determined. However, it must be used before any subsequent effects from that test are determined. For example, once a Damage test result is determined, the pen- alty cannot be applied to the Attack test that caused it. If the penalty isn't applied by the end of the next round, it's lost.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (-2 to Action test)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Effect (-2 to Action test), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_3.Name: Spirit Double
Circle_3.Effect: Creates a spirit double of the spellcaster
Circle_3.Description: Spirit. Summoning. This spell allows the spellcaster to summon a spirit to act as their double. The spellcaster stares at a reflective surface such as a puddle or a mirror and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a spirit appears from the surface and assumes a shaggy material form resembling the spellcaster. The spellcaster can issue verbal commands to the spirit, which it loyally obeys. The spirit can travel anywhere its form allows. Though the spirit possesses the same Attribute values as the spellcaster, its Mystic Defense is 10 and knows no magical abilities or skills. It typically performs basic tasks as a trusted, but unskilled assistant, or acts as a decoy. The spirit double can be mistaken for the spellcaster, often at a distance or in a crowd. Detecting the spirit double isn't the spellcaster requires a Per- ception (6) test. Spirit doubles are useless in combat, immediately fleeing at any danger. Any damage inflicted upon the spirit immediately ends the spell.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Disguise Difficulty)

Circle_4.Name: Aspect of the Menacing Tyrant
Circle_4.Effect: Grant target bonuses to social interaction at a cost
Circle_4.Description: Binding. This spell summons a spirit of terror and temporarily binds it to a willing  target. The magician touches and kneels before the target and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the spirit materializes over the  target and merges with him. The target’s posture immediately becomes rigid and all  movements forceful. His features sharpen, eyes gleam with otherworldly cruelty and  shadows cling to him.  While this spell is active, all gamemaster characters are treated as having a Neutral  attitude towards the target. The target gains a +6 bonus to tests based on intimidation  and fear. When the duration ends, any attitude of any gamemaster character  the target applied the benefit to decreases by one level.  Any actions by the target which directly aid anyone other than the bound character  (such as healing them, or even opening a door) cause the spell to end immediately.  If the target does not use the granted benefits, or the spell ends prematurely the spell  cannot be cast again for a full day and the target suffers a -3 penalty to Willpower-  based tests until they have a full nights rest. This spell may only have one active  casting at a time.  This spell can be very addictive, as the feeling of power it grants is a heady rush.  At the gamemaster’s discretion, the spirit may attempt to remain bound to the target  when the duration is over. To expel a reluctant spirit, the character must succeed at a  Willpower test against the Dispel Difficulty of the spell. This test may be attempted at  each duration interval (Rank minutes).   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 to Interaction  (Intimidate) tests)

Circle_4.Name: Dark Spy
Circle_4.Effect: See and hear through a nightflyer’s senses
Circle_4.Description: This spell allows the magician to see and hear through a nightflyer’s senses. It  must be cast after sunset and only on a nightflyer already under the magician’s control,  by being an animal companion or through use of the Command Nightflyer talent,  p. 135.  The magician closes one eye and makes a Spellcasting test against the creature’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, he takes control of the creature’s senses, allowing him  to make Perception tests as if he were the nightflyer.  The maximum distance to which the magician can use the nightflyer’s senses  equals his Spellcasting Rank in miles. The magician’s own senses are numbed, leaving  him unable to react properly to external stimuli—he is considered Harried for any  Action tests other than Perception tests using the creature’s senses.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Perception  tests), Increase Range (+1 mile), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Evil Eye
Circle_4.Description: This spell causes the magician’s pupils to shimmer with a flickering greenish  flame. The magician stares, then closes one eye and makes a Spellcasting test against  his Mystic Defense. If successful, the magician’s Frighten talent can now curse with the  evil eye and gains an additional Success on all successful uses of the Frighten talent.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 additional Success to Frighten tests)

Circle_4.Name: Last Chance
Circle_4.Effect: Grants +4 bonus Recovery test to a dead character
Circle_4.Description: This spell pulls a character back from the brink of death. The spell must be cast  within one minute of the character’s death. The magician makes a Spellcasting test  against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target makes a Recovery test  with a +4 bonus. If he has used all of the Recovery tests available to him that day, the  spell grants him a Step 4/D6 Recovery test. If the target’s Current Damage is reduced  below his Death Rating then he survives, otherwise he remains dead. This spell may  only be cast on a target once per day.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 bonus)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_4.Name: Nightflyer’s Cloak
Circle_4.Description: This spell allows the magician to assume the form of any creature able to be affected  by the Command Nightflyer talent (p. 135). The magician must know the Command  Nightflyer talent as a prerequisite. The magician mimics pulling on a cape or  cloak, then whispers the type of creature he is attempting to turn into and makes  a Spellcasting test against his natural Mystic Defense. If successful, he transforms  into the designated type of nightflyer. Clothing or equipment worn by the magician is  transformed with him—it reappears when the spell ends.  While in nightflyer form, the magician can use all of that creature’s normal abilities,  including flight and enhanced senses. He cannot speak or cast spells, but he can  use talents and abilities requiring only thought or eye contact. The magician becomes  vulnerable to Command Nightflyer talent. The magician may dispel the transformation  anytime he wishes by spending a Standard Action.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increased Effect (+2 to Perception  tests while in creature form)

Circle_4.Name: Viewpoint
Circle_4.Description: This spell creates a one-way window through a wall or similar barrier no more  than 2 yards thick by piercing astral space. The magician makes a Spellcasting (6) test.  If successful, a window up to one square yard in size is created on the wall, allowing  the magician to see through it. The barrier’s material is unaffected—a stone wall is  still as tough as stone, a wooden fence still provides the same protection, and so on.  There is a chance that someone on the other side notices the window. The magician’s  Spellcasting test result determines the Detection Difficulty for attempts to spot the  window.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)

Circle_4.Name: Aspect of the Shadow Hunter
Circle_4.Description: Binding. This spell summons a hunter spirit from a nearby dark or shad- owy area to bind to a willing target. The spellcaster reaches towards the tar- get's shadow, attempts to tear it from the surface, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a hunter spirit with a vaguely humanoid form-fea- tureless, save for faintly glowing red eyes-stalks from the shadows, circles the target, and merges with them. Only the red gleam from the target's eyes betrays the spirit's presence. The target proceeds to track down their quar- ry, which must be one individual, not a group. If the target doesn't know the quarry's Name or have some of their blood (even a smear), all benefits are halved. If the quarry's location is known and locating them is unnecessary, this spell automatically fails. The target gains +4 to tests to locate their quarry (e.g. Awareness, Mys- tic Pursuit, and Tracking), 2 to Attack and Damage test against their quar- ry, and 2 to their Physical and Mystic Defense. The bound character can also detect their quarry through obscuring elements, such as seeing them through magical darkness (including Ethereal Darkness) or hearing them through the din of a crowd. Once the bound character locates the quarry, they must engage the quarry or may make a successful Willpower (12) test to end the spell imme- diately and without penalties. If the bound character engages in any activi- ties that don't directly support tracking and engaging their quarry, the spell immediately ends and the spirit departs. If the bound character is trapped in an area of sunlight without any shadows to hide in, it's banished if it can't get to shade within a round. If the spell ends prematurely (except for the bound character choosing to end the spell), the bound character suffers greatly from everything being too bright with splitting headaches, giving them -4 to all Perception-based Action tests and Action tests that rely on sight, and the spirit cannot be summoned or bound again for a full day. The spellcaster may only have one instance of this spell active at a time (includ- ing Summon Shadow Hunter). This spell may be stored in the same spell matrix as Summon Shadow Hunter.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to tests to locate)

Circle_4.Name: Blood Servitor
Circle_4.Description: Spirit. This spell conjures a minor body for the spell- caster's spy spirits by combining blood with astral energy. The spellcaster pricks their finger and drips blood to the ground, taking 3 Blood Magic Damage for the duration, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, they conjure a physical form for their Orbiting Spy talent from their blood. It takes the form of a small flying creature unique to each spell- caster. The spellcaster can issue simple ver- bal commands to the spirit with a number of words no greater than their Orbiting Spy rank, though the servitor is capable of more complex actions. Because the servitor is linked by blood, the spellcaster can use the servitor's senses to see and hear through them as a Simple action, using their Orbiting Spy Step for any Perception tests, including astral sensing as per the talent. The servitor has a Movement Rate 12 and a De- tection Difficulty 10. If it takes any damage, the spell immediately ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 Detection Difficul- ty), Increase Effect (2 Movement Rate), In- crease Area (2 miles)

Circle_4.Name: Fatal Food
Circle_4.Description: This spell creates an effect in different foods, causing a fatal reaction when they are ingested and combined. Each required thread is woven to a different dish of food, such as a loaf of bread, bowl of soup, plate of roast, etc.-the targeted foods cannot be touching each other or already mixed together. The spellcaster points at the three different foods, envisioning the end result when consumed (which must be obviously unnatural, such as veins and eyes turning black), and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If success- ful, even a taste of each affected food, mixing them together, inflicts a dam- aging poison using the Effect Step [Onset: 1 minute, Interval: 8/1 minute]. Affected food remains potentially poisonous for the duration and any left uneaten after the spell ends returns to normal and can be consumed safely. Each victim can only be affected by one instance of this spell at a time and once affected, the spell's duration no longer matters, only the onset and in- terval of the poison.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Forget Hunger
Circle_4.Description: This spell makes a character forget their hunger. The spellcaster mimes pushing away a dish, refusing more food, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spell induces a strong feeling of nausea. The target ceases feeling hungry and does not feel hunger again for the duration. If the target eats anything, they become nauseous. Each day, the gamemaster makes a test using the Step equal to the number of consecutive days the target has gone without food, causing damage to the target (no armor provides protection). The target makes a Willpower (12) test at the beginning of each day. If successful, the spell effects on the target end. If the target goes three days without food, they grow weary and suffer -1 to Action tests. For each additional day without food, this increases by -1.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days)

Circle_4.Name: Spirit Servant
Circle_4.Description: Spirit. Summoning. This spell conjures a spirit servant to do the spell- caster's bidding. The spellcaster draws a circle on the ground and inscribes it with a personal glyph. The circle must lie undisturbed for a full night be- fore the spellcaster makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a spirit ser- vant emerges within the circle, appearing as an ethereal humanoid of a gen- der and Namegiver of the spellcaster's choosing (unless Named, in which case these features depend on the spirit). The spirit servant loyally serves the spellcaster as a skilled assistant, though unNamed spirits are limited tasks no more complicated than a butler could perform. They can carry up to 50 pounds. Spirit servants are intelligent and capable of speech, though usually quiet. Many spellcasters become attached to a particular servant, Naming them and summoning the same spirit time and again. The spirit servant retreats if attacked (Move- ment Rate 12 flying) and never initiates combat regardless of commands. If attacked, treat the spirit as a non-combatant. The spellcaster can only have one instance of this spell active at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (+10 lbs)

Circle_4.Name: Summon Shadow Hunter
Circle_4.Description: Summoning. This spell summons a hunter spirit with a vaguely human- oid form-featureless, save for faintly glowing red eyes-from a nearby dark or shadowy area. The spellcaster reaches towards their shadow, at- tempts to tear it from the surface, and makes a Spellcasting (8) test; the spirit's Mystic Defense. If successful, the hunter spirit stalks from the shad- ows, circles the spellcaster, and waits to be given a Name, sample of blood, or possession that belonged to their quarry. The spirit proceeds to track down their quarry, which must be one individual, not a group. The spirit tracks skillfully, making tests to locate their quarry at Step 8. If the target doesn't know the quarry's Name or have some of their blood (even a smear)-the spellcaster only has a possession-the spirit suffers -2 to all tests to locate their quarry. If the spellcaster has two of the require- ments (Name, blood, or possession), the spirit gains 2 to tests to locate their quarry. If all three, the spirit gains +4 to the tests and the spellcaster can spend their (not the spirit's) Karma on these tests. If the shadow hunter is trapped in an area of sunlight without any shad- ows to hide in, it's banished if it can't get to shade within a round. If the spell ends prematurely, the spirit cannot be summoned or bound again for a full day. The spellcaster may only have one instance of this spell active at a time (including Aspect of the Shadow Hunter). This spell may be stored in the same spell matrix as Aspect of the Shad- ow Hunter.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to tests to locate)

Circle_5.Name: Aspect of the Cruel Physician
Circle_5.Effect: Grants Recovery tests for a price
Circle_5.Description: Binding. This spell summons a spirit of pain and temporarily binds it to a willing  target. The magician rubs his hands against his clothes or a cloth as if to clean them  then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the  spirit materializes over the target and merges with him. The target’s eyes start to emit  a faint red glow and a menacing grin spreads across his face.  The target of the spell may perform elective surgery on an adjacent, willing character  as a Standard Action. The target of the surgery suffers a Wound and makes a free  Recovery test with a +6 bonus.  If the bound character suffers a Wound, the spell immediately ends. If the spell  ends prematurely or the duration expires and elective surgery is never performed,  this spell cannot be cast again for a full day. This spell may only have one active casting  at a time.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_5.Name: Blind
Circle_5.Description: This spell turns a character’s eyes into empty unseeing pits. The magician claws  at his own eyes and mutters under his breath, then makes a Spellcasting test against  the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target’s eyeballs disappear, leaving dark  empty holes. The “blind” target suffers a Full Darkness penalty to sight-based Action  tests. Each round, the target may make a Willpower test against the caster’s Willpower  Step. If successful, his eyes are restored and the spell ends. Targets who don’t  require vision to “see” are unaffected by this spell.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 to effective Willpower Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 to effective Willpower Step), Increase Range  (+10 yards), Additional Targets (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Circle of Astral Protection
Circle_5.Description: This spell creates a circle of magical protection. The magician walks the circumference  of the circle and then makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic  Defense of all characters inside the area of effect. The Mystic Armor of all allies within  the circle is increased by +4.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Increase  Area (+2 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Dust to Dust
Circle_5.Description: This spell destroys large groups of undead. The magician swallows hard and  makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense among the target group.  If successful, he makes an Effect test to determine how much damage is inflicted  on each target. Undead that take damage in excess of their Death Rating immediately  crumble to dust. The magician may affect up to Spellcasting Rank targets. This spell  does not affect disembodied spirits, corrupt Namegivers, or Horrors, but it does affect  undead Horror constructs, such as cadaver men. If there is any doubt, the gamemaster  determines whether the creature is undead and therefore affected by this spell.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards),  Additional Target (+1 Target; additional target threads do not count toward the  normal extra thread maximum)

Circle_5.Name: Reverse Withering
Circle_5.Description: This spell reverses the effects of the Wither Limb spell, p. 333, restoring the injured  limb. The magician touches the limb, infusing it with energy from another plane  and then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful,  the limb returns to its normal condition. The withered limb is not instantly restored  to full health. The target still suffers a –3 penalty to Action tests made using the limb  until the Wound associated with the withering is healed. After this Wound is healed,  the limb returns to full strength.   Success Levels: N/A   Extra Threads: N/A

Circle_5.Name: Wither Limb
Circle_5.Description: This feared spell atrophies legs, arms, wings, and tails. The magician points at one  of the victim’s limbs and whispers “wither,” then makes a Spellcasting test against the  target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, his Effect test determines how much damage is  inflicted. If the target suffers a Wound, his limb shrivels and weakens as the muscle  wastes away. The target is considered Harried in any round he uses that limb for any  Action Tests. If an arm is withered, the target cannot carry an object weighing more  than 2 pounds in or on that arm, including weapons or shields. If a leg or wing is withered,  the appropriate Movement Rate is halved. If both legs or wings are withered,  the target cannot use the appropriate form of movement, which usually means he is  Knocked Down (no Knockdown Test is made), and can only crawl at a Movement Rate  of 2. The Wound remains until the limb is restored. Only powerful healing magic, such  as the Reverse Withering spell, will allow full use of the limb to be regained.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Astral Seeking
Circle_5.Effect: Summons a spirit guide to answer a question
Circle_5.Description: Ritual. Spirit. This spell summons a spirit to lead the spellcaster on a journey to one of the netherworlds where answers to a question can be found. The spellcaster meditates with incense and anything else to help them focus their mind on the question, spending 10 minutes to weave each thread, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a tall entity ap- proaches the spellcaster gravely and silently, spreading gloom and mystery through the area. The spirit is shrouded in a deep black garment which con- ceals its entire form save for an outstretched hand. It's unknown if there is only one spirit who responds to this spell, or an otherwise unknown classi- fication of spirit. Regardless, the spirit detaches the spellcaster's astral form from their body, causing a Wound, and leads them to a specific netherworld where answers can be obtained. The answer is never straightforward, but rather comes in a vision that provides hints. For example, a spellcaster asking where to find a legendary object might relive events of the object's history (from the object's perspec- tive) that point the way to its current location. Extra threads allow the spell- caster to ask questions which clarify aspects of the vision to help interpret it, though this information isn't free and each question the spellcaster asks inflicts another Wound; the spellcaster doesn't have to ask all the questions allowed by their extra threads. Wounds inflicted by this spell don't cause damage. The spellcaster returns to their body that was left in a state of med- itative torpor when the duration ends. While using this spell, the spellcaster is unaware of anything transpiring to their body. This spell cannot be cast more than once per lunar month or the spell- caster becomes untethered from their body permanently. If this spell affects a target who hasn't learned the spell, they're in imminent danger of becom- ing permanently untethered from their body and it can never affect an un- willing target.  Success Levels: Reduce Duration (-10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 question), Reduce Duration (-10 minutes)

Circle_5.Name: Blood Lost
Circle_5.Description: healed This spell worsens injuries, making them harder to heal. The spellcaster makes wringing, squeezing actions with their hands, touches the target, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target is un- able to naturally heal Wounds until the spell ends-they may still heal Damage Points, though with -2 to all healing tests. Any Re- covery tests made to heal Wounds during the spell's duration are wasted, including the first Recovery test made to heal a Wound each day if the target currently has no damage. This Re- covery test must still be made, even if it has no effect. The target's Wounds can only be healed by magic (including healing aids), although this is hindered as well. If the target would heal a Wound through magic, they make a Recovery test against the Effect Step. If successful, the Wound is healed.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (-2 to Damage healed), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Cold Storage
Circle_5.Description: Ritual. Water-Cold. This spell keeps bodies or food from rotting and is often used during magical experiments or lengthy journeys. All the items must be in an active bone circle at the time of casting (Player's Guide, p. 318). Weaving each spell thread takes 15 min, during which the spellcaster must describe, stack, and sprinkle water over the items. After weaving all the threads, they throw a last handful of water into the bone circle and make a Spellcasting (8) test. If successful, the items stop decaying and can be moved from the bone circle.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 months)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 months)

Circle_5.Name: Creeping Dread
Circle_5.Effect: -2 Social Defense and +4 to fear effects and stealth
Circle_5.Description: This spell creates an area of shadows and dread. The spellcaster whis- pers unsettling things to themselves, crouches, pulls at their shadow, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the area grows dim and shad- ows lengthen more than they should, their forms not quite matching what casts them. The air contains a sense of foreboding and dread, unsettling everyone in the area. Those in the area suffer -2 Social Defense and tests to inflict fear or intimidation, or evade detection gain +4.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (-2 Social Defense), Increase Effect (2 to fear and stealth), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Scythe Ward
Circle_5.Effect: Nethermancer Circle+10/Mystic and -4 to Action tests
Circle_5.Description: Trap. This spell places a ward on an area. The spellcaster marks out the area of the spell (and can make a smaller area if desired) with personal, but distinctive glyphs, often indicative of an execution, says a key word or phrase if desired, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the ward is created and the area takes a gloomy, foreboding feeling. The ward deac- tivates briefly for anyone who utters the correct key word or phrase. This deactivates the ward until they pass through, which could allow someone to sneak through under the right circumstances.
