If anyone proceeds across the ward, an astral scythe materializes trail- ing cold, dread mist and cuts through those in the area, dealing Mystic dam- age equal to the Effect test result and inflicting -4 to Action tests until sun- rise (even if this exceeds the duration of the spell). The ward can affect any number of targets in the same round. A failed attempt to disarm the ward triggers it. Detection: 10 Disarm: Dispel Difficulty Initiative: Effect Step  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (2 Detection Difficulty)

Circle_5.Name: Whisper Through the Night
Circle_5.Description: This spell forces a spirit, whether previously summoned or free (this cannot affect bound or tasked spirits), to answer questions truthfully. A hostile or aggressive spirit must be restrained through Spirit Hold or similar to be affected by this spell and it cannot target spirits actively engaged in combat. Casting it on any spirit doesn't improve their mood and makes any subsequent dealings with the spirit more difficult. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting test against the spirit's Mystic De- fense. If successful, each success allows the spellcaster to ask one question, which the spirit must answer truthfully to the best of its ability (though it can tell half-truths). Each question takes at least one round to ask. If the spell ends before the spellcaster asks all the questions allowed, the remain- ing questions are lost. This spell doesn't give the spellcaster the ability to communicate with the spirit; Spirit Talk or similar may be necessary for that. Whether successful or not, this spell may only be cast on a given spirit once per lunar cycle.  Success Levels: See text  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 suc- cess)

Circle_6.Name: Astral Maw
Circle_6.Description: This spell summons a huge mouth from astral space. Rumors abound that it  summons a real Horror, which does little to diminish the fearsome reputation of magicians,  especially those who practice Nethermancy. The magician makes chewing  noises and makes a Spellcasting test against his Mystic Defense. If successful, a giant  fanged mouth, large enough to swallow a troll and surrounded by writhing tentacles,  is summoned from the depths of the netherworlds. Each round, the maw attacks a  target designated by the magician within range using the Effect Step for the Attack  test. It requires the caster’s concentration to maintain and direct the maw—if broken,  the maw disappears and the spell ends.  The following special rules apply to the astral maw:  Swallow: If the astral maw achieves three successes on its Attack test, any trollsized  or smaller target is swallowed whole. Once swallowed, the Effect test determines  how much damage is inflicted. Victims may escape from the maw by making  a successful Strength (12) test. Those killed while inside the astral maw remain in  astral space but can be retrieved by an appropriate spell or talent.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Bone Shatter
Circle_6.Description: This spell splinters and cracks bones, causing horrific damage. The magician  makes cracking sounds and then makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic  Defense among any targets in range. If successful, he makes one Effect test per Success  to determine how much damage is inflicted, directing no more than two Effect  tests against an individual target. A target who takes a Wound adds +5 to the Knockdown  Difficulty. The magician may affect up to Spellcasting Rank targets.   Success Levels: See text   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (+1 Success), Increase  Range (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Debilitating Gloom
Circle_6.Description: This spell causes otherworldly mist to fill the specified area and sap the life from  those within. The magician rhythmically stomps his feet on the ground while casting  the spell and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense within the  area of effect. If successful, a soul chilling mist seeps from cracks of green light that appear  in the ground. Anyone within the area of effect has their Movement Rate halved  and is incapable of running. Additionally, each round that they are within the area of  effect they suffer a Wound. These Wounds are cumulative each round, but will heal  after an hour of rest following their last exposure to the mist.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase  Area (+2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Friendly Darkness
Circle_6.Effect: Creates magical darkness; +2 bonus to one type of Action test for friendly characters
Circle_6.Description: This spell draws darkness from another dimension, extinguishing nearby light.  The magician cups his hands together, touches the target and makes a Spellcasting  test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area of effect. If successful, the darkness  conjured fills a 6-yard radius sphere centered on the target.  The darkness extinguishes all light, causing those within the area of effect to incur  a Full Darkness penalty to their sight-based Action tests.  The magician may select up to Spellcasting Rank characters within the area of  effect to be unaffected by the darkness and gain a +2 bonus on one type of Action test  (chosen on casting) – Attack test, Spellcasting test, Recovery Test, etc. Ordinary light  does not penetrate the darkness. Characters with knowledge of Nethermancy are unaffected  by the darkness caused by this spell, but unless they are chosen by the caster,  do not gain the +2 bonus.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Increase  Area (+2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Recovery
Circle_6.Description: This spell boosts a character’s life force. The magician touches the target and  makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target  makes a Recovery test with a +5 bonus (this may not be affected by healing aids). If he  has no Recovery tests available, the spell has no effect.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 bonus)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_6.Name: Step Through Shadow
Circle_6.Description: This spell allows the magician to move from shadow to shadow through astral  space. The magician, who must be standing next to a shadow—including his own—  makes a Spellcasting test against his Mystic Defense. The magician must be able to  see the destination shadow, but can use means beyond his natural eyesight to do so.  If successful, the nearby shadows become temporary “gates” at the ends of a “tunnel”  through the astral plane. The maximum distance that the magician can travel between  shadows is 100 yards. He travels through astral space at his normal Movement  Rate and is protected from the hazards therein. Once the magician exits astral space,  the spell ends. If the magician does not exit the other end before the spell ends, he is  trapped in astral space.  Trapped magicians can leave the astral plane via other means, if available to them.  The shadow-gates also allow astral entities to pass into the physical world.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 to Movement  Rate) Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Bone Walker
Circle_6.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell creates a large animated skeleton. The ritual must be performed within an active bone circle (Player's Guide, p. 318) and requires additional bones to make the walker, enough to form at least a troll-sized skeleton. The spellcaster spends an hour weaving each thread to the bones, carefully placing them in the shape of the skeleton; only one at- tempt can be made each day. After weaving all of the spell threads, the spell- caster touches the bones and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the bones join together and animate in the shape of a humanoid figure. The spellcaster can mentally control the bone walker while it is within the area, which follows the spellcaster, although this requires their concen- tration as a Standard action. Otherwise, the bone walker follows the last command received, such as guarding a given place, attacking any living crea- ture that passes, and so on. The spellcaster may have up to Nethermancer Circle instances of this spell active at one time and may be weaving threads for multiple Bone Walker spells at one time. Instead of tracking damage, the bone walker tracks Wounds. After suf- fering the listed number of Wounds, the bone walker is destroyed. If the bone walker is destroyed, the spell ends. The spellcaster cannot repair the bone walker. Extra threads woven for Increase Effect apply the template be- low the stat block for each Increase Effect thread. This spell is notably rare and known to few Nethermancers. Those who know it are very reluctant to teach it to anyone, and never to students they don't personally know well. It gives Nethermancers a bad reputation at best, leading many to assume they are necromancers. Anyone meeting a charac- ter using or known to use this spell is typically greeted with an Unfriendly or Hostile Attitude (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 142). Bone Walker DEX: 6 Initiative: NA Wounds: 3 STR: 6 Physical Defense: 10 Wound Threshold: 12 TOU: 8 Mystic Defense: 9 PER: 4 Social Defense: 6 Knockdown: 12 WIL: 4 Physical Armor: 4 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 3 Mystic Armor: 4 Move: 12 Actions: 1; Unarmed: 12 (12) Powers: Immune to Fear Resist Pain (2) Special Maneuvers: Brittle (Opponent, Blunt Weapon): Opponent attacks against the bone walker with a blunt weapon gain an additional success on a successful Attack test. Each Increase Effect Extra Thread DEX: 0 Initiative: NA Wounds: 2 STR: 2 Physical Defense: 2 Wound Threshold: +3 TOU: 2 Mystic Defense: 2 PER: +1 Social Defense: 2 Knockdown: 2 WIL: +1 Physical Armor: 2 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 0 Mystic Armor: 2 Move: 0 Actions: 0; Attack: 2 (2) Powers: None Special Maneuvers: None  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Effect (see text), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Grave Silence
Circle_6.Description: This spell creates an unmoving zone which smothers all sound. The spellcaster pulls out a glass or crystal bead, throws it at the target location, shattering it, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, no sound can be heard in the area. This makes verbal communication impossible and pre- vents casting any spells that require speech. Any sound-based talents are affected (e.g., Battle Shout), but their result acts as a Dispel test against the spell's Dispelling Difficulty. If successful, the talent takes place as usual and the spell ends. Otherwise the talent has no effect.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Netherwave
Circle_6.Description: This spell summons energy from a nether realm. The spellcaster spreads their arms wide, stretches their fingers out, casts their head back with eyes wide, spins in a circle, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mys- tic Defense of targets in the area. If successful, necrotic energy issues forth from the spellcaster in an undulating wave, temporarily sapping the vitality of those in the area. The Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted on each target and each target suffers -2 to their Initiative test next round.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (-2 Ini- tiative), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Nightmare of Foreboding
Circle_6.Description: Fear. This spell dramatically weakens a victim through sinister dreams. The spellcaster faces the target and throws sand and soot into the air, draws an 'X' in the falling debris, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target experiences terrifying nightmares of helplessness and weakness in the face of danger when they sleep. When the target goes to sleep each night, they make a Willpower (12) test in the face of the terrifying nightmares. Each success allows the victim to get 2 hours of quality sleep, while the remaining hours are not restful. When the victim wakes in the morning, they must make a Toughness (6) test. The Difficulty increases by 2 for every two hours of restless sleep. They suffer a cumula- tive -1 to Action tests and Initiative tests for each failed Toughness test, and an additional -1 if they fail by 5 or more. Receiving a full 8 hours of sleep re- lieves the target's fatigue and resets the penalty, but doesn't end the spell's effect. If the target doesn't sleep for at least 4 hours, they don't refresh their Recovery Tests and 8 hours of sleep are required before most adept train- ing. Spells that reduce sleep requirements (e.g., Air Mattress) are ineffective, but the victim's talents and knacks are still effective. Attempting to sleep additional hours in the day is not fruitful. See Gamemaster's Guide, p. 193 for additional information on fatigue. This spell is often used as a death threat or to cause convenient acci- dents. Rumor has it this spell was first created and used by members of the Hand of Corruption-a secret society-and those who use it are often sus- pected of working with the group.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Tears of the Scourge
Circle_6.Description: This spell calls on the spirits of the legions of people killed, tortured, or emotionally destroyed by Horrors during the Scourge to make others real- ize how terrible it truly was. A little salt water (tears or sweat suffices) is required to cast the spell. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area. If successful, the area fills with the screaming, wailing spirits of the dead and the targets collapse to the ground, weeping uncontrollably-they're incapacitated and Knocked Down. Each round, the targets may make a Willpower test against the Effect Step. If successful, they compose themselves and break free of the effect. The spell- caster and their allies cannot directly or indirectly harm an affected target. If they do, the spellcaster automatically suffers the spell effect for an entire day, with no chance to resist it or break free.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2-yard radius)

Circle_6.Name: Wall of Darkness
Circle_6.Effect: Creates a wall of darkness that blocks attacks
Circle_6.Description: This spell conjures a barrier of darkness. The spellcaster closes their eyes, stretches their arms and hands out, clenches their fists, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a wall of inky blackness appears in the area that blocks all forms of sight from both sides. The wall absorbs physical attacks and spells that pass through any part of the wall if the Attack test is below the Spellcasting result, transferring the energy to astral space. Even successful attacks passing through the wall receive -5 to the test result (this is applied after the wall's effects are determined). Anyone viewing astral space sees brilliant flashes where each attack strikes the wall. The wall has no substance and anyone can move through without being harmed.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (-2 penalty to test results), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (+4 yards wide)

Circle_7.Name: Aspect of the Casual Murderer
Circle_7.Description: Binding. This spell summons a spirit of murder and temporarily binds it to a  willing target. The magician slowly licks the edge of a bladed weapon and makes a  Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the spirit materializes  over the target and merges with him. The target’s fingernails elongate and  blood oozes from the nail bed. The target of the spell is overwhelmed with a desire  for bloodshed and gains a +5 bonus to close combat Attack and Damage tests against  opponents who are Blindsided, Knocked Down or Surprised.  If the target has the opportunity to attack a Blindsided, Knocked Down or Surprised  target, but does not, this spell immediately ends. If the target does not cause  any damage while affected by this spell or the spell ends prematurely for any reason,  they take a Wound. If this spell ends prematurely, or the target suffers a Wound as a  result, this spell cannot be cast again for a full day. This spell may only have one active  casting at a time.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 bonus)

Circle_7.Name: Astral Beacon
Circle_7.Effect: Creates a beacon in astral space
Circle_7.Description: This spell creates a bright flare of energy in astral space, exposing a character  to being Horror-marked as if he had just cast raw magic (see p. 261). The magician  crouches down, then leaps up and throws his arms into the air, making a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target makes all appropriate  tests as though they had just cast a Seventh Circle spell with raw magic in that location,  including Warping, Damage and Horror Mark tests.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+1 Circle)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 Circle), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Bone Pudding
Circle_7.Description: This spell turns a character’s bones into a pudding-like consistency. The magician  eats something messy, like a pudding, and then makes a Spellcasting test against the  target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target’s leg and arm bones become a sludgy  consistency, causing 3 Wounds (but no Damage Points). The usual penalties apply for  sustaining multiple Wounds (see Wound Penalties, p.381). Movement becomes nearly  impossible for the target and he has an effective movement value of 0. The target may  only perform defensive actions and actions which don’t require his limbs to support  any weight. The target’s bones return to their original consistency and the Wounds  may be healed after the spell ends.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+1 Wound)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 Wound), Increase  Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Constrict Heart
Circle_7.Description: This spell constricts a character’s heart with astral force. The magician extends  his hand in a grasping motion and then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s  Mystic Defense. If successful, the target’s heart is squeezed in a vice-like grip. Each  round, the Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted.  The magician must concentrate to inflict damage each round or the spell ends.  The target is immobilized and cannot take any actions which require movement unless  he makes a Willpower test against the Effect Step. If successful, the spell ends.  Some creatures are not affected by this spell, because they are undead and feel no  pain or have no heart or similar organ to constrict.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Foul Vapors
Circle_7.Description: This spell taps into the uglier regions of astral space, drawing vapors to the physical  plane. The magician holds his breath and makes a Spellcasting test against the  highest Mystic Defense among targets within 10 yards from the caster. If successful,  vapors brought forth from astral space completely fill the area of effect. The vapors do  not move once conjured. The magician is immune to the vapors’ effect. Those affected  by the fumes do not have to breathe them, only contact is necessary—the spell affects  both living and undead creatures. Each round, the magician makes an Effect test to  determine how much damage is inflicted on each character. Any characters that take  damage from this are Harried until the magician’s next round.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step), Increase Area (+2 yards), Remove  Targets (-Rank)

Circle_7.Name: Restrain Entity
Circle_7.Description: This spell traps an undead creature, Horror, Horror construct, or physically manifested  spirit. The magician makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, a brilliant circle of red light, limned with gold writing, encircles  the target, preventing it from moving.  339  The target can still communicate, assuming the magician wishes to talk to such  a creature. Each minute, the target may attempt to free itself by making a Willpower  test against the Effect Step. If successful, the restraint is broken and the spell ends.  Any hostile action against the entity immediately ends the spell.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Banquet of Dis
Circle_7.Description: This spell allows survival without food, water, or sleep for extended pe- riods. The spellcaster rubs their stomach, licks their lips, and makes a Spell- casting test against the target's Mystic Defense (6 if the target is willing). If successful, the target chooses one of the following cravings they want to suppress: eating, drinking, or sleeping (see Gamemaster's Guide p. 193). The target spends one Recovery Test for each craving they go without and this Recovery Test isn't refreshed until the spell ends. They must have at least that number of Recovery Tests each day. If the target has only one Recovery Test per day, they can only ignore one craving. The target suffers no lasting ill effects from deprivation of the craving(s) they ignore. However, the target reduces any healing they receive by -2 for each craving they ignore. Each target may only have one instance of this spell active upon them at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Additional Targets (+rank), Additional Craving (+1 craving) Death's Visitation Threads: 2 Weaving: 11/16 Casting: TMD Range: 40 yards Duration: Rank rounds Effect: Immobilizes target with fear Fear. This spell calls upon the spirit world to present a personal horror show to a character. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target sees a detailed vision of their death, the exact method decided by the spellcaster. While watching the vision unfold, the target knows they aren't dying, but the fear and hor- ror have a mesmerizing effect, preventing them from moving or acting, and also suffers -2 to Willpower-based tests. The target may still make defensive Action tests and retreat without penalty. Each round, the target may make a Willpower test against the spellcaster's Willpower Step. If successful, the target shakes off the fear and the spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (-2 to Willpower-based tests), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Desiccate
Circle_7.Effect: WIL+5/Mystic and -2 to next Action test
Circle_7.Description: Water. This spell dries items, turning soaked items dry and desiccating other items. The spellcaster makes a gesture with both hands as if they are wringing out a damp cloth and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense (typically 6 for normal objects). If successful, a wet target becomes dry and already dry targets have virtually all moisture removed, up to Effect test result pounds. This can be useful for preserving food or other organic material. When used against a living target, the Effect test deter- mines how much Mystic damage is inflicted and the target suffers -2 to their next Action test by the end of the next round.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2), Increase Effect (-2 to Action test), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Pattern Ligature
Circle_7.Effect: Extends the range of spells to a specific target
Circle_7.Description: This spell creates a small gap through astral space, forming a pathway that allows the spellcaster to cast spells at the target as if they were much closer; this cannot affect targets in astral space. The spellcaster must be able to see the target to create a path to them. The spellcaster makes tearing and pulling motions with their hands, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, a portal opens to the target-as long as the target remains within range, the spellcaster may cast any spell with a range greater than 'Touch' against them through the portal. The target may make a Willpower test as a Free action against the Dispel Difficulty (Player's Guide, p. 265) each round. If successful, the portal closes and the spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_7.Name: Spirit Bolt
Circle_7.Description: This spell directs a beam of violet energy at a target in astral space, dis- rupting their pattern. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted. This spell doesn't affect anything with a physical form, including materialized spirits, though can affect dual-natured Horrors. The spellcaster can use this spell against a target in astral space if they can see the target, such as through the Astral Sight talent or similar ability (this does not include astral sensing through objects).  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Wit Friend
Circle_7.Effect: Allows a second chance on some Perception-based tests.
Circle_7.Description: Binding. This spell summons a knowledge spirit to assist a willing tar- get with mental activities. The spellcaster taps the willing target's head and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the spirit coalesces before the target and merges with their head, a faint spark in their eyes and air of serenity belying the spirit's presence. Any time the target fails a Per- ception-based test outside of combat, the spirit allows the target a second chance. Any spent resources used for the first test (such as Karma) don't apply to the second, though the character can spend them again if available. If this second test fails, the action fails. The second test isn't compatible with other abilities that grant second tests, such as the Second Chance talent. The target can sincerely thank the spirit for its assistance as a Standard action, causing the spirit to depart and end the spell early without side effects. The spirit finds any form of physical exertion, including combat, be- neath it and immediately departs if the target engages in such. If the spell ends early for any other reason, the spellcaster cannot cast this spell again for a full day. The spellcaster may only have one instance of this spell active at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 min- utes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 min- utes), Increase Effect (2 on re-test)

Circle_8.Name: Aspect of the Astral Savant
Circle_8.Effect: Grants unfettered perception into astral space
Circle_8.Description: Binding. This spell summons a spirit of insanity and temporarily binds it to a  willing target. The magician closes the target’s eyes, whispers sibilantly, and makes  a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the spirit materializes  over the target and merges with him. The target’s eyes become black spheres  lit with an otherworldly flame. The target of this spell becomes partially subsumed in  astral space and perceives it without any filtration offered by the Astral Sight talent.  The target gains +4 to Physical Defense and Astral Sight tests and they do not cost  Strain for the duration.  Additionally, the bound character may study any true pattern by making an Astral  Sight test against the Mystic Defense to gain additional insight into how it functions. If  successful, he gains a +2 bonus per success to any Spellcasting tests and Effect Steps  against the target. Instead of bonuses, he may choose insight into the pattern, particularly  what is not available by standard Astral Sight. This information is at gamemaster  discretion – this is the insight of madness.  Each round, the effects of sensing raw astral space deal damage to the target  equivalent to a magician casting a First Circle spell with raw magic. This spell may  only have one active casting at a time.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 to Astral Sight tests)

Circle_8.Name: Horror Call
Circle_8.Description: This dangerous spell summons a Horror from astral space. The magician must  learn the Name of the Horror he wishes to call, then carefully inscribes a 4-yard radius  circle, painting it white and gold. Casting the spell is debilitating to the magician and  requires the use of Blood Magic, causing him 1 Blood Magic Damage, +1 Blood Magic  Damage for each failed Thread Weaving test during the casting.  This damage cannot be healed for a year and a day. Should the magician break  off the spell before completing it, the magical backlash causes him Step 25 damage  (Mystic Armor provides protection).  The magician touches the circle, calls aloud the Horror’s Name, then makes a  Spellcasting test against the Horror’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the Horror is summoned  from astral space into the protective circle. The Horror remains in the protective  circle until it makes a successful Willpower or Dispel Magic test against the  spell’s Dispel Difficulty, is released by the magician, or the spell ends. The Horror may  attempt a Willpower or Dispel Magic test once per hour—if successful, the spell ends.  Many Horrors will bargain with their summoner, offering great rewards in return  for release into the physical plane. If their offer is accepted, the Horror will usually  pay what is promised, but often returns to take back any treasure at a later date. Few  magicians teach this spell—a character should wonder about the motives of one who  does, because Horrors often make teaching the Horror Call spell to others part of any  deal they make. When the spell ends, the Horror returns to astral space.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 hours)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 hours), Increase Effect (+2 Dispel Difficulty)

Circle_8.Name: Netherblade
Circle_8.Effect: Allows a weapon to affect targets in astral space.
Circle_8.Description: This spell magically enhances a melee weapon so it can be used against opponents  in astral space. The magician touches the melee weapon and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the melee weapon takes on  a dark, smoky luster. During the spell’s duration the weapon cannot be used against  targets in the physical world—it simply passes through them, leaving them slightly  chilled but unharmed. The wielder must be able to see a target in astral space to hit it.  Most often, this means the wielder must use the Astral Sight talent, or a similar ability  or spell.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Damage),  Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_8.Name: Shadow Tether
Circle_8.Effect: Prevents targets from moving and are Harried
Circle_8.Description: This spell anchors characters to the ground by their shadows. The magician may  target a number of characters equal to his Spellcasting Rank. Both targets and their  shadows must be visible to him. The magician makes a Spellcasting test against the  highest Mystic Defense among the target group, +1 for each additional target.  341  If successful, the targets’ shadows are affixed to the ground, holding their owners  in place. Each round, a target may make a Strength test against the Spellcasting test  result to pull free of the spell’s effect. If successful, he breaks free of his shadow. Targets  tethered by their shadows are Harried.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Spirit Portal
Circle_8.Description: This spell creates a doorway into astral space through which spirits may enter  the physical plane or characters enter astral space. The spell must be cast within a  Bone Circle (p. 318) built by the magician. The magician makes a Spellcasting (6) test.  If successful, he punctures a hole into astral space, continuing to widen the opening  until he has created a doorway.  The magician has control over who uses the Spirit Portal, but must be within sight  of the portal to contest entry. To prevent a being from crossing through the portal, the  magician makes an Effect test against the being’s Willpower test. If failed, the being  can pass through the portal. If successful, it is prevented from passing through.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)

Circle_8.Name: Wither Away
Circle_8.Description: This spell causes a character to slowly wither away to the point of death. The  magician utters a dire prophecy about the target, then touches him and makes a Spellcasting  test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, his Effect test determines  the number of pounds of weight the target loses each month (natural Mystic Armor  reduces this). This continues until the spell ends or the character dies. If the target’s  weight sinks below 50 percent of his racial average, he becomes weak and dizzy and  is considered Harried while in this condition. If the target’s weight drops below 25  percent of his racial average, he takes Effect Step damage each month (which cannot  be healed until the spell ends), in addition to the weight loss.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 months)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 months), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)

Circle_8.Name: Armor of Bone
Circle_8.Description: tests This spell forms an armor of bone on the willing target. The spellcaster extends their arms, cracks their knuckles, and makes a Spell- casting (10) test. If successful, the willing tar- get's bones spontaneously grow through their skin (it doesn't feel great) and around their ar- mor, binding it tight against their skin. The tar- get gains 2 Physical Armor and 2 to fear-relat- ed tests (inflicting and resisting). Other characters react poorly to this spell and have an Unfriendly attitude at best (Gam- emaster's Guide, p. 143) and frequently worse. Nethermancers and those who deal with them regularly may be nonplussed and react normally. The target must have bones for this spell to work and it is incompatible with Air Armor.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 min- utes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 Physical Armor), Increase Effect (2 to fear), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_8.Name: Blood Boil
Circle_8.Description: This spell makes the target's blood boil, causing their skin and veins to ripple, and their pores to emanate steam. The spellcaster gurgles bubbling noises and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted. Each round, the spellcaster may make another Spellcasting test-as a Standard action, as usual-against the tar- get's Mystic Defense to inflict damage. If a Spellcasting test fails, the target 'simmers' and takes no damage that round. The spellcaster may not change the target and the target must remain within range. The spell only affects living targets with blood; it doesn't work against spirits, animated statues, plants, most undead, etc. The spellcaster may only have one instance of this spell active at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Damage Shift
Circle_8.Description: This spell shifts damage from the spellcaster to a victim. The spellcaster shakes their clothing vigorously, taps their forehead, and makes a Spellcast- ing test against the victim's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spellcaster cre- ates a connection between their patterns and may attempt to shift incoming damage from themselves to the victim. After a Damage test against the spell- caster is rolled, but before the damage is applied (including any reduction from Armor), the spellcaster may make an Effect test as a Free action. Dam- age from the Damage test-up to the Effect test result-is transferred to the victim and reduced by their Mystic Armor. Any damage in excess of the Effect test result is inflicted on the spellcaster and reduced by the appropri- ate Armor as normal. Once any damage is transferred to the victim, the spell ends, removing the connection. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time. Casting this spell is likely to draw the notice of any Horrors in the area.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step)

Circle_8.Name: Gravelight
Circle_8.Effect: +4 to Attack and Damage tests for affected targets
Circle_8.Description: This spell empowers undead and summoned spirits. The spellcaster raises both arms, spreads their fingers wide, loudly chants foul-sounding magical words to the sky, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a pale light swells from the ground into a misty orb that rises and fills the area, casting eerie shadows. Spirits the spellcaster summoned (those sum- moned with the Summon talent or summoning keyword) gain +4 to Attack and Damage tests while in the area. Undead in the area receive the same benefits.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Attack and Damage tests), Increase Area (+10 yard radius)

Circle_8.Name: Lock the Astral Gates
Circle_8.Description: This spell strengthens the veil between the physical and astral realms. The spellcaster must remain perfectly still with their eyes closed while weaving the threads for this spell and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area (minimum 10). If successful, a dull boom followed by the sound of rasping chains echoes through the physical and astral realms. The spellcaster immediately makes an Astral Interference test with a +6 bonus. In addition to the talent's normal effects, astral sensing and Summon tests, and Spellcasting tests that summon an entity (e.g., spells with the Binding, Spirit, or Summoning keywords) require additional suc- cesses equal to the number of successes on the Astral Interference test. This only affects the spell's area, which does not move once cast. Anything in the area attempting to move between the realms (for example, Displace, Man- ifest, Netherwalk, etc.) must first succeed at a Willpower test as a Simple action against the Astral Interference result.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Duration (min- utes), Increase Effect (2 to Astral Interference test), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_8.Name: Visions of Death
Circle_8.Description: This spell inflicts a vision of death on a Namegiver. The spellcaster touches the target and makes a Spellcasting test against their Mystic De- fense. If successful, the target sees the potential for them to die around every corner. Each circumstance brings vivid possibilities for their demise. This leaves the target disoriented and disturbed, suffering -4 to Action tests. After waking each day, the target may make a Willpower test against the Ef- fect Step. If successful, the target's disorientation fades and the spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Effect (-1 to Ac- tion tests), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)

Circle_9.Name: Astral Materialization
Circle_9.Description: This spell forces an astral entity to materialize in the physi- cal realm for a short period of time. The spellcaster must be able to perceive the target in astral space to use this spell. The spellcaster grimaces, clenches their fists, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If suc- cessful, the target materializes (even if it cannot normal- ly do so), often accompanied by a cold fog and glowing ichor, and remains in the physical realm for the duration. The materialized entity is obligated to take no hostile action against the spellcaster so long as no hostile action is taken against the entity. This prohibition doesn't apply to anyone else and hostile acts from anyone against the entity break this obligation. However, it still must remain materi- alized during this time. This is typically used to force nego- tiations with a spirit who isn't hostile yet, or drag back a Horror attempting to escape. The entity can make a Will- power test as a Simple action against the Effect Step to escape. If successful, the spell ends. Dual-natured entities can only be forced into the physical realm if their physical body is destroyed.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Ad- ditional Target (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Dark Sword
Circle_9.Description: This spell is an incarnation of the power of darkness. The spellcaster grasps an amulet bearing their personal glyph in one hand and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a shadowy weapon of the spellcaster's one-hand- ed Size limit and an appearance unique to the spellcaster forms around the amulet. Regard- less of appearance, it has no special abilities. The area around the spellcaster is filled with darkness, extinguishing all light and causing all within to suffer a Full Darkness penalty. Ordinary light doesn't penetrate. Those with knowledge of Nethermancy are unaffected by this darkness, which moves with the caster. The spellcaster can make close combat attacks with the weapon using their Spellcasting talent for the Attack test and the Effect test determines Mystic damage. The Attack test is a Simple action if the spellcaster used Nethermancy to attempt weaving threads this round. As usual, the spellcast- er cannot make more than one Spellcasting test in a round. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time. This spell cannot be cast on (e.g. Fifth Circle Wizard Karma ability) or by an- other character (e.g. Loan Spell).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_9.Name: Draining Eye
Circle_9.Description: This spell creates an eye that drains energy from another character. The spellcaster points a finger to their forehead and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, a third eye with a pupil that looks like a starry whirlpool emerges on their forehead. As a Simple action, the spellcaster looks at a liv- ing target within range and makes a Nethermancy test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spellcaster temporarily drains the target of life force, imbuing themselves with it. The target suffers -1 per success to their Action tests and the spellcaster gains +1 per success to their Action tests. The spellcaster can affect up to their Nethermancer Circle targets total. Their benefit is limited to the highest bonus provided from all affected tar- gets and a target cannot be affected more than once. The bonus ends if the target dies and this cannot affect undead.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to the Nethermancy test), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_9.Name: Mystic Vessel
Circle_9.Description: This spell conceals the spellcaster's nature from Horrors and any oth- er nosy types by storing their magic inside a willing, living Namegiver. The spellcaster draws their personal glyph on the target-on the crown of their head, between their brows, and over their heart-leaving behind ghostly wisps of magic, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster's mystic essence is placed into the living target vessel for the duration. The spellcaster cannot use any talents, magical abilities, threads, Discipline abilities (including Karma), etc., but also cannot be detected as an adept by any means. Instead, they appear as a mundane Namegiver. Howev- er, the vessel gives off a faint magical aura but doesn't have access to any of the stored magic. The Effect Step determines the Detection Difficulty for any tests to ascertain the true nature of the vessel. The spellcaster can regain their abilities and end the spell at any time by touching the target vessel as a Simple action. If the target dies or the spell ends through some other means (temporary or otherwise), the spellcaster regains all their abilities.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to Ef- fect Step)

Circle_9.Name: Silent Darkness
Circle_9.Effect: Creates an area of darkness and silence
Circle_9.Description: This spell conjures a stationary area of darkness and silence where the caster is standing. The spellcaster closes their eyes and whispers, the mag- ic eating the words as they weave threads, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area (minimum 10). If successful, the area is filled with a stationary, magical darkness that suppresses all sound. Anyone in the area cannot hear, see, or speak, and suffers a -4 pen- alty to Action tests due to the disorientation. The spellcaster is immune to the effects of the spell, but the area doesn't move with them. The Action test result of talents creating light or sound from within the area, such as Battle Shout, act as a Dispel test against the spell's Dispelling Difficulty.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_9.Name: Skin Shift
Circle_9.Description: This spell is patterned after the Horror power of the same name and literally rips the skin from a character. The spellcaster touches their victim with a quick wrenching motion and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target's skin starts to tear free from their muscle and fat, twisting and distorting around their body. The spellcaster's Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted and the victim suffers a Wound in addition to any other Wounds inflicted. No armor protects against this damage. Creatures with no skin or muscle are unaffect- ed by this spell. This spell is illegal in most nations and even knowing it is a crime in Throal.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (+1 Wound)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Effect (+1 Wound)

Circle_9.Name: Twist Emotion
Circle_9.Description: This spell warps the victim's emotions. The spellcaster makes a Spell- casting test against the victim's Mystic Defense. The number of successes in- dicates the degree to which the spellcaster can pervert the victim's feelings. Altering strong anger into simple affection requires one success; turning bitter jealousy into euphoria requires three successes. The gamemaster de- termines the spell's effectiveness, based on the circumstances of the given situation. Victims under the influence of this power may realize their emotions are being manipulated if they make a successful Perception test against the spellcaster's Willpower Step. The gamemaster should make this test for the character. If the test succeeds, the character realizes their emotions are somehow inappropriate, though they cannot control them. The character may make a successful Willpower test as a Simple action against the spell- caster's Willpower Step when the altered emotions arise. If successful, the spell ends immediately. When the spell ends by any means, the victim re- members everything experienced while under the spell's influence.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (see text)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Void Wave
Circle_9.Effect: Nethermancer Circle+6/Mystic and -2 to Action and Initiative
Circle_9.Description: tests This spell creates a powerful wave of roiling blackness in which the faint movement of tortured spirits can be seen. The spellcaster spreads their arms and open hands out wide, spins slowly while calling darkness from the astral realm, and makes a Spellcasting test. This result is compared against the Mystic Defense of everyone the wave touches. The wave is 6-yards wide and extends from the spellcaster to the range. If successful, the wave crashes through the targets and the spirits pull at their patterns, damaging, disorienting, and nauseating them. The Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted to each affected target-any additional damage from additional successes is added to the result to each target as appropri- ate. This damage is only inflicted once. All affected targets also suffer a -2 penalty to all Action and Initiative tests until the end of the next round. This spell cannot be affected by the Range Pattern talent.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to the Effect test result)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Effect (-2 to Action tests), Increase Range (+10 yards long), Increase Area (2 yards wide)

Circle_10.Name: Breach the Astral Gates
Circle_10.Description: This spell removes the distinction between the astral and physical realms in the area. The spellcaster chants while weaving threads, silvery glyphs appearing in the air before them outlining a gate, then kicks down the gates and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the astral and physical realms briefly become the same. This twists the landscape in strange ways-greater astral corruption increases this effect, making the environment considerably more malevolent. Dual-natured entities are forced into one body if both are within the affected area. Damage is resisted with the highest of the target's Physical or Mystic Armor regardless of the normal resistance, and Astral Sight and Awareness are interchangeable. Effects that allow the user to disappear into astral space (e.g. Step Through Shadow) have no effect, while those which store things in astral space (e.g. Astral Pocket) appear as a mundane counterpart. This applies to other abil- ities which may differ or affect only one realm-this list cannot be exhaus- tive for obvious reasons. The gamemaster may want to include some interesting environmen- tal effects from the merging of worlds in this fashion, particularly if the corruption level is Tainted or Corrupt. Examples could include corruption manifesting as black, crystalline briars which inflict Step 4 damage when a character moves adjacent to them (no armor reduces the damage), or as dripping ichor from trees in the area and casting spells inflicts penalties or damage-additional successes can be spent to reduce these effects.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Area (+4 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Onion Blood
Circle_10.Description: This spell is patterned after a power used by some Horrors, it literally peels a victim open. The spellcaster tears a parchment into pieces, plucks a flower, or peels an onion, and makes a Spellcasting test against the victim's Mystic Defense. If successful, the victim's skin and muscle peel away, layer by bloody layer. The spellcaster's Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted each round. If the victim suffers damage from the Effect test, they take a Wound in addition to any other Wounds inflicted. Characters other than the spellcaster make a Willpower test against the spellcaster's Willpower Step. If they fail, they're horrified by what they see and are Harried (this also affects Social Defense) until the victim is out of sight. Use of the spell is outlawed in Throal, except for legal executions of criminals who committed heinous crimes. Many other places in Barsaive fol- lowed suit. Although, most realistically lack the ability to enforce such laws on a high Circle spellcaster.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Effect (+1 Wound), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Restless Death
Circle_10.Description: This spell kills the willing target and brings them back to life. The spell- caster whispers the target's Name, draws ghostly glyphs in the air with their hands, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the willing target's spirit is torn from their body, killing them without dealing additional dam- age, and sends both to an astral realm. The target has no memory of this place, which is for the best. They return at the end of the next round; the Effect test result determines how much damage they heal. If the target's Current Damage is reduced below their Death Rating, they're returned to life and all ongoing effects on them end, including poison, spells (and the negative effects of prematurely ended binding spells), talents, fear, etc. Some effects may not be removed-most often blood magic-such as oaths sworn, Horror marks, etc. However, the target suffers a Pattern Wound which may not be healed by any means until a full month (30 days) passes.

Circle_10.Name: Soul Trap
Circle_10.Description: This spell traps the essence of a dead Namegiver in their body for a short time. The target cannot be dead for more than the spellcaster's Neth- ermancer Circle hours (this is separate from the duration). The spellcaster touches the corpse and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the pe- riod during which the target can be saved from final death through magical forms, such as a last chance salve, is extended by the duration. The spellcast- er can cast this spell once per day.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_10.Name: Spirit Tempest
Circle_10.Description: This spell summons minor tasked spirits to attack opponents in the area. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area (minimum 10). If successful, the spellcaster summons a vortex of vaguely humanoid spirits with glowing green eyes who attack all opponents in the area by passing through their body and clawing at their pattern. The spellcaster's Effect test determines how much Mystic damage is inflicted each round. Everyone-except the spellcaster-in the area is Har- ried. This includes the spellcaster's allies.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Area (2 yards), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Tap Horror Karma
Circle_10.Effect: Like taking Karma from a Horror
Circle_10.Description: This spell drains Karma Points from a Horror or Horror construct. The spellcaster reaches out with both hands toward the target, grips and makes a wrenching motion, as if tearing something out, draws their personal glyph over their heart, leaving shadowy magic leaking from it, and makes a Spellcasting test against the Horror or Horror construct's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spellcaster steals 1 Karma Point from the target each round, cleansing any astral corruption from the source and adding it into their Kar- ma pool. The spellcaster uses their Karma Step for these Karma Points, and cannot steal Karma from a target with no Karma Points.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 Kar- ma Point per round), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_11.Name: Journey to Life
Circle_11.Effect: Guides a dead character's essence back to
Circle_11.Description: their body Ritual. This spell guides a deceased character's spirit back from the Death's realm to their body, giv- ing them another chance at coming back to life. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster snakes a tendril of energy through a deceased character into the astral plane and be- yond. The tendril acts as a beacon and a pathway for the essence of the deceased. This is stressful and each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test costs 2 Karma Points and 5 Strain in addition to other costs. The spellcaster touches the body and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the target's essence finds its way back to the body. This journey takes as many days to complete as the deceased was dead before the spell was cast. Once the journey is com- plete, the character's Current Damage is reduced to one below their Death Rating. If the target died outside the duration, the spell fails. Still, there are some deaths the character cannot come back from. Such as sacrifice in a blood magic ritual, some powers used by dragons, Horrors, and the Denairastas. Anything where the character's essence is entirely consumed or scat- tered. The spellcaster must wait a year and a day before casting this spell again.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days)

Circle_11.Name: Walking Dead
Circle_11.Description: Ritual. This spell raises a recently-deceased Namegiver as a zombie. The target must have been killed within the last Nethermancer Cir- cle days. The spellcaster anoints the body with ointment and glyphs while weaving threads. Each Thread Weaving test takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster chants aloud and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the zombie rises at the end of the round. The spellcaster can have up to their Nether- mancer Circle in zombies at one time. The spellcaster can mentally command zombies within line of sight- this requires their concentration as a Standard action. If left uncontrolled, zombies act on their most recent instructions. They must remain within the spell's area, which follows the spellcaster. If they leave it, the zombies stand and mill around aimlessly until the spellcaster moves for them to be in the area again. They must be given new instructions. Zombies can be armed with any discarded weapons or just their meaty fists-the details are cosmetic and the results are the same. This is also true for armor. Zombies can understand complex commands such as, 'Wait for the next person to enter this room and kill them.' If a zombie is killed- again-it crumples to the ground, probably horribly dismembered and cannot be raised as a zombie again. However, they are suitable as a skeleton. Responsible Nethermancers always recycle bodies. Zombies use the statistics listed below; they're the same for all Name- givers except windlings, which cannot fly when animated and aren't threatening enough to be taken seriously. If a zombie suffers a Wound, it's destroyed. Regardless of the number of zombies animated, they only take one action per round and act as a group. They gain +1 to the following abil- ities per zombie after the first: Attack Step and Damage Step. Destroying a zombie naturally reduces these bonuses (destroying a zombie does not also inflict Wound penalties). Area spells can cause additional Wounds for each full multiple of the Wound Threshold inflicted on the zombies. Abilities that inflict a penalty on a single target (e.g. Mind Dagger's Physical Defense penalty) or abilities that provide a bonus against one opponent (e.g. Maneuver, Pack Tactics) cost an additional success against the zombie. If the penalty is a 'rider' on the dam- age (e.g. Earth Darts) and no extra success on the test, it inflicts damage but not the penalty. The zombies are considered a single target for abilities with a variable difficulty based on the number of targets (e.g., Battle Bellow). A knocked down zombie automatically stands up and has no penalty to their action(s). Zombie DEX: 6 Initiative: NA Wounds: 1 per zombie STR: 8 Physical Defense: 11 Wound Threshold: 10 TOU: 7 Mystic Defense: 10 PER: 5 Social Defense: 14 Knockdown: 8 WIL: 5 Physical Armor: 7 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 4 Mystic Armor: 7 Move: 10 Actions: 1; Attack: 18* (18*) Powers: Mob Mentality: The zombie cannot be Harried or Blindsided from po- sitioning. Abilities still cause these as usual. Special Maneuvers: Sweeping Attack (Opponent, Close Combat): The attacker may spend two additional successes on an Attack test to treat the attack as an area attack; they must be adjacent to at least two zombies to use this special maneuver. This special maneuver only costs one additional success if the attacker is using a two-handed weapon.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_12.Name: Crop Blight
Circle_12.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell blights an area. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour and the spellcaster may only attempt to weave a thread once a day. The spellcaster stands in the area as they weave each thread, trampling down an increasingly large patch each time. After all threads are woven, the spellcaster uproots a small plant, smashes it into pulp, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, all plant life in the area withers and dies within the hour. No plants grow there for the dura- tion.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 month)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 month), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_12.Name: Eclipse
Circle_12.Effect: Since the dawn of time, man dreamed of blocking out the sun.
Circle_12.Description: You get to do that. Ritual. This ritual spell conjures a dark cloud that billows upward to block the sun. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster pulls their cloak down over their face or blocks their eyes with their hands and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a cloud of darkness in the shape of a large disk rises out and away from the spellcaster, completely blocking the sun.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_13.Name: Gateway
Circle_13.Effect: Creates a path through astral space
Circle_13.Description: Ritual. This spell opens a rift in astral space, creating a path connecting two of the spellcaster's bone circles. The spellcaster marks out the loca- tion of the gateway and inscribes it with glyphs using ash, chalk, or salt. Each thread takes 10 minutes to weave. The spellcaster strikes through the glyphs representing the astral and physical realms and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, a gateway appears (3-yards tall and 2-yards wide) outlined by pinwheels of green sparks. The entrance gateway is stable for the duration, after which it closes. The path through astral space is safe for an equal number of minutes. For example, a Duration of 13 rounds means the entrance can be used for 13 rounds, while the path is safe for 13 min- utes. The exit closes after everyone exits the gateway or at sunrise. Charac- ters exiting the gateway may still need to deal with the waiting bone spirit. The path through astral space like a glowing green tunnel covered in sparks and silvery runes extending through the astral landscape. It enables the travelers to move at incredible speeds and shields them from ambient corruption and astral denizens. While they cannot interact with the trav- elers, the snaking tunnel may attract their attention. Characters within the tunnel cannot interact with astral space or perceive anything outside the tunnel. One character can pass through the gateway each round and travels at a rate of 10 miles per minute. Once the path is no longer safe, the silvery runes flicker and fade and the protections against astral corruption begin to fail. Characters take Step 17 damage each minute. The adept's natural Mys- tic Armor plus their Netherwalk rank (if any) protect against this damage.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 char- acter per round), Increase Range (+50 miles)

Circle_13.Name: Moon Shadow
Circle_13.Description: Ritual. This spell creates a pale duplicate of a character's shadow. The spellcaster requires a pattern item for the target character and must cast the spell at night, under the light of the moon. The spellcaster sketches the target in white sand or ash on the ground using a silver dagger while weav- ing the threads. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes 10 minutes. After the threads are woven, the spellcaster shuffles from one foot to the other and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, part of the victim's shadow is stolen, creating a second light-gray shadow in its place. This second shadow forms at the target's feet, then glides to the spellcaster. The target's remaining shadow lightens slightly. Even in stark light, their shadow is, at best, a light, wavering gray. Once created, the spell's effect has limitless range. When the target speaks, the shadow speaks the same words. The spellcaster can hear whatever the target hears through the moon shadow by concentrating as a Standard action. The spellcaster can harm the target by plunging a silver dagger into the moon shadow and mak- ing an Effect test to determine the damage is inflicted. This immediately ends the spell. Dispel Magic and similar must be used on the moon shadow, not the target.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Effect (2), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_14.Name: Call Forth the Army of Decay
Circle_14.Effect: Raises an army of the dead
Circle_14.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell animates corpses, creating a veritable army of the dead. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour and the spell must be performed in one of the spellcaster's active bone circles. Each hour, the spellcaster makes a Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) and pours the blood of a new sacrifice on the ground. The sacrifice can be an animal, but the ritual is often performed using death magic. After weaving the final thread, the spellcaster stands in the bone circle, raises their arms, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If suc- cessful, a horde of Namegiver corpses within the area rise from their resting places as undead. There must be at least 25 Namegiver corpses in the area-every full 25 corpses are a group of undead and this spell raises one group, but extra threads can raise more groups if the required corpses are available. The spellcaster can mentally command their undead hordes within line of sight-this requires their concentration as a Standard action. If left uncontrolled, undead hordes act on their most recent instructions. They must remain within the spell's area, which moves with the spellcaster, and become inert if they leave it, regaining animation if the spellcaster moves to include them. Undead hordes can be armed with any discarded weapons or just their fists-the details are cosmetic and the results are the same. This is also true for armor. If an undead horde member is killed-again-it crum- ples to the ground, probably horribly dismembered and cannot be raised again. Undead hordes use the statistics listed below; they're the same for all Namegivers except windlings, which cannot fly when animated and aren't threatening enough to be taken seriously. If an undead horde suffers a Wound, one of the undead within is destroyed. Regardless of the number of members in the horde, they act as a group and receive only the listed ac- tions. They suffer Wound penalties as normal, reflecting the horde's losses. Area spells can cause additional Wounds for each full multiple of the Wound Threshold inflicted on the undead hordes. Abilities that inflict a penalty on a single target (e.g. Mind Dagger's Physical Defense penalty) or abilities that provide a bonus against one opponent (e.g. Maneuver, Pack Tactics) cost an additional success against the undead horde. If the penalty is a 'rider' on the damage (e.g. Earth Darts) and no extra success on the test, it inflicts damage but not the penalty. The undead hordes are considered a single target for abilities with a variable difficulty based on the number of targets (e.g., Battle Bellow). A knocked down horde member automatically stands up and has no penalty to their action(s). The spellcaster can only have one active instance of this spell at a time. Undead Horde DEX: 6 Initiative: NA Wounds: 21 STR: 6 Physical Defense: 8 Wound Threshold: 9 TOU: 4 Mystic Defense: 6 PER: 4 Social Defense: 11 Knockdown: 8 WIL: 4 Physical Armor: 3 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 3 Mystic Armor: 3 Move: 12 Actions: 3; Attack: 26 (26) Powers: Immune to Fear Mob Mentality: The undead horde cannot be Harried or Blindsided from positioning. Abilities still cause these as usual. Resist Pain (8) Special Maneuvers: Sweeping Attack (Opponent, Close Combat): The attacker may spend two additional successes on an Attack test to treat the attack as an area attack; they must be adjacent to at least two undead horde members to use this special maneuver. This special maneuver only costs one additional success if the attacker is using a two-handed weapon.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Effect (+1 group), Increase Area (+100 yards)

Circle_14.Name: Shadow Palace
Circle_14.Effect: Creates a palace in an astral pocket
Circle_14.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell creates a doorway into astral space housing a palatial dwelling. The spellcaster slowly constructs a doorway in the air as each thread is woven. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 30 minutes. When the final thread is woven, the spellcaster knocks at where the door would be, asks 'Hello?' and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If suc- cessful, the door opens, and a dry voice whispers, 'Welcome home, master,' revealing a beautiful palace to them and their guests. Entering the palace without the spellcaster's permission requires a successful Willpower test against the spellcaster's Nethermancer Circle. The shadow palace is an astral pocket furnished and appointed as the spellcaster desires. Characters in the palace are free from the constraints of the physical space they just left. The palace is safe and clean-as safe and clean as a palace full of dangerous adepts can be. It's the ideal place to rest and recuperate. When the duration ends, everyone inside the palace reap- pears at the doorway's location.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_15.Name: Annihilate Pattern
Circle_15.Description: Ritual. This spell extinguishes the target's pattern. The spellcaster must possess and have a thread woven to one of the target's pattern items to cast this spell. The spellcaster cuts themselves and allows their blood to drip on the pattern item as they weave the threads. Each thread takes one hour to weave. They must take at least 10 damage which cannot be reduced by any means but may take more at their discretion. The spellcaster uses this blood to draw their personal glyph and a glyph they use to represent the target, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If the spellcaster is using a minor pattern item, this requires two additional successes; a major pattern item requires an additional success. If they know the target's personal glyph (not everyone has one), it reduces the number of additional successes required by one, or provides an additional success if the spellcaster is using a core pattern item. If successful, the target suffers a Wound each round and the Effect test determines how much damage is in- flicted each round-this can also inflict a Wound. The Effect Step gains a bo- nus equal to the damage the spellcaster inflicted on themselves while weav- ing threads. If the target's Current Damage meets or exceeds their Death Rating, they cannot be healed by any means, nor can they be brought back to life. They are beyond Death's domain and their pattern is extinguished. There is no return from this spell. This is particularly horrific to watch as the target's pattern is rent asun- der. They will probably never forget the screaming. The knowledge this spell exists at all is a closely held secret and is more of a rumor. The rare rumors with any additional information indicate it's possible each spell is unique for the intended target. If anyone in Barsaive has knowledge of its existence, it's certainly the Denairastas family of Iopos. This spell can only target Namegivers and it's unknown if it affects dragons.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2), Increase Effect (2), Increase Range (200 miles)

Circle_1.Name: Astral Sense
Circle_1.Description: This spell enhances the use of the Astral Sight talent. The magician rolls his eyes  upward and makes a Spellcasting test against his Mystic Defense. If successful, he is  able to use the Astral Sight talent without taking Strain, and is able to sense magical  presences that are not otherwise in his line of sight (for example, behind him, or  otherwise hidden). Characters who do not have the Astral Sight talent may default to  Perception for Astral Sight tests. A magician will often take a few moments after casting  the spell to sweep the area and verify that an astral presence is not coming from a  comrade. A magician may attempt to cast spells on a target that he has astrally sensed,  even if he cannot otherwise see the target.  Magicians use this spell for many different reasons. It is often cast to make extended  astral examinations less taxing. As with normal uses of Astral Sight, a magician  using Astral Sense to view a thread item can sense the item’s true pattern and would  learn it was magical in nature. However, he would not gain any information about the  item’s history or learn any Key Knowledge.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Bedazzling Display of Logical Analysis
Circle_1.Description: This spell reinforces the integrity of arguments or debates. The magician draws  himself up to his full height and makes a Spellcasting test against his Mystic Defense.  If successful, he gains a +4 bonus to any Charisma-based tests involving conversation,  debate, argument, or other verbal forms of oratory.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Increase Effect (+2 bonus)

Circle_1.Name: Catseyes
Circle_1.Description: This spell grants the ability to see in near-darkness. The magician blinks several  times, then touches the target lightly on his eyelids and makes a Spellcasting test  against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, the target gains the Low-Light Vision  ability (p. 46)—his pupils become vertical slits, like those of a cat, reflecting incoming  light and appearing to glow in the dark. The spell has no effect on targets with Low-  Light Vision.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 minutes)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 minutes), Additional Targets (+Rank)

Circle_1.Name: Divine Aura
Circle_1.Effect: Glean information about a target’s state of being.
Circle_1.Description: This spell detects aspects of the aura of living beings. The magician peers intently  at the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If  successful, he chooses what he wishes to divine from the target’s aura—this could be  anything from an emotional state, like anger or fear, to hurt or fatigue. The magician  may determine one aspect for each success.   Success Levels: See text   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 Success), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Flame Flash
Circle_1.Description: Fire. This spell fires a burst of blue arcane flame at an opponent. The magician  makes wild, fanning gestures and crackling, whooshing sounds, then extends his  hands outward and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If  successful, flames flash from his fingertips, bathing the target in magical fire. The magician’s  Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted each round.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step),  Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Iron Hand
Circle_1.Effect: +3 bonus to close combat Damage tests
Circle_1.Description: This spell enhances a character’s ability to inflict damage. The magician mimics  striking someone, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense.  If successful, the target’s skin turns matte grey and gains a +3 bonus to all close combat  Damage tests he makes during the spell’s duration.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+2 bonus), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional  Target (+Rank)

Circle_1.Name: Mage Armor
Circle_1.Description: This spell creates a ghostly violet layer above the target. The magician punches at  the target, uttering a soft “thudding” sound, as if an ineffectual blow had landed, and  makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, add +3 to  the target’s Physical Armor for the spell’s duration. Targets that have been enchanted  with this spell have a faint violet glow that is noticeable in the dark or with astral  sight.   Success Levels: Increase Duration (+2 rounds)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (minutes), Increase Effect (+2 Physical Armor),  Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_1.Name: Mind Dagger
Circle_1.Effect: WIL+2/Mystic and a -2 penalty to Physical Defense
Circle_1.Description: This spell attacks an opponent by sheer force of will, staggering them. The magician  whips his arm forward as if throwing a dagger and makes a Spellcasting test  against the target’s Mystic Defense. If successful, his Effect test determines how much  damage is inflicted once. Additionally, the target suffers a -2 penalty to Physical Defense  until the end of the next round.   Success Levels: Increase Effect (+2 Effect Step)   Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+2 rounds), Increase Effect (-2 penalty to  Physical Defense), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_1.Name: Quicken Pace
Circle_1.Description: This spell increases walking speed. The magician walks briskly in a circle around  the target, then touches him and makes a Spellcasting test against the target’s Mystic  Defense. If successful, the target increases his Movement Rate by +2 yards.
