After making a Damage test, but before applying additional effects (e.g., Knockdown), the target makes another Attack test at the same Step previously used (the target may defend as normal). If successful, the spellcaster's Effect test determines how much Mystic damage the target suffers (additional successes from the echo blow Attack test add 2 to the Effect Step per additional success). The spell ends once the target makes an echo blow attack, whether it was successful or not.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Peacebond
Circle_8.Description: This spell enforces peace. The spellcaster touches the target and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If the target is willing, the Difficulty Number is 6. If successful, the target is peace-bonded for the spell's duration. Whenever a successful attack damages the target, compare Peacebond's Effect Step against the attacker's Mystic Defense. If successful, the attacker takes the same damage inflicted on the target (no armor reduc- es). However, this works both ways. If the target attacks someone, the target suffers the same damage inflicted from their attack.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Ricochet Attack
Circle_8.Description: This spell creates a ball of energy filled with glowing, glass-like shards which ricochets to hit multiple different targets. The spellcaster conjures a glowing shard they gingerly push into the shape of a ball with both hands and make a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense of the tar- gets. If successful, the Effect test determines how much Physical damage the target suffers. The initial spell has 3 shards which must have different targets. The Spellcasting result is compared against each affected target individually. After dealing damage to the target, if there was an additional success, the shard ricochets to a different target and the Spellcasting result is reduced by 5. This continues until a shard affects a target and has no additional success- es, or there are no targets within range. A target can be affected by multiple shards, but cannot be affected by the same shard consecutively. All targets must be within range of the caster.  <Jaan casts Ricochet Attack with no extra threads. This gives them three shards, which requires three different targets. Their Spellcasting re- sult is 23 against their highest Mystic Defense of 19. The spell is successful and they resolve damage (spending Strain and Karma for Willforce as usual). Now, they check for additional successes against Mystic Defenses of 19, 14, and 12; the number of additional successes doesn't matter. The shards ricochet off two targets, but not the third. Jaan has one shard ricochet from one target to a new target, and the other shard ricochet to one of the initial three targets, but not the one it came from. The Spellcasting result is now 18 against these two targets with Mystic Defense 14 (already hit once) and 12. Jaan resolves damage again (spending Strain and Karma for Willforceagain), then checks for additional successes. One shard ricochets again and the only viable target in range was hit in the first round. The Spellcasting result is now 13 against Mystic Defense 12. The damage resolves (spending Strain and Karma for Willforce yet again), then checks for additional suc- cesses, of which there are none. In all, Jaan considers this very suc- cessful, with six different attacks>  Success Levels: Additional Target (see text)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1 initial shard)

Circle_9.Name: Channel Raw Magic
Circle_9.Effect: Channels astral energy through a target
Circle_9.Description: This spell damages a character by channeling raw astral energy through their astral pattern. The spellcaster wrinkles their brow in concentration and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If success- ful, the target suffers raw magic damage each round for the duration. The Damage Step is based on the area's astral corruption (Player's Guide, p. 261) as though the target cast a spell using raw magic of Circle equal to the spell- caster's Wizard Circle. Only natural Mystic Armor provides protection.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 Cir- cle), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Counterspell
Circle_9.Description: This spell allows the spellcaster to use Dispel Magic more efficiently in their vicinity. The spellcaster holds up two fingers and traces a triangle within a circle on the ground around them, fills the space between with glyphs, then makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the glowing circle expands like a wave around the spellcaster, filling the air briefly with the glyphs before disappearing. The area moves with the spellcaster. The spell- caster may use Dispel Magic as a Free action for an additional Strain once per round against any spell that originates from or has a target within the area. This can only affect spells and can affect spells with an instant or 1 round duration as they are cast, including damaging effects.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 at- tempt per round), Increase Area (2-yard radius)

Circle_9.Name: Glowing Swarm
Circle_9.Description: This spell conjures a swarm of energy in the form of dangerous, glow- ing insects - similar to fireflies - which can be directed to attack a target character. The spellcaster makes buzzing noises and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a glowing swarm of insects appears. Controlling the swarm requires concentration. If the spellcaster doesn't command the swarm, it simply hovers 5 yards in the air and waits. The swarm may be directed to attack any target in range and directing the swarm to attack is a Standard action using Spellcasting as the Attack test against the target's Physical Defense. This can be done on the same round when the spell is suc- cessfully cast. The target is Harried until the spellcaster stops concentrating or the swarm is directed to a new target. If the attack is successful, the Effect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted. Additional success- es on the Attack test give 2 to the Effect Step. If the target moves outside the range, the swarm behaves as though it doesn't have a command.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect Step (2 to Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards) Immovable Object [Animate] Threads: 0 Weaving: 13/18 Casting: 10 Range: 10 yards Duration: Rank rounds Effect: Wizard Circle20 This spell renders almost all efforts to move a target against their wish- es useless. The spellcaster reaches out to the willing target with one hand and slams their other hand to the ground, gripping the ground, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target takes on a dull, metallic cast and any attempt to move or knock them down is resisted - the Difficulty or originating action result (as appropriate) is compared against the Effect Step. This applies to actions which throw or move the target (e.g. Horn Sweep), or are intended to knock them down (e.g. Attack to Knockdown). This doesn't apply to Knockdown tests resulting from suffering a Wound. Any increases to the spell's Dispel Difficulty increase the Effect Step by the same amount. If the resistance is successful (the Effect Step is greater), the attempt to move the affected target fails. If the resistance fails (the Difficulty is equal to or less than), the affected target is moved and the effect on them ends. Clearly, they weren't immovable after all. This spell may be stored in the same spell matrix as Immovable Object [Inanimate].  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2), In- crease Range (+10 yards), Additional Targets (+Rank) Immovable Object [Inanimate] Threads: 0 Weaving: 13/18 Casting: 10 Range: 10 yards Duration: Rank minutes Effect: Wizard Circle20 This spell renders almost all efforts to move an inanimate target useless. The spellcaster reaches out to the target with one hand and slams their oth- er hand to the ground, gripping the ground, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target takes on a dull, metallic cast and any attempt to move or knock them down is resisted - the Difficulty or originating action result (as appropriate) is compared against the Effect Step. This may not be cast on a target currently controlled by someone else (e.g. holding a weap- on). The affected object can still be destroyed, just not moved - an affected door cannot be opened normally, but can still be destroyed, leaving an open- ing. Any increases to the spell's Dispel Difficulty increase the Effect Step by the same amount. If the resistance fails (the Effect Step is equal to or less than), the affect- ed target is moved and the effect on them ends. Clearly, it wasn't immovable after all. This spell may be stored in the same spell matrix as Immovable Object [Animate].  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Targets (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Wipe Matrices
Circle_9.Effect: Removes spells from the target's matrices
Circle_9.Description: This spell disrupts the target's spell matrices, erasing the spells con- tained within. The spellcaster wipes the air in a clockwise circle with their right hand, a clockwise circle with their left hand, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful one spell matrix is emptied at random. If the target was astrally sensed, the spell (if any) in the matrix is revealed first and the spellcaster may force a different random spell matrix to be affected instead (the spellcaster doesn't know the spell in the new matrix). If the spellcaster can affect all the target's matrices, just do that. If a spell in the affected matrix is currently in the process of being cast, the target can choose to abort the spell or complete it using raw magic. This spell can target any type of spell matrix - Standard, Enhanced, Armor, or Shared - or matrix object. The spell matrix emptied by this spell may be re- attuned to hold a new spell or the spell it previously contained as normal.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (+1 spell matrix affected)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 spell matrix affected), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_9.Name: Wound Mask
Circle_9.Effect: Creates a mask to absorb damage
Circle_9.Description: This spell conjures a mask that provides protection by drawing attacks made against the willing target onto itself. The spellcaster pushes and prods their face, as if fitting something onto it, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a thin copper mask completely covers the target's face. Al- though there are no eye, nose, or mouth holes in the mask, the target sees, breathes, and speaks normally. They gain a +5 bonus to Physical Defense. Any Attack test against the target's Physical Defense that fails to hit the target, hits the mask instead. Successfully using an active defense ability (e.g. Avoid Blow and Riposte) against the attack prevents it from hitting the mask. The mask has Physical Armor 10 and if the current damage reaches or exceeds 25, the mask immediately shatters, ending the bonus to Physical Defense. However, all damage from the attack is still absorbed by the mask. If the mask's current damage is less than 25 at the end of a round, it heals all damage.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Physical Defense), Increase Effect (2 to the mask's Physical Armor)

Circle_10.Name: Absorbing Sphere
Circle_10.Description: This spell conjures a tiny red sphere eccentrically orbiting a character in a protective pattern. The spellcaster waves their finger in a circle around their head and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the orb is con- jured around the target. If an Attack test against the target is less than the sphere's Effect Step, the attack hits the orb instead - even if it would miss the target. The attacker makes a Damage test as normal, but the sphere ab- sorbs all the damage. After the sphere is struck, the Effect Step is compared against the attacker's Mystic Defense. If this is successful, the Damage from the attack absorbed by the sphere is fired back at the attacker in a glowing ray; Mystic Armor protects.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_10.Name: Block Magic
Circle_10.Effect: Enhances Astral Interference to affect all magic
Circle_10.Description: This spell enhances the Astral Interference talent to severely constrict the flow of astral energy into an area. The spellcaster adopts a defensive posture - holding out, crossing their arms, or putting more weight on their back foot-then makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster immediately uses Astral Interference as a Free action. The penalties from Astral Interference apply to all magic-based Action tests - including tal- ents, spells, and magic items - made in the affected area. The penalty only applies once regardless of the number of talents used in a test. This doesn't give Astral Interference the ability to affect creature, spirit, or Horror pow- ers unless the spellcaster uses another ability to do so.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Astral Interference)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Astral Interference)

Circle_10.Name: Draw and Quarter
Circle_10.Description: This spell severely damages a restrained target with circlets of energy. As the spellcaster binds the target's limbs, a glowing black and silver circle of astral energy forms around each limb. The spellcaster claps sharply and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the circles pull away from the target, tearing at their limbs. The Effect test determines how much damage is inflicted, which can't be reduced or redi- rected by any means. If the target suffers any damage, they take an addi- tional 4 Wounds. This spell is used as a means of execution in some parts of Barsaive.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Effect (+1 Wound)

Circle_10.Name: Spell Fusion
Circle_10.Description: This spell allows the spellcaster to use their Hold Thread talent to cap- ture and fuse together spells cast by other spellcasters into a single spell they can recast. The spellcaster forms their personal glyph with both hands and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a sphere of astral energy covered in mystic runes and their personal glyph forms and hovers next to the spellcaster. Whenever another spellcaster casts a spell within range, the spellcaster can attempt to capture the spell as a Free action by making a Hold Thread test against the target's Spellcasting test. If successful, the sphere captures and holds the target spell's energy. The spellcaster must concentrate as a Standard action each round to maintain this spell. If their concentration is broken, this spell ends. The spellcaster may choose to end this spell early. When the spell ends, the spellcaster may focus all the spells captured within the sphere at a single target within range by making a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. This requires two successes to be successful. If successful, the target suffers the effects of each captured spell as if they were cast on them at the same time. Alternatively, the spellcaster may dispel all the captured spells when the spell ends with a successful Dis- pel Magic (12) test as a Free action. If this fails, they must be cast at a valid target within range, even if that target is the spellcaster. This cannot target random, inanimate objects unless that's the only valid target for the spell.  Success Levels: Increase Range (+10 yards)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Talent Shredder
Circle_10.Effect: Removes the ability to use a talent
Circle_10.Description: This spell suppresses an adept's ability to use their talents. The spell- caster claws at themselves with both hands and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, a coil of inky darkness shoots from their hands to the target. The spellcaster chooses one of the tar- get's talents - this doesn't give the spellcaster an inventory of the target's talents, they either guess or are familiar with the target's talents - and the target cannot use the talent. Talents with a permanent effect are unaffected.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 tal- ent), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_11.Name: Fragile Pattern
Circle_11.Description: Ritual. This spell erodes the True Pattern of an item, such as a thread item. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spell- caster makes ripping, tearing gestures, then makes a Spellcasting test against the target item's Mystic Defense. If successful, the number of suc- cesses determines the effects (which aren't cumulative): 1) physically dam- ages the item and it must be repaired before it can properly function again; 2) damages the item's True Pattern and new threads can no longer be wo- ven, but existing threads are unaffected and can continue to be improved; 3) damages the item's pattern and any existing threads are destroyed; 4) com- pletely destroys the object and its True Pattern. If this is cast on a pattern item, it doesn't seem to directly affect the associated True Pattern. However, there are many stories of seemingly un- related catastrophic bad luck associated with spellcasters who 'protected' themselves by destroying their pattern items. It's taboo to engage in this be- havior and such spellcasters are rarely welcome or received training of any kind.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (see text)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Spellcasting test)

Circle_11.Name: Shatter Pattern
Circle_11.Description: Ritual. This powerful spell disrupts and damages a target's pattern. The spellcaster weaves the threads through the target's pattern and uses the higher of the weaving difficulty and the target's Mystic Defense as the Dif- ficulty. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spell- caster draws glyphs on the target as they weave threads, shatters a glass ob- ject, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If suc- cessful, the glyphs sear into the target's skin and the spellcaster spends the Shatter Points listed in the Effect to weaken the target's pattern; all points must be spent. Each point can be used to reduce one of the target's Attribute Values (note: this isn't the Step), Defenses, Mystic Armor, or Wound Thresh- old by 1. This cannot reduce anything below 1. The target also suffers total or near-total amnesia because their pattern was weakened to the point of collapse. A target cannot be affected by more than one casting of this spell at a time. Astral examination reveals something is terribly wrong with the target's pattern, but uncovering the cause and effects with Astral Sight requires significant study and multiple additional successes. Lifesight is much more effective at diagnosing the cause and effects of this spell. The target is also easier to affect with Alter Life enchanting, reducing the Difficulty by -2.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Effect (2)

Circle_11.Name: Strong Pattern
Circle_11.Effect: 2 to an item's Mystic Defense, Physical and Mystic Armor
Circle_11.Description: Ritual. This spell enhances the durability of an item's True Pattern. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster draws glyphs on the target as they weave threads and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the glyphs glow briefly and fade, remaining on the surface for the duration (though difficult to see). The item gains 2 Mystic Defense, Physical Armor, and Mystic Armor. All the bonuses are applied only to the item and not to the item's owner or user. The item's owner can ignore the increases to Mystic Defense as with anyone they give explicit permission to handle the item. For example, the increased Mystic Defense doesn't apply if the owner is using Item History on the item, or if giving it to another charac- ter to use it. The spellcaster can have up to their Wizard Circle active castings of this spell.  Success Levels: NA  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 year), Increase Effect (2)

Circle_12.Name: Forge Strawman
Circle_12.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell creates falsemen - animated statues used as servants. Prior to the ritual, the spellcaster must have the statue to be affected. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster makes a straw doll while weaving threads and makes a Spell- casting (10) test. If successful, the statue is animated, becoming a strawman (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 471) for the duration. The spellcaster and anyone the falseman is directed to follow can verbally command it, which requires concentration to provide instructions explicit and simple enough to follow correctly. The falseman isn't intelligent and follows instructions quite liter- ally - off the cuff commands can have disastrous and/or hilarious results. Some very poor debaters like to use them to enhance their arguments, but only the most gullible and those looking to have their biases confirmed are convinced by such acts. If left uncontrolled, the falseman acts on its most recent instructions. It isn't naturally aggressive and makes a poor defender, but can be commanded to do so. However, it cannot wield a weapon. A ward of this ritual can be created on the falseman to extend the dura- tion of the spell. This is an exception to the restriction that a ward must be placed on a location. However, the affected falseman is typically limited to a specific location to keep it out of trouble.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_12.Name: Quick Study
Circle_12.Effect: Reduces time to learn a skill
Circle_12.Description: Ritual. This spell reduces the time required to learn or improve a PER- based skill. The spellcaster prepares themselves, focusing on the skill and organizing their thoughts to absorb all the required knowledge while weav- ing the threads. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. Once all threads are woven, the spellcaster concentrates, visualizing the task and how to most efficiently learn the curriculum, and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the time required to learn or improve the Percep- tion-based skill is halved, rounded up to the nearest day. The final time re- quired must be within the duration of this spell - casting it again doesn't extend the duration. They must be dedicated to learning the skill and cannot learn or improve other skills, learn or improve talents, advance their Circle, learn new Disciplines or talents, etc. during this time. Otherwise, the sin- gle-minded dedication from this spell is lost and the typical time is required.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+7 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+7 days

Circle_13.Name: Hold Pattern
Circle_13.Effect: Holds a completed spell in a spell matrix
Circle_13.Description: Ritual. This spell holds a spell, complete with woven spell threads and extra threads, into a spell matrix, holding it until the spellcaster is ready to cast it. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster prepares a glyph to contain the spell with powdered quartz (50 silver) while weaving the threads, weaves the spell they wish to hold, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster makes a Hold Thread test against the spell's Dispel Difficulty. If successful, the crystal glyph shimmers and floats into the spellcaster, bonding with the spellcast- er's affected matrix. The spell is held for the duration. A spell can be held at any point during its casting, including after extra threads. For example, a spellcaster can weave all the threads for Razor Orb with all their allowed extra threads, leaving only the Spellcasting test unresolved and postponing making a Spellcasting test to release the held spell. The spellcaster doesn't have to hold all the required and declared threads, though can only use extra threads which were declared at the time of casting as per usual. Releasing the held spell is a Simple action; if the spellcaster can't take the required action to complete the spell, whether Spellcasting or an appropriate Thread Weaving talent, the spell fizzles. The held spell can't be higher Circle than the spellcaster's Hold Thread rank. The spellcaster takes Blood Magic Damage equal to the spell's Circle and the number of held threads (the thread in an Enhanced Matrix doesn't need to be held). The spellcaster may heal the damage once the spell is cast, the duration expired, or this spell ends. If the spell is removed from the spellcaster's matrix for any reason, this spell ends without the held spell being cast. This spell can be cast once per day and the spellcaster can only have one active casting at a time. This cannot affect ritual spells.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Hold Thread test)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (2 to Hold Thread test)

Circle_13.Name: Observe Event
Circle_13.Description: Ritual. This spell allows the spellcaster to use their Evidence Analysis talent to observe past events in the area, a dangerous undertaking. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster clear- ly states what event they want to observe, for example, 'I wish to see the last crime committed here,' or 'I wish to see what Dester Lynnpea last did here,' and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the spellcaster immediately uses their Evidence Analysis talent to play with the flow of time and attempt to observe past events. They make an Evidence Analysis test as usual, though use the modifiers in the event modifier table instead of those listed in the talent for time. The Difficulty they exceed determines how far in the past the spellcaster can po- tentially see. If successful at meeting the required time frame for the event, the spellcaster is granted a vision of the past, taking 1 Strain per round of observation. The spellcaster gets a clear sense of when the event occurred as they observe the scene and can view it from any angle, but this does not necessarily make the event they are watching transpire clearly. The event is replayed as it happened, round by round, until the spellcaster decides they've seen enough or can't handle the Strain, in which case the spell ends. The gamemaster may find it beneficial to use a timer to get an idea of how long the spellcaster is viewing the event. Not necessarily a 1-to-1 con- version, since descriptions take longer to pass along in words than when viewed. ***** Event Modifier Table Event Occurred Modifier Event Occurred Modifier Up to 1 Week Ago 0 Up to 10 Years Ago +12 Up to 1 Month Ago +1 Up to 50 Years Ago +15 Up to 2 Months Ago 2 Up to 100 Years Ago +18 Up to 6 Months Ago +3 Up to 200 Years Ago 21 Up to 1 Year Ago +6 Up to 500 Years Ago 24 Up to 5 Years Ago +9 Over 500 Years Ago Impossible ***** <Jess casts Observe Event in the course of investigating a mystery. She weaves the threads over the course of the day, including three extra threads for Increase Effect, and gets 23 as the Spellcasting result. This gives her +10 total on her Evidence Analysis test (+6 from extra threads and +4 from successes). She makes her Evidence Analysis test with a +10 bonus, get- ting 36 as the result. The base Difficulty Number was 15, meaning she exceed- ed the Difficulty by 11. Looking at the Event Modifier Table, this means she can see events up to 5 years ago. The event was within the last 2 months, so she's in good shape. She watches the event unfold and after six rounds and 6 Strain, she's seen enough to know what she needs to know.>  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Evidence Analysis test)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Evidence Analysis test)

Circle_14.Name: Astral Prison
Circle_14.Description: Ritual. This spell enchants an area to become an astral prison. The spellcaster creates a magical pattern in the area using sand and pulverized crystals (500 silver) while weaving threads. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 30 minutes. After weaving the threads, the spellcaster ex- claims 'Clang!' and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the pattern is consumed and becomes the magical pattern for the spell, leaving nothing remaining. The spellcaster may either set the spell to activate when the next entity enters the area (and provide some clarification as to what it affects) or trigger it as a Free action. They don't have to be present to trigger it and could include a separate alarm to notify them when something enters the area. The Effect Step is compared against the Mystic Defense of everything in the area. If successful, all affected things in the area are transported to an astral realm, leaving behind a small, ceramic egg with the spellcaster's personal glyph on it as the key to the prison. This spell may be cast once per day. Jess casts Observe Event in the course of investigating a mystery. She weaves the threads over the course of the day, including three extra threads for Increase Effect, and gets 23 as the Spellcasting result. This gives her +10 total on her Evidence Analysis test (+6 from extra threads and +4 from successes). She makes her Evidence Analysis test with a +10 bonus, get- ting 36 as the result. The base Difficulty Number was 15, meaning she exceed- ed the Difficulty by 11. Looking at the Event Modifier Table, this means she can see events up to 5 years ago. The event was within the last 2 months, so she's in good shape. She watches the event unfold and after six rounds and 6 Strain, she's seen enough to know what she needs to know. Time doesn't pass within this astral prison, though the perception of time theoretically still exists. Destroying the egg ends the spell and re- turns everything from the prison to the nearest safe area - throwing the egg into Death's Sea doesn't drop everything into Death's Sea, but somewhere relatively safe on the shore. If this spell isn't triggered within the duration, it ends. If used, the spell persists until the egg is broken. Dispelling it requires possessing the egg and is a more complicated way of accomplishing the same as destroying it, but you still have that egg.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (2), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_14.Name: Awaken Magic
Circle_14.Description: Ritual. This spell either enhances and awak- ens the willing target's magical potential or their pattern is destroyed. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The willing target must be present while the spellcaster weaves the threads. During this time, they ar- range crystals and construct elaborate casting patterns using expensive powders (1000 silver). Once complete, the spellcaster exhales gently onto the patterns and makes a Spellcasting (30) test. If successful, the magic of the ritual is gen- tly (as gently as possible) infused into the tar- get's pattern, wracking them with pain for the spell's duration (as possible). The end result is unknown until the end of the duration - either the target has the potential (and assumed drive, otherwise why are they doing this?) to become an adept or their pattern unravels from the strain; their death is both messy and final. The minimum duration is one day.  Success Levels: Decrease Duration (-2 days)  Extra Threads: Decrease Duration (-2 days)

Circle_15.Name: City in a Bottle
Circle_15.Description: Ritual. This legendary ritual spell allegedly allows the spellcaster to cap- ture a complete city and surrounding terrain, and place it in a bottle. This spell can only be utilized as a ward or Named spell, it simply isn't possible to harness the energies required without a more permanent pattern. This can also greatly extend the duration, which is valuable considering the time investment. Weaving each thread takes seven days and requires eight hours a day. The spellcaster can attempt to weave only one thread over a period of two lunar cycles (about eight weeks). A specially prepared colored glass bottle must be present throughout the ritual and the spellcaster slowly builds a model of the city within the bottle while weaving the threads. The spell- caster pulls at the city in the distance and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the designated area within range vanishes from the earth and is magically stored in the bottle. The previous site of the captured area be- comes an empty plain. The first thing those living in the captured area notice is the sky, which becomes a whirling mass of colors, changing as the light outside the bottle strikes it. The bottle forms impenetrable walls around the captured terrain; anything within the area is trapped in the bottle for the spell's duration. The same walls also protect those inside from external entities. The bottle is completely invulnerable while the spell is in effect - nothing can penetrate it, although those outside can see in with the right visual aids. When the spell ends, the missing terrain and inhabitants return to the former location regardless of where the physical bottle is located. According to persisted rumors, entire cities weathered the Scourge by using a Named version of this spell. They're said to exist in bottles kept in a powerfully warded vault, awaiting a hero to unlock the Key Knowledge nec- essary to restore them to the outside world. Each Named version is different in execution based on the particular needs and the most famous version was used on Parlainth to great tragedy.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_1.Name: Alter Tracks
Circle_1.Description: This spell alters tracks, causing different tracks to be left behind. The spellcaster stomps on the ground, brushes at the tracks, and makes a Spell- casting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster chooses what creature the tracks resemble-this cannot change the number of apparent individuals, nor can it mimic the tracks of dragons, spirits, Horrors, etc, and all must be the same creature. The tracks left by allies (including animals) in the area are those of the selected creature; the affected area travels with the spell- caster. This effect also fouls attempts to track those affected if they remain in the area, increasing the Difficulty Number by +4. These altered tracks re- main after the spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to Tracking Difficulty), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Animal Friendship
Circle_1.Description: This spell makes animals less wary of the target. The spellcaster adjusts their posture to appear smaller and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If suc- cessful, Unfriendly (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 142) animals have their attitude adjusted up to Neutral-this has no effect if the animal's attitude is above Neutral or they're already Hostile towards the target (e.g. sees the target as potential prey). The target cannot have ill-intentions or be hostile towards any affected animals or this spell is broken. If the spell breaks, all animals react aggressively toward the target, becoming Enemies until the next new moon.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Additional Targets (+Rank)

Circle_1.Name: Badger Mettle
Circle_1.Effect: 2 to Defenses against and tests to resist fear
Circle_1.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell improves a willing target's resistance to fear. The spellcaster plants their feet, growls and hisses, uses their hands to appear threatening (e.g. clenches and presents their fists), and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a badger spirit crawls out of the ground in front of the target and snarls menacingly, imbuing the target. They gain 2 to Defenses against fear effects and any tests to resist ongoing fear effects. The target may immediately make a resistance test if allowed, applying the result to all applicable fear effects.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to appropriate Defenses and tests)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to appropriate Defenses and tests), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_1.Name: Cetacean Breathing
Circle_1.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell allows a target to hold their breath for a long time. The spellcaster makes a low call, like a song, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the call echoes, is returned, and a majestic cetacean spirit swims gracefully through the target, who takes a deep breath in response. The target doesn't need to take another breath until the duration expires. If the target has an additional method to extend their breathing (e.g., Deep Diver, The Adept's Journey: Mystic Paths, p. 284), the effect of this spell is cu- mulative. If the target voluntarily takes a breath, such as to speak or cast certain spells, the spell immediately ends. Extra threads can be woven to allow the target to take breaths while the spell is active, allowing them to speak or use a suitable talent once for each extra thread. Note: the duration cannot be ef- fectively extended with multiple castings of this spell.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (+1 breath), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_1.Name: Hungry Like the Wolf
Circle_1.Description: Spirit. This spell grants the willing target enhanced senses for the pur- pose of tracking quarry. The spellcaster paws at the ground with their foot impatiently, sniffs at the air, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a wolf spirit trots into view and approaches the target, then stretches and yawns in front of them. The target must crouch and make eye contact with the spirit, which nods and licks the target's face before disappearing. This leaves the spellcaster's personal glyph glowing on the target's forehead for the duration and gives them 2 to Tracking tests. In addition, the Difficulty Number for the target isn't increased due to weather conditions. This spell must remain active for the duration the target is tracking their quarry and can be renewed without a new test as long as it remains in the spell matrix. The spellcaster may only have one instance of this spell active at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Tracking), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_1.Name: Pack Mule
Circle_1.Effect: Summon a spirit to carry things.
Circle_1.Description: Spirit. Summoning. The spell summons a tasked and surly equine spirit to carry things. The spellcaster spends 30 minutes fashioning an equine form with hair from a mane or tail attached, runs their hand over the figure, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, an equine spirit is sum- moned, manifesting before the spellcaster. The spirit travels at Movement Rate 14 and obeys all the spellcaster commands to 'halt' or 'follow,' but don't get cute, it's been a long day for the spirit already and these boxes still need to get to Paramus by nightfall tomorrow. The spellcaster must provide suitable containers (saddlebags, crates and rope, cart, and so on) to hold goods for the spirit to carry or pull. The spirit can move 100 pounds and has Physical and Mystic Armor 10. If it takes any damage, is further from the spellcaster than the spell's range, or used as a riding animal regard- less of the size of the rider (this includes win- dlings), the spell ends. The spellcaster can only have one casting of this spell at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 day), Increase Effect (+50 lbs), Increase Effect (2 Movement Rate), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_1.Name: Woodland Helpers
Circle_1.Description: Spirit. Summoning. This spell summons a combination of small animals and beast spirits from the area to help the spellcaster perform menial tasks. The spellcaster whistles or sings while dancing and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a swarm of small, (mostly) harmless animals native to the area and ap- propriate beast spirits arrive to help the spell- caster with their chores. This makes light of any work, increasing the rate unskilled labor is accomplished by *2. The spellcaster must help in the efforts and the summoned entities must remain within range at all times. They're useless in combat, even the threat of violence scatters them and immediately ends the spell. Despite their number, the helpers aren't the most efficient and prone to frolicking and playing. Some Shamans use this spell simply to gather company. This spell won't summon ar- thropods under any circumstance.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 min- utes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 min- utes), Increase Effect (+1 to multiplier), Increase Range (+10 yards)

Circle_2.Name: Bear's Sleep
Circle_2.Effect: Heals a Wound and 2 to a Recovery test
Circle_2.Description: Spirit. This spell places the willing or unconscious target into a deep sleep to improve healing. The spellcaster stretches exaggeratedly, scratches themselves, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test; if the target is unconscious, use their Mystic Defense (minimum 10). If successful, a bear spirit ambles up to the target, flops next to them, and the target falls onto the softly snor- ing bear into a deep slumber where they're totally unaware of the world around them. The bear spirit slowly dematerializes and the target gently falls to the ground if they aren't already laying down. After they awaken from their magical slumber, they heal a Wound and gain 2 to their first Re- covery test of the day. The target doesn't wake unless they succeed at a Willpower (10) test, take damage, or the spell ends. If the target wakes before the eight hour du- ration elapses (which may not be reduced by any means), the target gains no benefits from this spell. A target may only benefit from this spell once every seven days.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Recovery test)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Recovery test), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_2.Name: Bleeding Edge
Circle_2.Effect: Weapon gains +6 to Damage tests against the target
Circle_2.Description: This spell empowers a melee weapon (including natural weapon, e.g. claws) against a target who damaged the spellcaster within the past day. The spellcaster touches the weapon, scratches the injury, and makes a Spell- casting (6) test. If successful, the weapon bleeds from the striking surface, the blood dripping to the ground. The weapon gains +6 to Damage tests against the target.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Damage tests), Additional Target (+1 melee weapon)

Circle_2.Name: Hair Frenzy
Circle_2.Description: This spell causes the target's hair to fly wildly around in a distracting manner. The spellcaster grabs their hair and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target's hair whips back and forth, twisting into knots, etc., and is Harried. The target must pos- sess significant hair or fur to be affected. For example, t'skrang are unaffect- ed unless they're wearing a wig for some reason.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Invoke Turtle Snap
Circle_2.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a strange turtle spirit with heavy armor plates and a foul disposition. The spellcaster glares at the target, hunches their shoulders while pulling their head back, claps their hands sharply, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the summoned spirit surfaces from the ground as if it were water, imbues the spellcaster with ghostly armor, all while glaring at the target, and strikes at the target by extending its neck. The Effect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted and the spellcaster gains 2 Physical Armor un- til the end of the next round.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Effect (2 Physical Armor), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Invoke Wolf Maim
Circle_2.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a wolf spirit with a hungry growl to maim a target. The spellcaster bares their teeth, snarls, and makes a Spellcasting test against the Mystic Defense of the target. If successful, the summoned spirit prowls out from behind the spellcaster and lopes after the target, bit- ing their leg to disable them. The Effect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted and the target's Movement Rate is halved until the end of the next round.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_2.Name: Porcupine Quills
Circle_2.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell causes clumps of quills to emerge from the will- ing target's skin, greatly dissuading opponents from grappling them. The spellcaster runs their hand up each arm, elicits a sharp, pained sound when reaching their elbow, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a por- cupine spirit moseys to the target and displays their quills. The target must touch the black and white quills, which detach and quickly work their way through the target's body, emerging from their skin in patches. Any attempt to grapple the target requires an additional success and the opponent auto- matically suffers Step 8 damage. Armor doesn't protect against this damage, and only applies to the initial grapple attempt. The target gains no benefits if they grapple an opponent.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_2.Name: Tiger Roar
Circle_2.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell alters the timbre of the target's voice, lowering the pitch and increasing the intensity. The spellcaster lowers their head slightly and stares intensely, utters a low growl, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a tiger spirit roars, briefly stunning those around, and leaps into the target, causing them to roar with a similar fear-inducing qual- ity. The target gains 2 to Battle Bellow, Battle Shout, and Howl tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to affected tests), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_3.Name: Autumnal Bear
Circle_3.Effect: +4 to Wound Balance tests and reduce knock back
Circle_3.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell summons a happily rotund bear spirit to improve the willing target's stability. The spellcaster makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a bear spirit snuffles in from eating some tasty food in prepa- ration for the coming winter and plops down decisively on the target with a big yawn. The target gains +4 to Wound Balance tests and tests to resist being moved (this doesn't include active defenses, such as Steel Thought). Any distance the target is unwillingly moved is halved, rounded down. This excludes distance forcefully moved due to gravity.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Wound Balance)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Wound Balance), Additional Targets (+Rank)

Circle_3.Name: Creeping Viper
Circle_3.Description: Spirit. Summoning. This spell summons a small viper spirit to attack an opponent. The spellcaster sings a crooning song while making slow, sinu- ous movements with both hands and their body, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a viper spirit slides out from their clothing and the spellcaster indicates the target. The spirit sneaks up to the victim at Move- ment Rate 4 (climbing) using the Effect Step for Stealthy Stride tests. Once in range, the viper spirit attacks the target once each round using the Effect Step to determine Initiative if required. The viper spirit uses the Effect Step for Attack tests of its bites and has Damage Step 3. If a bite inflicts damage, the target is affected by its damaging poison Step 8 [Onset: Instant, Interval: 3/1 round]. Once the viper spirit affects the target with its poison, whether the victim resists or not, the spell ends and the spirit disappears. The viper spirit has Physical and Mystic Defense 10 and the spell immediately ends if it suffers any damage.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step) Crow's Affinity Threads: 2 Weaving: 7/12 Casting: 6 Range: Touch Duration: Rank minutes Effect: 2 to Mystic Pursuit tests Spirit. This spell grants the willing target a greater affinity for the con- nection between an item and their quarry. The spellcaster flashes something shiny between their fingers, catching the light with it, whistles sharply, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a crow spirit lands on the target's head and pecks them between the eyes, leaving the spellcaster's personal glyph glowing on the target's forehead for the duration and gives them 2 to Mystic Pursuit tests. The range for Mystic Pursuit is doubled. This spell must be maintained for the duration the target is following the connection and can be renewed without a new test as long as it remains in the spell ma- trix. The spellcaster may only have one instance of this spell active at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Mystic Pursuit), Increase Effect (+1* Mystic Pursuit range), Additional Tar- gets (+rank) Eagle's Bearing Threads: 2 Weaving: 7/12 Casting: 6 Range: Touch Duration: Rank +10 minutes Effect: +3 to Interaction tests Pack. Spirit. This spell summons a raptor spirit, causing a willing char- acter to adopt a more noble bearing and appear as a higher social status than they actually are. The spellcaster makes a haughty expression, slowly softening to a smile, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, an eagle spirit swoops in and lands on the target's shoulder, surveying those around them before melding into the target. The target gains +3 to Interaction tests as long as they act as they're from a superior social station. The target must behave honorably, politely, and observe all appropriate social expectations for their assumed status.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Interaction tests), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_3.Name: Flash of Insight
Circle_3.Description: Pack. This spell improves the willing target's ability to use the Anticipate Blow talent. The spellcaster takes a handful of dust, blows it toward the tar- get(s), and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the willing target sees futures and patterns in the dust. These additional flashes of insight about their opponents and possibilities give them 2 to Anticipate Blow tests and an additional success on those tests.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Anticipate Blow tests), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_3.Name: Invigorate
Circle_3.Description: This spell improves the willing target's ability to heal damage. The spell- caster touches the willing target's forehead with their thumbs and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spellcaster's personal glyph is left behind on the target's forehead for the duration and they gain 2 to their Recovery tests and talent tests to heal their damage. This doesn't affect heal- ing (including Recovery tests) generated by external sources, such as heal- ing aids and spells. This spell can be cast on a given target once per day.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to healing tests), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_3.Name: Invoke Falcon Dive
Circle_3.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a falcon spirit to tear the target's face off. The spellcaster screams, slices at the target with the blade of their hand, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the summoned spirit screams into the physical world, passing through the spellcaster before striking the target's face with its tearing talons. The Ef- fect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted and the spirit imparts some of their agility on the spellcaster as they pass through, giving them 2 Physical Defense until the end of the next round.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (2 Physical Defense), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_3.Name: Wake with the Sun
Circle_3.Effect: Wakes a target at a specified time
Circle_3.Description: Pack. This spell is particularly popular with guards and anyone standing watch. It helps a target to sleep and wakes them at sunrise. The spellcaster speaks a command word to the target, imitates a call appropriate for sun- rise, touches a willing target, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If success- ful, the target falls into a restful slumber and wakes at sunrise. Noises and events can wake them as normal, though this doesn't end the spell's effects and they quickly fall back to sleep when they return to bed. Once the target rises in the morning, they may delay their first Recovery test upon waking by up to an hour as they prepare to face the day. Extra threads can reduce the time required for a full night's rest by 1 hour each, to a minimum of four hours. Other effects cannot reduce the min- imum time below four hours. The spellcaster can only have one active cast- ing of this spell at a time.  Success Levels: Additional Target (+1)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (-1 hour), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_4.Name: Awaken
Circle_4.Description: Pack. This spell awakens an unconscious or sleeping target. The spell- caster claps their hands together three times, lightly touches the target, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target immediately regains consciousness: If the target's Current Damage exceeds their Unconscious- ness Rating-but not their Death Rating-they may make a Recovery test; the target must have a Recovery Test available. If this doesn't reduce the target's Current Damage below their Unconsciousness Rating, they don't re- gain consciousness and must wait at least one hour to benefit from this spell again. If the target is unconscious due to a magical effect, compare the Spell- casting test result against the Dispel Difficulty or the Step of a cause for unconsciousness without a Dispel Difficulty (e.g. poison). If successful, the target immediately regains consciousness. This spell doesn't provide heal- ing to targets whose Current Damage doesn't exceed their Unconsciousness Rating, nor anyone who deliberately takes damage to exceed that threshold for the purpose of receiving healing from this spell.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to the Recovery test)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to the Recovery test), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Bioluminescence
Circle_4.Description: This spell causes the target to glow brightly, making them easy to spot. The spellcaster runs both hands down their face, over their closed eyes, to their body, suddenly opens their eyes wide, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target emits a bright, watery glow. Non-magical stealth is impossible for the target; attempts at using magical stealth (e.g. Stealthy Stride, illusions, magical darkness, etc.) must succeed against this spell's Dispel Difficulty. If failed, they have no effect. If successful, this spell is suppressed for the duration of that ability. Attempts to find the target gain 2 to appropriate tests.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Dispel Difficulty)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Dispel Difficulty), Increase Effect (2 to find), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Invoke Hedgehog Nip
Circle_4.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a hedgehog spirit which protects the spell- caster and attacks a target. The spellcaster yawns, curls up, wrapping them- selves with their arms, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the summoned spirit trots to the spellcaster and quickly scales them, imbuing them with ghostly spines, and leaps from them at the target with tiny, ferocious teeth bared. The Effect test deter- mines how much Physical damage is inflicted. Anyone who successfully attacks the spellcaster in close combat takes 2 damage until the end of the next turn. This damage isn't reduced.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 damage to attackers)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Effect (2 damage to attackers), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_4.Name: Mark of the Crane
Circle_4.Effect: Grant bonuses to certain Charisma-based actions
Circle_4.Description: Binding. Pack. This spell summons a crane spirit to temporarily bind to a willing target. The spellcaster stands with perfect posture, their arms held gracefully by their sides, raises their chin, lowers their head respectfully, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test against a willing target. If successful, a crane spirit gracefully circles and lands before the target, walking into them with dignity. The target stretches fully, adopts a noble bearing, and gains the following benefit: +4 to Charisma-based Action tests to improve Atti- tude, request favors, gather support, and lead. The target must not perform any crass actions or otherwise act without refinement; this includes their speech. Everything they do must be with grace and dignity at all times. Fail- ure to do so ends this spell immediately. If this spell prematurely ends for any reason, the target suffers -2 to Ac- tion tests and Defenses as their confidence is crushed, until they receive a full 8 hours of sleep (this cannot be reduced). This spell may only have one active casting at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (+1 to as- sociated tests)

Circle_4.Name: Meerkat Estate
Circle_4.Description: Spirit. Summoning. This spell summons a meerkat spirit mob to act as a sentry for the area. The spellcaster sits in the approximate middle of the area, weaving threads, leaps to their feet in a crouch, popping their head up, darting their eyes from side to side, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the mob emerges from the ground within the area, manifesting before the spellcaster. The spellcaster tells the dominant female the area to watch-if it's smaller than the maximum area (e.g. a room or not beyond a stream)-whether to watch silently or raise an alarm if there's an intrud- er, and indicates anyone exempt from its scrutiny. The meerkat spirits use the Effect Step to make Awareness and Astral Sight tests to detect things within 12 yards of the area they're guarding. They automatically detect anything crossing the established perimeter if the Effect Step is greater than or equal to the intruder's Mystic De- fense. This includes things with a physical or astral presence. The meerkat spirits aren't bound to the loca- tion, but the spell relocates their burrow to the area for the duration. Extending the duration through an enchantment simply extends their stay in the area. If they're treated well and enjoy their time, the mob may decide to make it a permanent re- location. This doesn't extend the spell's effects and the result may not be as helpful as it sounds.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_4.Name: Octopus Snare
Circle_4.Description: Spirit. This spell allows the spellcaster to control gripping tentacles. The spellcaster wig- gles their arms then extends them, stretches their fingers out, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a small, ephemeral octopus swims through the air into the spellcaster , and long tentacles emerge from their arms. Each round as a Simple action, the spellcaster may attempt to grapple a target in the area by making an Effect test against the target's Physical Defense. If successful, a tentacle shoots from around their arm and wraps itself around the target, grappling them (Player's Guide, p. 230). The Effect Step is the Grappling Difficulty. The spellcaster can maintain grapples up to their total targets (extra thread for Additional Target), but can end a grapple at any time and attempt to grapple a different target. The tentacles may drag grappled targets toward them at Movement Rate 6. To resist, the target makes a successful Strength test as a Simple ac- tion against the Effect Step. The tentacles have Physical and Mystic Defense equal to the spellcaster's Shaman Circle+4 and any damage by an edged weapon (including claws) immediately severs them, other sources must cause at least 5 damage. However, the spellcaster simply grows a new tenta- cle if one is severed.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Effect (+50 lbs), Additional Target (+1 maintained grap- ple)

Circle_4.Name: Share Senses
Circle_4.Effect: Allows a character to perceive through another character
Circle_4.Description: This spell allows a willing character (the rider) to perceive what the willing target perceives. The spellcaster runs two fingers down the faces of the rider and target, over their eyes, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the rider can concentrate as a Standard action and perceive all the senses of the target if the target is within the area. They're limited to the target's perceptions, don't make independent tests to perceive anything, and cannot use abilities through the target. The rider doesn't perceive through their senses when perceiving through the target's senses. The target is aware when the rider is perceiving through their senses.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+10 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+10 minutes), Increase Area (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1 rider)

Circle_5.Name: Bear's Might
Circle_5.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell summons a bear spirit to infuse the target with its raw power. The spellcaster throws their arms wide, hands open, bellows, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a bear spirit lumbers onto the battlefield towards the target, bellowing at the opponents, stands up- right, and imbues the target. They gain 2 to close combat Damage tests. If the target is wielding a two-handed melee weapon, they gain an additional 2 to close combat Damage tests and the Bear's Might special maneuver: Bear's Might (Target, Close Combat): The target may spend additional successes on a close combat Attack test with a two-handed weapon to throw the target 2 yards per success. Treat this distance as falling (Gam- emaster's Guide, p. 168).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to close combat Damage), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_5.Name: Brightleaf
Circle_5.Description: This spell causes materials in the surroundings to glow gently. The spell- caster crouches, touches a natural element with both hands (e.g., rubbing leaves between their fingers), and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If success- ful, natural elements of the surroundings glow with a soft white light, such as leaves and stones. This eliminates darkness and shadows in the area. If darkness or shadows are caused by magic, this ends those effects if the Spellcasting result succeeds against their Dispel Difficulty.  Success Levels: Increase Area (2 yards)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Spellcasting test), Increase Range (+10 yards), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_5.Name: Darting Mongoose
Circle_5.Description: Spirit. This spell grants a willing target a burst of incredible speed. If the spellcaster declares this spell, the spellcaster may cast and resolve it before Initiative is determined for the round, or weave extra threads as nor- mal, taking advantage of this on the subsequent round once all threads are woven. If the spellcaster casts the spell, they still determine their Initiative as normal for the purpose of any effects requiring it and can only take Free actions for active defenses (e.g., Avoid Blow and Steel Thought) until the end of the round. The spellcaster crouches, makes a beckoning gesture, and makes a Spell- casting (10) test against a willing target. If successful, a mongoose spirit darts up the target's leg in a blur, filling the target with a burst of energy and giving them lightning reflexes. The target gains 2 to their Initiative test and +4 Movement Rate.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Initiative)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Initiative), Increase Effect (2 Move- ment Rate), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Hulking Champion
Circle_5.Description: Pack. This spell causes the willing target to grow significantly larger. The spellcaster puffs up their chest with arms stretched wide, bellows, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, the target immediately grows 50% larger than their normal size, gaining 2 to Strength and Toughness-re- lated tests (including Damage tests and healing Action tests from talents, but not Recovery tests from healing aids, resting, spells, etc.). Namegivers other than Windlings can reach farther, allowing them to make close combat attacks against opponents up to 4 yards away if there are no intervening ob- stacles, such as other characters; this includes allies. The target's clothing and armor also change size, returning to normal when the spell ends. However, weapons and other equipment don't change size other than to accommodate the target's new size. Care should be taken when using this spell in confined spaces.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to associated tests), Additional Targets (+rank) Lion's Pride Threads: 2 Weaving: 9/14 Casting: 6 Range: Touch Duration: Rank minutes (special) Effect: Improves the target's leadership Spirit. This spell makes the target a natural leader. The spellcaster adopts a formal posture with a straight back, gazes around them, surveying the animals, people, and area, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If success- ful, a lion spirit appears with a flowing mane and regal bearing, and roars at the target, giving them an aura of leadership. The target gains +6 to Animal Leadership and Leadership tests. All tests must be made within the spell's duration to gain the effect. For example, a successful Leadership test contin- ues to benefit from these effects after the duration expires.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2 to Leadership tests)

Circle_5.Name: Pelican Soar
Circle_5.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell allows a willing target to glide on air currents. The spellcaster spreads their arms, pantomimes soaring through the air, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, a pelican spirit soars in, lands in front of the willing target, and opens its mouth in offering. The target must reach in and remove the offering of glowing ephemera which suffuses through their body, leaving a glyph unique to the spellcaster on their fore- head. If the target falls from a height of at least 10 yards in an area with air currents, the glyph flares brightly and glowing white wings emerge from their back, allowing them to glide safely to the ground. The target has no control over their path (use the Scatter Diagram, Player's Guide, p. 394), but travels an equal number of yards horizontally in that direction as they fall vertically. Once begun, this effect does not end until the target lands. After being used, the spell ends on the target. Each target can be affected by this spell once per day.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_5.Name: Relax
Circle_5.Description: This spell relaxes the target and helps their natural healing process. The spellcaster performs a calming activity for the willing target, such as gen- tly singing, leading them in meditation, etc., and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target is overcome with a deep sense of relaxation and falls into a peaceful trance for up to the duration. They're aware of their surroundings and can end the trance at any time. The target gains +4 to Re- covery tests and Willpower tests to resist ongoing effects for the duration, and the amount of time required to make a Recovery test is reduced by 30 minutes (minimum 10 minutes). This reduction in time to make a Recovery test is not cumulative with reductions from other sources. The spellcaster may only have one active casting of this spell at a time.  Success Levels: Additional Target (+1)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to Recovery test), Increase Effect (-10 minutes for Recovery test), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_5.Name: Sparrow Raid
Circle_5.Effect: Harries and causes damage to insects
Circle_5.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a swarm of sparrow spirits to harass an op- ponent. The spellcaster throws their arms wide, spins, chirps loudly, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense of opponents in the area. If successful, a flock of sparrow spirits fly from the spellcaster's sleeves and engulf opponents. Opponents in the area are Harried and their Movement Rate is halved. If any opponents in the area are insects, insect spirits, or similar (black mantises, jehuthras, spider spirits, etc.), the tiny bird spirits peck and tear at them in a frenzy of chirps and aggression. Such opponents also suffer Dam- age Step 8 each round. Armor doesn't protect against this damage.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 Ef- fect Step), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Diminutive Warrior
Circle_6.Description: This spell reduces a willing target in size. The spellcaster flicks their fingers, reacts as though stung, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If suc- cessful, the willing target shrinks to one-half their size and one-eighth their mass. They gain +4 Physical Defense and tests to evade detection, and other tests which may benefit from their new, smaller size. However, they suffer -4 to Strength and Toughness-related tests (this doesn't include Damage or healing tests). The target's clothing and armor change size, returning to normal when the spell ends. Weapons are still wielded as normal and adjust to the wield- er's new size-though it may look ridiculous.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (2), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_6.Name: Invoke Rhinoceros Rush
Circle_6.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a rhinoceros spirit to toss a target. The spell- caster points at the target, stamps the ground, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the summoned spirit thun- ders at the target and slams into them. The Effect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted. Additional successes throw the target back with the force of the spell, this distance is treated as falling damage (Game- master's Guide, p. 168).  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 yards knock back)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2), Increase Effect (2 yards knock back), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_6.Name: Lifelight
Circle_6.Effect: Creates a globe of protective light
Circle_6.Description: This spell gathers the essence of life reflected by astral energy. The spell- caster closes their eyes, spreads their arms, arches their back, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a globe of warm light appears, hovering a yard above the spellcaster's head and illuminating the area. Undead or insect spirit attempting to enter the area makes a Willpower test against the Spellcasting result. If one is successful, the light goes out and the spell ends. Otherwise, the entity remains outside the area until the spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_6.Name: Mark of the Bison
Circle_6.Effect: Improves Damage, Movement Rate, and Wound Balance
Circle_6.Description: Binding. Pack. This spell summons a massive bison spirit to temporarily bind to a willing target. The spellcaster draws up their posture and inhales deeply to make themselves appear larger, paws at the ground, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, an enormous and enraged bison spirit with threatening horns and a prominent ridge on its spine charges into the willing target. The target seems to increase significantly in bulk and their eyes flash with rage. They gain the following benefits: 2 Movement Rate, 2 Wound Balance, +4 to close combat Damage tests-with an additional 2 if using a two-handed weapon-and attacks with a two-handed weapon knock the target back 2 yards, which is treated as falling damage (Gamemas- ter's Guide, p. 168). The target may not use a second weapon and must be within four yards of an ally either at the beginning or end of their turn each round. If this spell prematurely ends for any reason, the target's Movement Rate is halved and they're knocked down if they resist a knockdown effect- no effect allows them to resist-until they can spend at least an hour rest- ing. This spell may only have one active casting at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to close combat Damage), Increase Effect (2 Wound Balance)

Circle_6.Name: Personal Space
Circle_6.Effect: Enhances deception at the cost of
Circle_6.Description: This spell clears the area surrounding the spellcaster. The spellcaster throws their arm out, casting small objects such as pebbles or acorns, and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area. If successful, everyone in the area is thrown 2 yards away from the spellcaster and makes a Knockdown test against the Effect Step. If there's empty space where a target could land, they land there. However, if there is no empty space, the target lands on whatever is occupying the space and it must make a Knockdown test against the Effect Step as well if appropriate.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (2 yards), Increase Area (2 yards) Rat's Trickery Threads: 1 Weaving: 10/15 Casting: 10 Range: 10 yards Duration: Rank minutes Effect: Enhances deception at the cost of honesty Pack. Spirit. This spell enhances the target's deceptive abilities. The spellcaster wiggles their nose, rubs their hands together with a grin, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, an adorable rat spirit pokes its head out, looks around, disappears and reappears next to the target, scurries up their leg, and imbues the target. The target gains 2 to deceptive actions, including lying, hiding, concealing things, etc. However, the target suffers -2 to direct or honest actions, such as Attack tests, sincere interac- tions, casting combat spells, etc.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 min- utes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 min- utes), Increase Effect (2), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_6.Name: Shrunken Foe
Circle_6.Description: This spell reduces a target in size. It's often used to make prisoners easier to deal with. The spellcaster flicks their fingers dismissively and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target shrinks to one-half their size and one-eighth their mass. They suffer -4 to Strength and Tough- ness-related tests (including Damage tests, but not healing). The target's clothing and armor change size, returning to normal when the spell ends. Weapons are still wielded as normal and adjust to the wield- er's new size-though it may look ridiculous. This may provide the target with bonuses to evade detection at the gamemaster's discretion.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (-2), Ad- ditional Target (+1)

Circle_6.Name: Sworn Protector
Circle_6.Effect: Provides bonuses to two targets based on their role
Circle_6.Description: Pack. This spell connects the patterns of two willing characters, obligat- ing them to aid each other. One takes the role as the protagonist, the other as the protector. This spell must be cast on both targets using the Pack Leader- ship ability and requires two adjacent targets. The spellcaster places a hand over the heart of each target, they place a hand over the spellcaster's hands, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the patterns of both targets become briefly visible and align, with threads weaving between each. The targets must be adjacent to each other to receive the spell's benefits. The protagonist gains 2 to their Standard action each round and any resulting tests (e.g., Damage, Effect, etc.). They also gain an additional 2 to tests that target their protector and any resulting tests. The protector may use an appropriate active defense (e.g., Avoid Blow, Riposte, Steel Thought) against an ability targeting the protagonist. If suc- cessful, the protector becomes the target of the effect instead. This doesn't allow the protector to use an additional active defense against the ability. This can be used against area effects causing the protector to be affected twice. If the protector is in the Defensive Stance, they gain 2 to Defenses and Armor.
