Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (+1 to protagonist), Increase Effect (+1 to protector)

Circle_7.Name: Cat's Victory
Circle_7.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell summons a small cat spirit to show the target what winning looks like. The spellcaster smiles cruelly, inspects their nails, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a small, spotted cat spirit with black feet prowls and bounds to the target, then pounces into them so the target sees how to get down to business. The target gains +4 to close combat Attack and Damage tests when attacking a given opponent the first time each round. This bonus applies to each opponent the target attacks, but only the first attack in a round. Opponents damaged by this attack also suffer -4 to their Wound Threshold until the end of the next round-the tar- get benefits from this reduction with the inflicting attack.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1), In- crease Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+rank)

Circle_7.Name: Invoke Mink Snap
Circle_7.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a mink spirit that refuses to move in straight lines to attack a target. The spellcaster bounces from foot to foot, performs a throat-wringing motion followed by a bite, and makes a Spellcasting 2 test against the Mystic Defense of the target. The spellcaster can affect tar- gets within range through solid objects if they can be detected (e.g. with Lifesight). If successful, the summoned spirit pokes its head out and looks around, then dives and bounds through the area, disappearing and reap- pearing as it moves erratically, then leaps at the target from behind, biting at its neck (or the nearest approximation). The Effect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (2 to Spellcasting test), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Karmic Connection
Circle_7.Description: This spell taps into a character's Karma, using it to save their life. Be- cause of the high Karma Point cost to the target, the spell is generally only cast on those in imminent danger of dying. The spellcaster touches the tar- get's forehead and heart, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target immediately spends a Recovery Test and 5 Karma Points, and makes a Recovery test. The Karma Dice are not added to their Recovery test. If the target doesn't have all the required Karma Dice, the spell immediate- ly fails. The spellcaster may spend up to 5 Karma Points which add Karma Dice for the target to use on their Recovery test (use the spellcaster's Karma Step). If the target's Current Damage equals or exceeds their Death Rating, the spellcaster must cast the spell within Spellcasting rank minutes to have any effect.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2 Karma Points may be spent)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Karma Points may be spent), In- crease Effect (2 to the Recovery test)

Circle_7.Name: Mark of the Tarrack
Circle_7.Description: Binding. Pack. This spell summons a tarrack spirit to temporarily bind to a willing target. The spellcaster stamps and hops at the ground, spreads their arms, screams, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If successful, an ag- gressive and energetic tarrack spirit dashes into the willing target. The tar- get immediately begins hopping from foot to foot, spreads their arms wide, screams aggressively, and gains the following benefits: +4 Movement Rate, can split their movement (Player's Guide, p. 386) without penalty or spend- ing Strain to do so, and gains 2 ranks to the Great Leap talent (even if they don't have the talent). The target must move at least half their Movement Rate each round. Failure to do this ends the spell immediately. If this spell prematurely ends for any reason, the target's Movement Rate is halved and they are Harried until they can spend at least an hour resting. This spell may only have one active casting at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+4 Movement Rate)

Circle_7.Name: Soothing Atmosphere
Circle_7.Effect: Creates a soothing atmosphere for socializing
Circle_7.Description: This spell creates a soothing sensation within an enclosed space, one of calm and relaxation. The spellcaster performs an action associated with relaxation with ceremonial overtones, such as singing, playing music, shar- ing libations, etc., and makes a Spellcasting test against the highest Mystic Defense in the area (minimum 10). If successful, everyone in the area (in- cluding those who enter later) find themselves more agreeable and able to focus. They gain 2 to their Defenses against Action tests to deliberately re- duce their Attitude toward another target, or similar effects (including fear and intimidation), and 2 to Sustained Action tests that require socializing and wouldn't have negative effects, such as decreasing the target's Attitude, inflict fear or intimidation, etc. Violence ends the spell.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 to Defenses), Increase Effect (2 to affected Action tests), Increase Area (2 yards)

Circle_7.Name: They Grow Up so Fast
Circle_7.Description: This spell greatly increases the rate at which the tar- get animal matures. The spellcaster croons a melancholy lullaby and makes a Spellcasting test against the creature's Mystic Defense. If successful, the animal grows at a significantly greater rate, seeing a month of development per day. The spellcaster (and likely others) must tend to the affected animal during this time and ensure they have sufficient food, water, and attention-their needs increase proportionally. The animal's growth cannot be accelerated once they reach maturity and the effects of this spell end when it's reached. This spell cannot af- fect Namegivers and other sapient creatures-the rapid development is incompatible with the needs of their growing minds. Many Shamans only cast this spell under the most dire circumstances due to the stress it places on the animal. Each year of maturation through this spell (or fraction thereof) also reduces the animal's lifespan appropriately, burning the stick at both ends. This spell can only affect a given animal once per lunar cycle and the spellcaster can have one active instance of this spell at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_7.Name: Wolf Pack
Circle_7.Description: Spirit. Summoning. This spell summons two wolf spirits. The spellcaster crouches, prowls in a cir- cle, throws their head back, howls, and makes a Spell- casting (10) test. If successful, the wolves arrive in an eerie glow with creeping mist on the ground. The wolf spirits act as a single creature and the spellcaster controls them with a Standard action using Spirit Talk. They aren't proactive and don't attack, chase, etc., without instruc- tions to do so. If the wolves move beyond the range from the spellcaster, they act on their most recent instructions. Wolf spirits travel at Movement Rate 16 as a pack and must remain within 4 yards of each other at all times, wolves outside this area can do nothing except return to their pack. The wolves are nearly insubstantial. They move through open spaces around defenders unimpeded (essentially moving through them, but not literally through them). Wolf spirits within range of the spellcaster can be ordered to attack one target, quickly surrounding them (often inflicting Harried), by making a Spirit Talk test against the target's Physical Defense; each hound after the first attacking the target gives +1 to this test. If successful, the Effect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted. Each wolf spirit after the first who attacked the target gives +1 to the Effect test. Additional successes on the Spirit Talk test give 2 to the Effect test. Opponents damaged by the wolves have their Movement Rate halved until the end of the next round. Wolf spirits are difficult to injure, but the astral pattern containing the spell can be disrupted by attacks that target their Physical Defense (Shaman Circle+8) or Mystic Defense (Shaman Circle+4). If a single attack inflicts damage equal to or greater than the Effect Step, the wolf spirit is released and disappears.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 minutes)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 minutes), Increase Effect (+1 wolf spirit), Increase Effect (2 Movement Rate)

Circle_8.Name: Dampen Karma
Circle_8.Description: This spell reduces the potency of the target's Karma. The spellcaster holds up an open palm, closes it, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the target must spend one additional Karma Point for each Karma Point they would normally spend. This addi- tional Karma Point is a cost and provides no additional effect. For example, a Wizard using a Karma ability to change the range of a spell from Self to Touch must spend an additional Karma Point, and an Archer using True Shot must spend an additional Karma Point for each Karma Point they spend to gain a bonus to their Attack test.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1 addi- tional Karma Point cost), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Guiding Visions
Circle_8.Effect: Alters perceptions to answer a question
Circle_8.Description: Ritual. This spell alters the spellcaster's perception of reality to guide them to see meaning in the world around them and find the answer to a question. The spellcaster meditates on the question at dawn, spending 10 minutes to weave each thread, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If success- ful, how the world looks, sounds, feels, tastes, etc., subtly changes, providing nuance and additional meaning to help guide them to the answer they seek. This is a psychedelic and distracting experience, inflicting -1 to Action tests over the next seven days as the spellcaster's grip on what is real becomes loose. The surroundings provide hints to the spellcaster over the next day. Meaning comes from seeing natural elements, interacting with Namegivers, watching animals, etc. These moments stand out as being both too real and surreal-perhaps significant flowers appear to bloom out of season, or two foxels stop playing to speak directly to the spellcaster. Extra threads can al- low the spellcaster to ask a question that clarifies the intention of these mo- ments when they sleep that evening and are revisited by the visions. Each question asked increases the Action test penalty by -1. The spellcaster isn't obligated to ask all the questions they have available. The duration for the Action test penalty cannot be reduced below two days. This spell cannot be cast more than once per lunar month or the spell- caster becomes trapped between visions, uncertain of what is real.  Success Levels: Reduce Duration (-1 day)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 question), Reduce Duration (-1 day)

Circle_8.Name: Hunger Pangs
Circle_8.Description: This spell makes a character feel their hunger. The spellcaster grimaces, places both hands on their stomach, doubles over, and makes a Spellcast- ing test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spell induces sudden, overwhelming hunger pangs in the target. They suffer -2 to Action tests. The hunger makes the target irritable and easily distracted-they sin- gle-mindedly seek food and continually eat what food they find, yet they still remain hungry while the spell is active. If they haven't eaten at least a small meal or large snack within the past hour, they're also Harried. The target makes a Willpower (12) test at the beginning of each day. If successful, the spell effects on the target end. Despite the feeling, this spell has no effect on how much time before a character suffers from starvation (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 195).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days)

Circle_8.Name: Invoke Fox Skirmish
Circle_8.Description: Spirit. This spell summons a tricksy fox spirit to confound and amuse (by hurting things). The spellcaster hops from foot to foot, grins, and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the summoned spirit pokes its head out with a sly grin, bounds in the area, and bites the target. The Effect test determines how much Physical damage is inflicted. The spirit continues to harass and yip at the target, much to their extreme annoyance, causing the target to suffer -2 Physical Defense and be Blindsided against the next attack against their Physical Defense before the end of the next round. Once attacked, regardless of success, the spirit leaves and the target no longer suffers the Physical Defense penalty or is Blindsid- ed.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (-2 Physical Defense)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 Effect Step), Increase Effect (-2 Physical Defense), Increase Range (+10 yards), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_8.Name: Search the Stars
Circle_8.Effect: Accumulating penalties if sound advice isn't heeded
Circle_8.Description: Ritual. This spell answers questions about another Namegiver. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster brings out their preferred divining tool while underneath the night sky while weaving the threads. They whisper the target's Name, create a mental picture, and think of their questions while they perform their reading. Once the reading is complete, the spellcaster looks up at the stars and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, the spell- caster glimpses insight among the stars. The spellcaster may ask questions about the target, though these ques- tions are limited and may not receive direct answers. They receive one ques- tion per success, plus any from extra threads. The spellcaster may not ask more questions than their Shaman Circle. More specific questions receive more specific answers. Topics can include their location (relayed in relation to symbolic references to other landmarks, though the spellcaster should have some clues where to start, a description of their immediate surround- ings, etc.), emotional state, physical state, possible futures, events of their past, blood oaths sworn, etc. Most answers don't present the whole story, but should set the spellcaster on the path for finding those answers.  Success Levels: See text  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1 question) Star-Crossed Threads: 2 Weaving: 12/17 Casting: TMD Range: Touch Duration: 2 days Effect: Accumulating penalties if sound advice isn't heeded Fate. This spell provides a vision of calamity if the target engages in a particular course of action. The spellcaster uses their preferred tool to tell the fortune of the target (e.g., bones, carved sticks, stars, etc.) and makes a Spellcasting test against the target's Mystic Defense. If successful, they see disaster if the target follows a specific path. Each day the target pursues this course, they suffer a cumulative -1 to Action tests (up to Shaman Cir- cle), whether related to this disastrous foretelling or not. Additionally, more things seem to go wrong and obstacles fill their path. The spellcaster may cast this spell once per moon cycle (tracking from full moon to full moon).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days)

Circle_8.Name: Viper Fangs
Circle_8.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell allows a willing target to produce venom. The spellcaster moves sinuously, hisses, and makes a Spellcasting (6) test. If suc- cessful, a viper spirit slithers down the spellcaster's arm and leaps into the target, imbuing them with a colored glow appropriate to a poisonous snake. The target adds the poison effect to their attacks (this includes natural weapons) that successfully cause damage. This can affect all of the target's attacks using natural weapons which can pierce the skin (e.g. claws, but not a kick from a booted foot or strike with a tail) or one attack with a weapon. The damaging poison is Step Shaman Circle+4 [Onset: 1 round, Interval: 2/1 round].  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds/minutes), Increase Effect (+1 Step), Additional Target (+Rank)

Circle_9.Name: Claw Talisman
Circle_9.Description: This spell transforms the caster's hand into a vicious claw. The spell- caster must previously prepare a talisman in a one hour ritual where they infuse it with part of their pattern, then manipulate it with their knowledge of beast spirits to make it part of both. This can be a physical object unique to the spellcaster or physically tattooed on their hand (which isn't subtle and can be identified). The spellcaster clutches their talisman and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, their hand transforms into a claw with an appearance unique to the spellcaster. The spellcaster can make close combat attacks with it using their Spell- casting talent for the Attack test and the Effect test determines Physical damage. If the Attack test is successful, the target suffers -4 Wound Thresh- old until the end of the next round. The Attack test is a Simple action if the spellcaster used Shamanism to attempt weaving threads this round. Note: the spellcaster cannot make more than one Spellcasting test in a round. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time. This spell cannot be cast on (e.g. Fifth Circle Wizard Karma ability) or by an- other character (e.g. Loan Spell).  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Effect Step), Increase Effect (-4 to Wound Threshold)

Circle_9.Name: Cling to Life
Circle_9.Description: Spirit. This spell heals the target when they need it most. The spellcaster glowers at the world in general, bares their teeth, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target spends a Recovery Test and a wolverine spirit snarls at everything and merges with the target. The first time the target's Current Damage equals or exceeds their Death Rating, they roll the banked Recovery test with the Effect Step as a bonus. External benefits (e.g. booster potions or other spells) don't improve this test. If their Current Damage is below their Death Rating, they never died. If it's below their Un- consciousness Rating, they don't fall unconscious. However, they may still be knocked down due to a Wound. This spell lasts until sunrise and may only be cast once per day on a giv- en target. The spellcaster may cast this spell up to their Shaman Circle times per day.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2)

Circle_9.Name: Dietary Restriction
Circle_9.Description: Fate. This spell alters the dietary needs of a willing target. The spellcast- er chews a mouthful of vegetation or meat while staring at the target, indi- cating if the target will be an herbivore or obligate carnivore respectively, and makes a Spellcasting (15) test. If successful, the willing target's diges- tive tract subtly changes to reflect their new dietary restriction. Herbivores can only eat plant matter, nothing derived from animals (including eggs and dairy products). Obligate carnivores can only eat meat, nothing plant-based. If the target consumes anything proscribed, the spell immediately ends. The target gains 2 to one type of test once per round appropriate to an animal with their new diet. For example, carnivores could receive 2 to Awareness, close combat Attack, close combat Damage, Initiative, Tracking, etc. Herbivores could receive 2 to Awareness, Acrobatic Defense, a specific active defense (e.g., Avoid Blow or Resist Taunt), Danger Sense, etc. Ranged combat or activities inappropriate for animals (e.g., Forge Weapon, Spell- casting, Summon, etc.) cannot be improved. Increasing the effect allows the target to select another test to benefit once per round-the same benefit can be selected multiple times to use it more than once per round. If the spell ends prematurely for any reason, the target's body immedi- ately reverts to its original nature. This is pretty messy. The target is Harried from the cramping and deeply unpleasant feeling until they receive a full 8 hours of sleep (this cannot be reduced) and they don't regain their Recovery Tests as usual from this rest. The spellcaster can only attempt to cast this spell once per day and may have up to their Shaman Circle instances of this spell at a time.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (+1 test per round)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Effect (+1 test per round), Additional Target (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Improve Karma
Circle_9.Description: This spell improves a willing target's ability to use Karma. The spellcast- er holds their closed hand in front of them, opens it palm up, makes a Spell- casting (10) test, and spends 1 Karma Point per target-this is a cost and not added to the Spellcasting test. If successful, the target gains +1 Karma Step. If the target cannot use Karma, this spell has no effect.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (+1), Ad- ditional Target (+1)

Circle_9.Name: Mold the Skin
Circle_9.Duration: Rank hours Effect: Changes cosmetic features

Circle_9.Name: Songbird
Circle_9.Description: Spirit. This spell conjures a bird spirit to act as the spellcaster's spy. The spellcaster whistles a lively song and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If suc- cessful, they summon a bird spirit to use with their Orbiting Spy talent. The spellcaster can issue mental commands to the spirit, which it follows to the best of its ability. However, the spirit will only observe and not interact with the surroundings. The spellcaster can use the spirit's senses to see and hear as a Simple action, using their Orbiting Spy Step for any Perception tests. The servitor has a Movement Rate of 16 (flying) and has a Detection Difficul- ty of the spellcaster's Orbiting Spy Step. Any attacks cause the bird spirit to immediately flee. If it takes any damage, the spell immediately ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2 De- tection Difficulty), Increase Effect (2 Movement Rate), Increase Area (2 miles)

Circle_10.Name: Imbue Spirits
Circle_10.Description: This spell empowers spirits who willingly aid the spellcaster. This in- cludes free spirits who aid the spellcaster, summoned by the Summon talent or the summoning keyword, or with the spirit keyword. The spirit is willing if the spell has the spirit keyword. The spellcaster covers their fingers in water, draws their personal glyph on their forehead, flicks it into the air, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the water sparkles with an ethe- real light and hangs in the area. All spirits who come to their aid willingly gain +3 to Attack, Damage, and Defenses, and spells with the spirit keyword gain the benefit of an extra thread. This extra thread doesn't count toward the maximum number of extra threads. These benefits only apply while the spirit is within the area, which moves with the spellcaster. The spellcaster must be entered into a Spirit Pact (The Adept's Journey: Mystic Paths, p. 187) to cast this spell.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (2 to Attack, Damage, and Defenses), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_10.Name: Weather Change
Circle_10.Description: This spell creates localized changes in weather. The spellcaster softly calls out the names of the winds and rains, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, they can make incremental changes to the weather within the area. Each hour the spellcaster may do one of the following: • Increase or reduce the temperature by 5 °F. • Increase or reduce the wind speed by 5 mph. • Increase or decrease the chance of precipitation by 10%. If it's currently raining, the change may stop the rain. Each change takes one minute to effect. The gamemaster determines if precipitation changes afterward.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (+1 change per hour), Increase Area (2 miles)

Circle_10.Name: Wild Boar
Circle_10.Description: Pack. Spirit. This spell infuses a willing target with the fury of a boar spirit. The spellcaster claws at their skin with both hands while tossing their head and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a boar spirit bursts from the spellcaster, gores the target viciously while merging into them, and the willing target gains Fury 4 (Gamemaster's Guide, p. 251) and +4 Move- ment Rate. The Fury rank provided may not exceed the spellcaster's Shaman Circle.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 rounds)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 rounds), Increase Effect (Fury 2), Increase Effect (Movement Rate +4), Additional Targets (+rank)

Circle_10.Name: Wisent Vitality
Circle_10.Description: Spirit. This spell enhances the target's Unconsciousness and Death Rat- ings. The spellcaster draws their personal glyph on the target's forehead with ointment and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the spell- caster or target spends a Recovery Test, the ointment becomes like dried blood that cannot be removed, and the target increases their Unconscious- ness and Death Ratings by the Effect Step. This doesn't affect their Current Damage when this spell ends.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (2), Ad- ditional Targets (+Rank)

Circle_11.Name: Death Vow
Circle_11.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell enforces an agreement between the spellcaster and another willing character. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes 10 minutes. The spellcaster stipulates the terms of the contract, cuts their palm-taking 2 Blood Magic Damage-shakes hands with the target, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the terms of the contract take effect as stated. The contract can be outrageous, but both characters must abide by it. This spell enforces the spirit of the contract, not the let- ter-there is no manipulating the agreed terms. The spellcaster can reduce the duration in the contract's terms if desired. If the vow is broken, the oath-breaker immediately suffers their Death Rating in damage and 2 Wounds-in addition to any Current Damage they may have and any Wound(s) they may suffer as a result of that damage. One of the Wounds is a Blood Wound. Once the vow is broken or the duration expires, the spell ends and the spellcaster can heal the spell's Blood Magic Damage. Because of the risks involved, a spellcaster should be careful not to use this spell to force another into a totally unacceptable contract. The other party may decide to expunge their shame by breaking the vow and dying, possibly taking the spellcaster with them, rather than be bound by a dishon- orable or unfair contract.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (+1 week)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (+1 week)

Circle_11.Name: Reattach Limb
Circle_11.Description: Ritual. This spell attaches a severed limb in good condition to its owner. It must be reattached to the point where it was lost. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes 10 minutes, during which time the spellcaster must remain within 5 yards of the target. The target cannot move during the process. The spellcaster applies their personal healing salve for this spell to the limb and point of attachment while weaving threads and makes a Spell- casting (15) test. If successful, the target must spend a Recovery Test and 5 Karma Points-the spellcaster must make up the difference of any Karma the target is not able to spend. None of these provide any additional benefits other than powering the required magic. If these costs cannot be paid, the spell fails. The limb attaches itself to the target; muscles, bones, and arteries are magically grafted into their original position and the target heals dam- age equal to the Effect test result.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2)

Circle_12.Name: Eternal Day
Circle_12.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell conjures a small, bright spark that rises upward and expands in size until it replaces the sun. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spellcaster rearranges their clothing, ensur- ing nothing obscures their vision, points their hand upward, and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, a spark flies from the spellcaster's hand, streaking toward the sky, where it takes on the shape of the sun. The spark hangs in the same place in the sky; the sun is no longer seen. This daylight radiates outward in the area.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_12.Name: Wild Hunt
Circle_12.Effect: Summons an horde of beast spirits
Circle_12.Description: Ritual. Spirit. Summoning. This ritual spell summons a pack of primal hunters. The spellcaster prepares by drawing a glyph for each of the spirits while weaving threads. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes 10 minutes. After weaving the final thread, the spellcaster whistles sharply and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, primal hunters materialize around the spellcaster, eager for the impending hunt. Every 10 primal hunt- ers are a group and this spell creates one group, but extra threads can create more groups. The spellcaster can mentally command their primal hunters within line of sight-this requires their concentration as a Standard action. If left un- controlled, primal hunters act on their most recent instructions. They must remain within the spell's area at all times, which follows the spellcaster, dematerializing if they leave it. Any so affected primal hunters move toward the spellcaster to return at full speed, materializing once they enter the area. The spellcaster cannot order them outside the area by any means to have them be dematerialized and waiting to return-that's poor form. Primal hunters use the statistics listed below; they're the same for all primal hunters. If a primal hunter is Wounded, the magic bringing it to the physical world is sundered and it departs. Regardless of the number of members in the pack, they act as a group and receive only the listed actions. They suffer Wound penalties as normal, reflecting the pack's losses. Area spells can cause additional Wounds for each full multiple of the Wound Threshold inflicted on the primal hunters. Abilities that inflict a pen- alty on a single target (e.g. Mind Dagger's Physical Defense penalty) or abili- ties that provide a bonus against one opponent (e.g. Maneuver, Pack Tactics) cost an additional success against the beast spirits. If the penalty is a 'rider' on the damage (e.g. Earth Darts) and no extra success on the test, it inflicts damage but not the penalty. The primal hunters are considered a single target for abilities with a variable difficulty based on the number of targets (e.g., Battle Bellow). A knocked down primal hunter automatically stands up and has no penalty to their action(s). This spell can be cast once per day. Primal Hunters DEX: 7 Initiative: NA Wounds: 8 STR: 7 Physical Defense: 10 Wound Threshold: 12 TOU: 6 Mystic Defense: 9 PER: 5 Social Defense: 9 Knockdown: 11 WIL: 5 Physical Armor: 5 Recovery Tests: 0 CHA: 5 Mystic Armor: 5 Move: 16 Actions: 1; Attack: 23 (23) Powers: Fury (2) Mob Mentality: Primal hunters cannot be Harried or Blindsided from positioning. Abilities still cause these as usual. Resist Pain (6) Special Maneuvers: Sweeping Attack (Opponent, Close Combat): The attacker may spend two additional successes on an Attack test to treat the attack as an area attack; they must be adjacent to at least two primal hunters to use this special maneuver. This special maneuver only costs one additional suc- cess if the attacker is using a two-handed weapon.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 hours)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 hours), Increase Effect (+1 group), Increase Area (+10 yards)

Circle_13.Name: New Moon's Blessing
Circle_13.Effect: Benefit for starting a new endeavor
Circle_13.Description: Fate. Ritual. This spell provides a bonus to a willing target for a partic- ular new undertaking. The spellcaster anoints the willing target with oils and the target indicates the specifics of their new effort, while weaving the threads. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes 30 minutes. When the threads are woven, the spellcaster draws their personal glyph over the target's heart and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, a clean, fresh scent exudes strongly from the target for the remainder of the day and the glyph sparkles faintly on their skin even if drawn over clothing. They gain +1 to Action tests directly related to their new endeavor until the next new moon. This must be an entirely new venture, not simply a continuation of a previous activity. However, it can be similar to something the target engaged in previously. For example, opening a trading partnership with a trading house in Tra- var when the character previously engaged in establishing a trade route to a recently opened kaer is acceptable. Opening a trading partnership with a second trading house in Travar isn't acceptable. The bonus provided cannot be greater than the spellcaster's Shaman Circle. The spellcaster may cast this spell once per year on a given target.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (+1)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (+1)

Circle_13.Name: Restore Pattern
Circle_13.Description: Ritual. This ritual spell restores a damaged or warped pattern to whole- ness. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes one hour, during which time the spellcaster must remain within 5 yards of the target. The target cannot move during the process. The spellcaster draws the damaged portions of the target's pattern on their skin, using a mixture unique to each spellcaster. The spellcaster draws their personal glyph and makes a Spellcasting (10) test. If successful, the target must spend a Recovery Test and 10 Karma Points-the spellcaster must make up the difference of any Karma the target is not able to spend. None of these provide any additional benefits other than powering the required magic. If these costs cannot be paid, the spell fails. The target heals damage equal to the Effect test result and compares the result to the Step of any ability that damaged the target's pattern (e.g. Shatter Pattern). If successful, the ability ends. This spell may be cast once per day per tar- get.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2)

Circle_14.Name: Council of Beasts
Circle_14.Description: Ritual. Spirit. This powerful ritual summons all the beast spirits within the area and calls them to attend the spellcaster. The spellcaster creates one glyph per thread that combines their personal sigil with representations of the beast spirits in the area. Each Thread Weaving test (or equivalent) takes one hour. The spell- caster calls to the spirits and makes a Spellcast- ing (10) test. If successful, they can use their Summon [Beast Spirits] test within the spell's duration to summon a council of beasts. The talent still takes 30 minutes to perform and the test result is compared to the Mystic Defense of all the beast spirits in the area. All spirits the test succeeds against arrive at the spellcaster within 30 minutes under a metaphorical banner of peace to be respected by all parties. If the peace is broken, the spellcaster will have ex- plaining to do and amends to make. If they are involved in breaking the peace, they can never summon the types of beasts in attendance again and all react violently to them on sight. This summon must be made within the duration and the council concludes by the end. Once the council concludes, the spell im- mediately ends. Once the council of beasts is assembled, the spellcaster may bring a sin- gle matter before them and ask their judgment. The topic can be anything of concern to the beasts, whether relating to their domain, threats to them in general, requesting a favor and compensation, etc. This is deliberately broad, but it must be of interest to them, even if that interest is only benefit- ing through payment. The council weighs what the spellcaster says and of- fers, and provides its advice. The spellcaster cannot force the spirits to obey in any way, though can use Spirit Talk to sway them through persuasive arguments. These spirits can be powerful allies if they choose to help the spellcaster; they know everything related to their domain. The gamemaster determines the spirits' reaction based on a few factors: the relevance of the topic, offers and risks, the spellcaster's presentation of the topic, and how much respect they showed for the spirits in the past. Has the spellcaster forged friendships, treated them well, ensured they were compensated for summonings, engaged in a contest of wills, bound spirits to items, etc.? At the gamemaster's discretion, the spirits choose whether or not to support the position depending on the aforementioned factors. If the spellcaster showed complete irreverence for spirits in the past, the reaction could range from outright denial of the request and returning home, to com- plete outrage and overtly hostile action once the council is complete and the onus of peace is lifted. The spellcaster can only have one active casting of this spell at a time. This spell cannot be cast on (e.g. Fifth Circle Wizard Karma ability) or by an- other character (e.g. Loan Spell).  Success Levels: Increase Area (+1 mile)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2 to the Summon test), Increase Effect (2 to the Elemental Tongues tests), Increase Area (+1 mile)

Circle_14.Name: Regrowth
Circle_14.Description: Ritual. This spell regrows one of the willing target's body parts. The spellcaster applies ointments to the target's body and paints the skin around the lost part with glyphs. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes 30 minutes. After weaving the threads, the spellcaster draws their personal glyph and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the target must spend a Recovery Test and 5 Karma Points-the spellcaster must make up the difference of any Karma the target is not able to spend. It's re- stored to its original function over the course of the duration, during which the target must spend a Recovery Test upon waking each day. This Recovery Test doesn't heal any damage. If the target cannot spend the Recovery Test for any reason, the spell ends and the partial regrowth withers away, re- turning them to their previous state. Any 'modifications' made (e.g. blood charms, magical tattoos, etc.) are lost.  Success Levels: Decrease Duration (-2 days)  Extra Threads: Decrease Duration (-2 days)

Circle_15.Name: Delay Fate
Circle_15.Effect: Allows a target to delay their fate for a time
Circle_15.Description: Fate. Ritual. This spell allows the target to escape their fate for a time. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes 30 minutes, during which time the spellcaster must remain within 2 yards of the target. The target cannot move during the process. The spellcaster applies glyphs to the tar- get while weaving threads and makes a Spellcasting (20) test. If successful, the target must spend 10 Karma Points-the spellcaster must make up the difference of any Karma target is not able to spend. The Karma Points pro- vide no additional benefits. If these costs cannot be paid, the spell fails. The markings seep into the target's skin for the duration, appearing faded, but visible. Once as a Free action, the target may delay their fate. They don't take damage for the remainder of the encounter, but it's still recorded (with ar- mor applied as usual). This includes pattern damage (e.g. Pattern Shredder, Sever Talent), Wounds inflicted by abilities (e.g. Skin Shift), etc. When the encounter ends, the spell ends and the target suffers all the damage and otherwise avoided effects at once-this doesn't include Wound Threshold reduction. This damage cannot be reduced or otherwise mitigated by ef- fects. Each multiple over the target's Wound Threshold inflicts an additional Wound (e.g. a target with a Wound Threshold of 17 suffering 36 damage takes two Wounds). This spell can be cast once per lunar cycle.  Success Levels: Increase Duration (2 days)  Extra Threads: Increase Duration (2 days) Full Moon's Curse Threads: 6 (see text) Weaving: 19/24 Casting: 20 Range: Touch Duration: Up to 7 days (see text) Area of Effect: 1-mile radius

Circle_15.Name: Full Moon's Curse
Circle_15.Description: Ritual. This spell causes insanity to descend on an area during the full moon. The spellcaster must burn fragrant boswellia and bursera resins while they weave the threads. Each Thread Weaving (or equivalent) test takes one hour. After weaving the threads, the spellcaster makes a Spellcast- ing (20) test. If successful, nothing happens until after sundown on the next day of the full moon. After sundown, the Effect Step is compared against the Willpower Step any Namegivers in the area-animals are unaffected. If successful, affected targets descend into madness. All their worst impulses come to the fore and become their actions. This doesn't mean things imme- diately descend into violence and full debauchery, but it's definitely a weird- er, almost mirror-universe version of the area. Each time a character may be forced to indulge their particular madness, they may make a Willpower test against the Effect Step to resist. If successful, they retain their senses for the next hour and should use that time to leave. If they don't, the madness attempts to takes hold once more after the hour. The madness fades with sunrise, but the memories don't. These effects last for up to the three days of the full moon, up to the spell's duration. If the full moon doesn't occur with- in the duration, this spell has no effect.  Success Levels: Increase Effect (2)  Extra Threads: Increase Effect (2), Increase Area (+1 mile)
